entries.name: Creating a Jujutsu Sorcerer
entries: You can make a jujutsu sorcerer quickly by following these suggestions. First, put your highest ability score in Dexterity, followed by Charisma. Second, choose the Sorcerer Student, Sorcerer Family, Curse User, or the New Shadow Style Student background. Third, choose the Cursed Spirit, Cursed Vessel, or Human race.

entries.name: Design Notes
entries: {@b Damage Dice Progression:} When a feature says you increase the damage dice of an attack by 1 step or tier, it follows: {@dice 1d4}>{@dice 1d6}>{@dice 1d8}>{@dice 1d10}>{@dice 1d12}>{@dice 2d6}>{@dice 2d8}>{@dice 2d10}>{@dice 2d12}>{@dice 4d6}... and so on.

{@b Playability:} This class and the Jujutsu Kaisen campaign setting functions as a modification of the Dungeons & Dragons 5e ruleset. It is not designed to be used alongside official 5e base classes, races, or content.

classFeature.name: Cursed Technique
entries: Starting at 1st Level, you were born with a cursed technique. Cursed techniques are unique abilities congenitally engraved into the user's body, specifically in the brain's right prefrontal cortex. Most jujutsu sorcerers are born with an innate technique that manifests around the age of five or six, continually carving its formula into the user over time.
Your cursed technique shapes how you fight and how you express your cursed energy, while your Jujutsu School at 3rd level reflects the training tradition that develops your talents.

entries.name: Technique Components
entries: Techniques typically require somatic and verbal components to cast, represented as S or V followed by a number. For S, the number represents the number of free hands necessary; V represents the number of free mouths required.

When a technique's action cost is reduced to a free action or lower, you may reduce its S by 1. At the DM's discretion, you may lower a technique's V by 1 at the cost of increasing S by 2. If a technique requires an integral component, its S or V cannot be reduced below 1.

entries.name: Multiple Techniques
entries: If you possess more than one Innate Technique, only one can be used at a time. If you are actively using one of your cursed techniques (such as a summoned shikigami, a duration-based ability, or any other ongoing effect), activating a different cursed technique will immediately end all effects of the currently active technique.

Some passive effects will remain. Work with your DM to determine which effects would be dispelled and which would remain passively. Imbuing a cursed technique into a Domain Expansion does not count towards using more than one innate technique.

classFeature.name: Cursed Energy
entries: Your training has allowed you to control the cursed energy within yourself. Charisma is your cursed energy modifier for your cursed energy fueled features.

You have a number of cursed energy points equal to twice your Jujutsu sorcerer Level + your Charisma modifier, as shown in the cursed energy column of the Jujutsu sorcerer table. You can spend these points to fuel various Jujutsu sorcerer class features, including your cursed techniques.
When you spend cursed energy, it becomes unavailable until you finish a long rest; at the end, you regain all spent cursed energy.

entries.name: Cursed Energy DC and Attack
entries: Some cursed energy applications require their target to make a saving throw. The saving throw DC is calculated as follows:
{@b Cursed Energy attack roll} = proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier.
Your own cursed energy deals half as much damage to yourself.

classFeature.name: Unarmored Movement
entries: At 1st Level, your speed increases by 10 feet while you are not wearing armor or wielding a shield. This bonus increases when you reach certain Jujutsu Sorcerer Levels, as shown in the Jujutsu Sorcerer table.

classFeature.name: Unarmored Defense
entries: Beginning at 1st Level, while wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity or Strength modifier + your Charisma modifier.

classFeature.name: Martial Arts
entries: At 2nd Level, your martial arts practice gives you mastery of combat styles that use unarmed strikes. You gain the following benefits while you aren't wearing armor or wielding a shield:

items: You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for your unarmed strikes' attack and damage rolls.

You can roll a {@dice d4} in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strike. The damage die increases as you gain Jujutsu Sorcerer Levels, as shown in the Martial Arts column of the Jujutsu Sorcerer table.
When you use the Attack action with melee weapon attack on your turn, you can make one melee weapon attack as a bonus action.
You gain access to Sorcerer Weapons. You may use your martial arts damage die instead of the sorcerer weapon damage die if you choose to.

classFeature.name: Cursed Energy Enhancement
entries: At 2nd Level, you began to focus your cursed energy to manipulate it better. You learn the following enhancements at the Novice tier:

entries.name: Cursed Strike
entries: Before you make an unarmed strike, you may spend up to your proficiency bonus in cursed energy to empower your next strike. This enhancement lasts until you hit a creature with your unarmed strike or until a minute passes.

You deal additional force damage equal to {@dice 1d6} per point spent on a hit. You can also use this in response to rolling a natural 20 on an attack roll with an unarmed strike or landing a black flash instead of beforehand.
You can only use this enhancement again after it has ended. You can use this feature a number of times per round equal to your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Cursed Armor
entries: On your or another creature's turn, and for an amount of cursed energy up to your Charisma modifier (no action required), you can surround yourself with cursed energy for one minute.

You gain temporary hit points equal to the amount of cursed energy spent times 5 for the duration. You can only benefit from this feature again after 1 minute or having your temporary hit points reduced to 0. You cannot use this enhancement if you cannot take actions due to a condition or effect.

entries.name: Cursed Weapon Enhancement
entries: As a bonus action, you can spend cursed energy up to your proficiency bonus to add to a melee, ranged or improvised weapon you are wielding for 1 minute. Whenever you hit a target with the weapon, it takes extra force damage equal to the amount spent.
You can only use this enhancement again after 1 minute has passed. Other creatures cannot use your Cursed Weapon Enhancement. The duration remains while you are not wielding the weapon.
When using Cursed Weapon Enhancement on a ranged weapon, its projectiles deal extra force damage equal to the amount spent.
If you do this 25 times or more to the same weapon, it becomes a Semi-Cursed Tool as long as it's not already a cursed tool.

entries.name: Higher Tiers
entries: As you progress as a Sorcerer, your Cursed Energy Enhancement skill increases. There are 2 tiers above Novice: Advanced and Expert.

items: {@b Advanced Cursed Strikes.} The dice of your cursed strikes increases to a {@dice d8}.
{@b Advanced Cursed Armor.} The temporary hit points gained per cursed energy spent increases to 10.
{@b Advanced Cursed Weapon Enhancement.} The amount of force damage added increases to twice the amount spent.
{@b Expert Cursed Strikes.} The dice of your cursed strikes increases to a {@dice d10}.
{@b Expert Cursed Armor.} The temporary hit points gained per cursed energy spent increases to 15.
{@b Expert Cursed Weapon Enhancement.} The amount of force damage added increases to three times the amount spent. You can now use Cursed Weapon Enhancement as a free action.

classFeature.name: Cursed Martial Arts
entries: At 2nd Level, your Jujutsu training has taught you advanced martial techniques. You can now do any of the following:

entries.name: Cursed Blast of Blows
entries: Immediately after taking the Attack action on your turn, you can spend 3 cursed energy to perform two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.

entries.name: Cursed Patient Defense
entries: You can spend 3 cursed energy to take the Dodge action as a bonus action on your turn.

entries.name: Cursed Wind Step
entries: You can spend 3 cursed energy to take the Disengage or Dash action as a bonus action on your turn, and your jump distance doubles until the end of your turn.

classFeature.name: Jujutsu Schools
entries: Starting at 3rd Level, you have begun to learn from a jujutsu school or teacher how to improve your cursed energy. Choose between Tokyo Jujutsu High, Kyoto Jujutsu High, Hiroshima Jujutsu High, Ainu Jujutsu High, and Sendai Jujutsu High.

entries.name: Tokyo Jujutsu High
entries: You came from a more liberal school, where you decided not to focus on the basics but on improving your Innate Technique.

You learn three enhancements to apply to one of your Lapse, Extension, and Reversal techniques. A technique cannot have multiple enhancements, nor can one enhancement be applied to multiple techniques.

items: {@b Power Enhancement.} You can now spend additional cursed energy up to your proficiency bonus on the technique to add an additional damage die per additional cursed energy spent to the total damage.
{@b Cursed Energy Economy.} The cursed energy cost of the technique is now reduced by your proficiency bonus (to a minimum of 1 cursed energy).

{@b Distance Enhancement.} You can spend up to your proficiency bonus in additional cursed energy on the technique to add 10 ft. to the range for each additional cursed energy spent unless it's touch range.

entries: When an enhancement is applied to a Lapse technique, it will also apply to any further Technique Improvements related directly to it. You may reallocate your enhancements after a short or long rest.

entries.name: Kyoto Jujutsu High
entries: You have come from a very conservative school, where you learned to ignore your individuality as a jujutsu sorcerer and instead focus on mastering the basic Jujutsu arts.
Your Cursed Martial Arts techniques each now only cost 1 cursed energy to perform, and you may increase one Cursed Energy Enhancement by a tier.
In addition, one of your cursed martial arts is more refined:

items: {@b Cursed Blast of Blows.} When you use this feature, you can now make three unarmed strikes instead of two.
{@b Cursed Patient Defense.} You gain advantage on Constitution and Strength saving throws when you use the Dodge action granted by this feature.

{@b Cursed Wind Step.} When you take the Dash action granted by this feature, you instead gain extra movement equal to twice your speed. When you take the Disengage action, you gain extra movement equal to your speed.

entries: You gain one additional refined martial art at 6th and another at 10th Level.

entries.name: Hiroshima Jujutsu High
entries: You have come from a school that values Intelligence. You have learned more about how to use cursed creations to your advantage.

items: {@b Talisman Proficiency.} You gain proficiency in Talismans and are gifted a set of them.
{@b Sorcerer Studies.} You gain proficiency in Arcana or expertise if you already have it. Additionally, you may use Charisma in place of Intelligence.

{@b Faster Imbuement.} When enhancing a cursed tool or imbuing your cursed technique into a tool, the first two Cursed Weapon Enhancements performed outside of combat count toward enhancing or creating a tool.

entries.name: Ainu Jujutsu High
entries: You have come from a school up north. This school values barrier techniques over all others.
You learn three enhancements that you can apply to your barrier techniques (not domain barriers). Each barrier technique cannot have multiple enhancements.

items: {@b Health Enhancement.} You can now spend additional cursed energy up to your proficiency bonus on the technique to add 5 hit points to the barrier per cursed energy spent.
{@b Cursed Energy Economy.} The cursed energy cost of the barrier is now reduced by your proficiency bonus (to a minimum of 1 cursed energy).
{@b Distance Enhancement.} You can spend up to your proficiency bonus in additional cursed energy on the barrier to add 10 ft. to the radius for each additional cursed energy spent.

entries: You may reallocate your enhancements after a short or long rest.

entries.name: Sendai Jujutsu High
entries: You have come from a school that values defensive techniques. As a result, you are naturally more evasive and durable.
Your Cursed Martial Arts techniques now only cost 1 cursed energy, and you may increase one Cursed Energy Enhancement by a tier.
In addition, you gain one of the following benefits:

items: {@b Durable Body.} You gain a damage reduction equal to your Constitution modifier.

{@b Cursed Patient Defense.} You gain advantage on Charisma saving throws when you use the Dodge action granted by this feature. You keep the benefits even if your speed drops to 0, and gain advantage in either Strength or Constitution saving throws if your speed is 0 when you activate this Dodge action.
{@b Sturdier Frame.} You gain a +2 bonus to your armor class.

entries: You gain one additional benefit at 6th and another at 10th Level.

classFeature.name: Sorcerer Training
entries: At 3rd Level, you have been focusing on improving yourself as a sorcerer. You will gain one of the following feats that you meet the requirements of (you may ignore one prerequisite when picking a body feat):

items: {@b Body Feats:} Athlete History, 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!, Reinforced Immune System, or Resilient
{@b Taijutsu Feats:} Taijutsu Sorcery, Slam Master, or Acrobatic Fighter
{@b Barrier Technique Feats:} Basic Barrier
{@b Cursed Energy Feats:} Immense Cursed Energy
{@b Cursed Energy Manipulation Feats:} Cursed Energy Manipulator, or Advanced Energy Usage
{@b Cursed Creations Feats:} Shikigami Summon

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th Level, and again at 8th, 12th, 15th, 16th, 18th, and 19th Level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2 or two ability scores of your choice by 1. As usual, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You may also ignore this increase to gain a Cursed Feat that you meet the prerequisite of.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th Level, and again at 8th, 12th, 15th, 16th, 18th, and 19th Level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2 or two ability scores of your choice by 1. As usual, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You may also ignore this increase to gain a Cursed Feat that you meet the prerequisite of.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th Level, and again at 8th, 12th, 15th, 16th, 18th, and 19th Level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2 or two ability scores of your choice by 1. As usual, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You may also ignore this increase to gain a Cursed Feat that you meet the prerequisite of.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th Level, and again at 8th, 12th, 15th, 16th, 18th, and 19th Level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2 or two ability scores of your choice by 1. As usual, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You may also ignore this increase to gain a Cursed Feat that you meet the prerequisite of.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th Level, and again at 8th, 12th, 15th, 16th, 18th, and 19th Level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2 or two ability scores of your choice by 1. As usual, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You may also ignore this increase to gain a Cursed Feat that you meet the prerequisite of.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th Level, and again at 8th, 12th, 15th, 16th, 18th, and 19th Level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2 or two ability scores of your choice by 1. As usual, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You may also ignore this increase to gain a Cursed Feat that you meet the prerequisite of.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th Level, and again at 8th, 12th, 15th, 16th, 18th, and 19th Level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2 or two ability scores of your choice by 1. As usual, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You may also ignore this increase to gain a Cursed Feat that you meet the prerequisite of.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Starting at 5th Level, you can attack twice, instead of once, when you take the Attack action during your turn.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack Improvement
entries: At 17th Level, the number of attacks you can make when you take the Attack action increases to three.

classFeature.name: Binding Vow
entries: Starting at 5th Level, you can create a binding vow with a willing creature or yourself, entering into a strict contract formed by Jujutsu.
At any point, you can create one binding vow and choose one of the vows listed in the Binding Vows section. During a combat encounter, once per round, you can make a Binding Vow (no action required).

You can have a maximum number of binding vows equal to 1 + your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 2) at a time. During a Short or Long rest, you can end a number of Binding Vows equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1).

classFeature.name: Curse-Empowered Strikes
entries: At 6th Level, you have begun focusing more cursed energy into your strikes or weapon. Your attacks with weapons or unarmed strikes (your choice when you gain this feature) now count as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistances and immunities to damage from nonmagical attacks.
Depending on your Charisma modifier, you may add an extra force damage die to the total damage:

entries.caption: Jujutsu Density Damage Dice
rows: {@dice 1d4}
{@dice 1d6}
{@dice 1d8}
{@dice 1d10}
{@dice 1d12}

entries: At 11th Level, the attacks with the option you haven't chosen at 6th Level are now considered magical and will deal the additional die.

classFeature.name: Advanced Studies
entries: Starting at 7th Level, your talents have been refined by the teachings of your Jujutsu High. You receive benefits depending on which school you chose at 3rd level. See your Jujutsu School description for details.

classFeature.name: Advanced Sorcerer Training
entries: At 7th Level, you have furthered your focus on improving the strengths you have already been developing.
You gain one of the following feats that you meet the requirements of but excluding their Level requirements, if any:

items: {@b Body Feats:} Athlete History, Colossal Physique, 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!, Untraceable Speed, Reinforced Immune System, Strong Body, Human Wall, or Resilient
{@b Taijutsu Feats:} Taijutsu Sorcery, Slam Master, Acrobatic Fighter, Cursed Martial Artist, or Cursed Weapon Wielder
{@b Barrier Technique Feats:} Basic Barrier, Barrier Master, New Shadow Style: Simple Domain, or Hollow Wicker Basket
{@b Cursed Energy Feats:} Immense Cursed Energy, or Overflowing Cursed Energy
{@b Cursed Energy Manipulation Feats:} Cursed Energy Manipulator, Cursed Energy Slash, Cursed Energy Ray, or Cursed Energy Explosion
{@b Cursed Creations Feats:} Shikigami Summon or Cursed Blacksmith
{@b Binding Vow Feats:} Vows Master or Favored Vows

classFeature.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: At 9th Level, due to extended periods of use, your cursed energy constantly recovers itself from the adrenaline of battle.
While in combat, you regain 1 cursed energy at the beginning of your turn, up to your maximum. The combat must be one where your life is at risk.

classFeature.name: Cursed Energy Enhancement Improvement
entries: At 10th level, all of your Cursed Energy Enhancements have their tier increased by one (from novice to advanced, or advanced to expert).

classFeature.name: Advanced Energy Usage
entries: At 11th Level, you have learned some more basic principles any sorcerer should apply, whether in battle or investigation. You will gain two of the following Cursed Energy Uses:

entries.name: Cursed Energy Tracker
entries: You have increased your skill in tracking remains of cursed energy as well as active cursed energy.

You may make a Perception check, adding your Charisma modifier, to see if there are any remains of cursed energy in the place you're at. If you succeed, you know exactly who the energy is from. If you do not know the person, you can only track where the trail leads. The DC is equal to the cursed energy DC of the creature you're tracking.

You know when any creature you can see spends cursed energy and how much they spend. Additionally, if you have proficiency in Perception, as a free action you can focus on a creature for 1 minute, seeing the 'sparks' of cursed energy before technique usage. For that minute, when that creature forces you to make a save or makes an attack against you using their cursed technique, they must roll Stealth (using Charisma) against your Perception. If you win, you get advantage on the saving throw or the attack is made with disadvantage.

entries.name: Cursed Energy Concealer
entries: You have become more skilled at hiding and retaining your cursed energy passively.

You may now take the Hide action as a bonus action. When you take the Hide action, you can attempt to conceal your cursed energy by making a Stealth check. For the next 10 minutes, wherever you pass you will not leave cursed energy tracks, so anyone trying to track you needs to succeed on a contested check against your roll + your Charisma modifier.
While both you and your Cursed Energy are hidden, the benefit of being an Unseen Attacker also extends to your cursed energy saving throws, forcing affected creatures to roll with disadvantage.

entries.name: Negative Flow
entries: Understanding that the core of cursed energy is negative emotions, you've now grown to synthesize your own cursed energy when distressed.

Whenever you are under the effects of the conditions frightened, berserk, bloodlusted, are under half your maximum hit points, missing a limb lost in this encounter, or in an encounter that's total CR is higher than your Level, you will gain temporary cursed energy equal to half of your Charisma modifier at the start of your turn (cannot go above 1/4th of your cursed energy maximum).

{@b Focused Rage.} During your turn, while using Negative Flow, you can concentrate as a free action to increase the temporary cursed energy gained to your full Charisma modifier. When the circumstances end, you can no longer concentrate on this feature.

entries.name: Curse Enhanced Senses
entries: Cursed energy can increase your physical capabilities and finely tune your senses.

As a bonus action for 4 cursed energy, you can hone your five senses, adding half of your Charisma modifier to Perception checks for one minute. If you have the Cursed Energy Reinforcement feat, this becomes your full Charisma modifier. Outside of combat, this lasts one hour instead.

entries.name: Object Infusion
entries: You've become more adept at imbuing cursed energy into mundane objects.

You can infuse a nonmagical tiny object as a free action. After infusing 10 tiny objects per long rest, subsequent infusions cost 1 cursed energy per 10 objects. Larger objects require more action time and cursed energy.

An object stays imbued for 1 minute, counts as an improvised weapon, and has double its normal hit points. You can spend double the cursed energy to make the object reactive (explodes when disturbed).

entries.name: Cursed Energy Flotation
entries: As a free action, you may touch a non-magical tiny object and imbue it with cursed energy, causing it to float. The same cost scaling applies as Object Infusion.
An object stays floating for a number of minutes equal to your Charisma modifier. A floating object is weightless and stays in place unless moved.

entries.name: Cursed Energy Battery
entries: You can accumulate cursed energy in an object that becomes a battery. As an action, you may store Cursed Energy (up to your Charisma modifier + proficiency bonus), and half of the energy spent is stored.

Attempting to store more energy than an object can hold causes it to explode, dealing magical force damage based on size. You cannot spend stored energy for personal features, but it may be used by external things like Cursed Corpses or Barriers.

classFeature.name: Efficiency Fundamentals
entries: At 11th level, you have gained the fundamentals of efficiency with utilizing cursed energy.

You gain 2 selections to reduce the cursed energy cost of. For each selection, choose a feature that costs cursed energy that you possess. Each selection has its cursed energy cost reduced by half of your proficiency bonus (rounded down), to a minimum of 1 cursed energy.
If you choose to reduce the cost of a Domain Expansion, it is only reduced by half of the amount it would normally be reduced.
You may change these selections upon leveling up. When you select a feature to reduce the cost of, you pick one specific cost rather than the entire feature.

classFeature.name: High Output Sorcery
entries: Starting at 13th level you’ve begun to learn some of the most explosive sorcery techniques, utilizing extreme levels of output.
You learn one of the following abilities: Chanting, Maximum Output, or Explosive Blow.

entries.name: Chanting
entries: By not choosing to omit any part of your technique’s activation requirements, going through the chants and the dances, you can draw out power beyond your limit.

You may choose one of your Innate Technique features and recite one of your chants as an Action (V-1). You must then maintain concentration on Chanting as if concentrating on a spell until the beginning of your next turn. After successfully maintaining concentration, the next time you use that Innate Technique Feature that has a recitation within two turns of completing chanting, it will gain two of the following benefits of your choice:

items: Your technique will ignore resistances and treat immunities as resistances.
You may double the original Range, Width, or Radius of your Technique.
You may triple the original Range, Width, or Radius of your Technique. (You must have doubled the range already through Chanting in order to apply this bonus).
If the Innate Technique feature requires a saving throw, the target(s) makes their save at disadvantage. You also raise the DC by your proficiency bonus for that saving throw.
If your Innate Technique feature has you make an attack roll, you gain advantage on that attack roll and may add your proficiency bonus to that roll an extra time.
The cost of your Innate Technique feature is halved.
Your Innate Technique will ignore all forms of cover.

Your Innate Technique's Action cost will be lowered by 1 (Ex. Full Turn Action -> Action -> Bonus Action -> Free Action). If the feature is a Reaction and its Action Cost is reduced you will gain an extra Reaction specifically for that feature. (You must be Complex Chanting in order to apply this bonus)
The duration of your Innate Technique feature is doubled.
The duration of your Innate Technique feature is tripled. (You must have doubled the duration already through Chanting in order to apply this bonus).

If an Innate Technique feature inflicts damage by causing a target to make a save, when taking the Resistant feature into consideration, the DC used for the save will be the total DC instead of the base DC. (You must be Complex Chanting in order to apply this bonus).

The cost of your Innate Technique feature is reduced to ¼. (You must be Complex Chanting in order to apply this bonus)(You must have halved the cost already through Chanting in order to apply this bonus. This reduction does not stack with the ½ reduction, but replaces it).

If your Innate Technique feature would force your opponent to make a saving throw, they roll 3 dice instead of one and take the lowest result for the roll. (You must be Complex Chanting in order to apply this bonus).

If your Innate Technique feature would have you make an Attack roll, you may roll 3 dice instead of 1 and take the highest result for the roll. (You must be Complex Chanting in order to apply this bonus).

entries: Every subsequent turn you spend your Action chanting the technique it will gain another two bonuses of your choice. The chanted technique may only have a number of bonuses applied to it equal to 1+ your Intelligence or Wisdom mod. In addition, the chanted Innate Technique feature will have its final damage boosted by 20% per round you spent chanting. (After all the dice have been rolled, multiply the total damage(s) by 1.2). This damage bonus is capped at 100%. (2x Damage).

Rather than using an action, you can perform a more complex variation of Chanting that combines both complex hand signs and delicate recitations as a Full Turn Action (S-2, V-1). When Chanting in this manner you may add four bonuses from the above list to the chanted technique, and every subsequent turn you spend a Full Turn Action chanting the technique in this way it will gain four more bonuses of your choice. Additionally, the chanted Innate Technique feature will have its final damage boosted by 50% per round you spent Complex Chanting. This damage bonus is capped at 100%. (2x Damage).

If your Innate Technique feature that benefits from Chanting allows you to make more than one attack roll or forces a target to make more than one save or has multiple ranges, you may only choose one of those components at a time to apply the benefit to. (Ex: You could choose to apply a saving throw disadvantage to the first saving throw of the technique, but in order to apply one to the second saving throw of the technique you would have to add that bonus to that part as well). You cannot perform Maximum Output on an Innate Technique feature benefiting from Chanting. You cannot apply the benefits of Chanting towards a Domain Expansion. You cannot ready an action for the chanting feat.
When using Cursed Concentration, you do not gain any of its benefits when concentrating on this feat specifically.

entries.name: Maximum Output
entries: Typically sorcerers only use at most two thirds of their output on any one move, but when you really need to you know how to use 100%

As a bonus action, you must spend Cursed Energy equal to the original cost of one of your lapse, reversal, extension, or maximum techniques twice. The first time you pay this cost, any reductions that apply to the technique are used; the second time, none are applied. Doing this will amplify its cursed energy output, making it gain two of the following benefits the next time it's cast before the end of your next turn:

items: The technique's range and area of effect are increased by half its original range, rounded to the highest 5. For example, a technique with 10ft. of range would become a 15ft. range technique. This range increase does not work with touch-based techniques.

The technique's damage dice is increased by half of its original dice or damage bonus if the technique is a flat damage rounded up. For example, a technique with 10d10 die of damage would add 5d10 die of damage to the total damage die.
The technique's cursed energy attack roll bonus to hit is increased by half of your proficiency bonus, rounded up.
The technique's DC is increased by half of your proficiency bonus rounded up.
The technique's duration is increased by half of its original duration. For example, a technique that lasts a minute would last 1 minute and 5 rounds.

entries.name: Explosive Blow
entries: You’ve reached a level of bodily reinforcement far above the average sorcerer, able to pack your fists with unreal amounts of cursed energy.

As a bonus action, you can concentrate on channeling a very condensed portion of your cursed energy into either your limbs or a weapon for 6 cursed energy. For 1 minute after you perform this bonus action, the next unarmed strike or attack with a weapon you land adds half of your attacks' damage dice (rounded down) as extra damage onto the damage roll for your attack.

For 12 cursed energy instead of 6 cursed energy, the melee attack that you use with this feature will add your attacks' damage dice to the damage roll instead of only half. Additional damage dice from explosive blow are not doubled on a critical hit.

On a successful melee attack that benefits from Explosive Blow, the creature must succeed a Strength saving throw, or be knocked back a number of feet equal to either your attacks' ability modifier or Charisma modifier times 10 in the direction of your choice. If the creature collides with an unsecured object or structure, then the creature and the unsecured object or structure receive {@dice 1d10} bludgeoning damage per every 10 feet the creature traveled before colliding with the surface (to a maximum of {@dice 10d10}). If it was another creature, they take damage equal to half of your attack's damage roll instead.

classFeature.name: Exorcise
entries: At 13th Level, you've learned to eliminate weaker cursed spirits without spending effort or energy.
As an Action, you can automatically destroy a cursed spirit within 30 ft. of you that you can see that has a CR equal to or lower than half of your proficiency bonus (rounded down).

classFeature.name: School Graduation
entries: Starting at 14th Level, you have finally graduated from your Jujutsu School, now becoming a full-on Jujutsu Sorcerer. You gain benefits depending on which school you have chosen. See your Jujutsu School description for details.

classFeature.name: Near The Pinnacle
entries: At 17th Level, your strength grows by the moment; you'll soon reach the pinnacle of a normal sorcerer. You gain a +4 which you may distribute among your ability scores.
Also, you must choose one of the following peaks to reach:

entries.name: Peak of Might
entries: Your Strength score maximum increases to 24. The increase is lost if your Strength maximum would become equal to or higher. Additionally, you may shove or grapple a creature whenever you hit a critical with a melee weapon attack that uses Strength.

entries.name: Peak of Speed
entries: Your Dexterity score maximum increases to 24. The increase is lost if your Dexterity maximum would become equal to or higher. Additionally, you can take the dash action as a bonus action (or as a free action if you already could do it as a bonus action).

entries.name: Peak of Endurance
entries: Your Constitution score maximum increases to 24. The increase is lost if your Constitution maximum would become equal to or higher. Additionally, your maximum hit points increase by your Jujutsu sorcerer level added twice.

entries.name: Peak of Intellect
entries: Your Intelligence score maximum increases to 24. The increase is lost if your Intelligence maximum would become equal to or higher. Additionally, you can now ignore one prerequisite before taking a cursed feat (not prerequisites requiring another feat, race, innate technique, or class).

entries.name: Peak of Wisdom
entries: Your Wisdom score maximum increases to 24. The increase is lost if your Wisdom maximum would become equal to or higher. Additionally, you gain one cursed feat that you meet the prerequisites of, proficiency in one skill or tool, and expertise in one skill.

entries.name: Peak of Sorcery
entries: Your Charisma score maximum increases to 24. The increase is lost if your Charisma maximum would become equal to or higher. Additionally, your Cursed Energy maximum increases by 20.

subclassFeature.name: Tokyo Jujutsu High
entries: You came from a more liberal school, where you decided not to focus on the basics but on improving your Innate Technique.

You learn three enhancements to apply to one of your Lapse, Extension, and Reversal techniques. A technique cannot have multiple enhancements, nor can one enhancement be applied to multiple techniques.

items: {@b Power Enhancement.} You can now spend additional cursed energy up to your proficiency bonus on the technique to add an additional damage die per additional cursed energy spent to the total damage.
{@b Cursed Energy Economy.} The cursed energy cost of the technique is now reduced by your proficiency bonus (to a minimum of 1 cursed energy).

{@b Distance Enhancement.} You can spend up to your proficiency bonus in additional cursed energy on the technique to add 10 ft. to the range for each additional cursed energy spent unless it's touch range.

entries: When an enhancement is applied to a Lapse technique, it will also apply to any further Technique Improvements related directly to it. You may reallocate your enhancements after a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Tokyo School Advanced Training
entries: At 6th level, your school training deepens. Reallocate one of your Tokyo school enhancements whenever you finish a short or long rest.

Choose one technique you already enhanced through this school. That technique gains an additional enhancement from the same list, though each individual enhancement can still apply only once to a given technique.

subclassFeature.name: Tokyo School Mastery
entries: At 10th level, your mastery of technical refinement sharpens further. Choose one more of your Lapse, Extension, or Reversal techniques to receive three school enhancements, following the normal Tokyo school rules.
You may also reallocate all Tokyo school enhancements whenever you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Kyoto Jujutsu High
entries: You have come from a very conservative school, where you learned to ignore your individuality as a jujutsu sorcerer and instead focus on mastering the basic Jujutsu arts.
Your Cursed Martial Arts techniques each now only cost 1 cursed energy to perform, and you may increase one Cursed Energy Enhancement by a tier.
In addition, one of your cursed martial arts is more refined:

items: {@b Cursed Blast of Blows.} When you use this feature, you can now make three unarmed strikes instead of two.
{@b Cursed Patient Defense.} You gain advantage on Constitution and Strength saving throws when you use the Dodge action granted by this feature.

{@b Cursed Wind Step.} When you take the Dash action granted by this feature, you instead gain extra movement equal to twice your speed. When you take the Disengage action, you gain extra movement equal to your speed.

subclassFeature.name: Kyoto School Advanced Training
entries: At 6th level, you gain one additional refined martial art from the Kyoto school list.

subclassFeature.name: Kyoto School Mastery
entries: At 10th level, you gain another additional refined martial art from the Kyoto school list.

subclassFeature.name: Hiroshima Jujutsu High
entries: You have come from a school that values Intelligence. You have learned more about how to use cursed creations to your advantage.

items: {@b Talisman Proficiency.} You gain proficiency in Talismans and are gifted a set of them.
{@b Sorcerer Studies.} You gain proficiency in Arcana or expertise if you already have it. Additionally, you may use Charisma in place of Intelligence.

{@b Faster Imbuement.} When enhancing a cursed tool or imbuing your cursed technique into a tool, the first two Cursed Weapon Enhancements performed outside of combat count toward enhancing or creating a tool.

subclassFeature.name: Hiroshima School Advanced Training
entries: At 6th level, your studies let you work faster and more precisely. When you create or enhance cursed tools outside combat, you reduce the time or effort required as determined by your DM, using the same principles as Faster Imbuement.

subclassFeature.name: Hiroshima School Mastery
entries: At 10th level, your crafted cursed creations reflect expert technique. You gain advantage on checks made to analyze, repair, or safely modify cursed tools, talismans, and similar cursed creations.

subclassFeature.name: Ainu Jujutsu High
entries: You have come from a school up north. This school values barrier techniques over all others.
You learn three enhancements that you can apply to your barrier techniques (not domain barriers). Each barrier technique cannot have multiple enhancements.

items: {@b Health Enhancement.} You can now spend additional cursed energy up to your proficiency bonus on the technique to add 5 hit points to the barrier per cursed energy spent.
{@b Cursed Energy Economy.} The cursed energy cost of the barrier is now reduced by your proficiency bonus (to a minimum of 1 cursed energy).
{@b Distance Enhancement.} You can spend up to your proficiency bonus in additional cursed energy on the barrier to add 10 ft. to the radius for each additional cursed energy spent.

entries: You may reallocate your enhancements after a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Ainu School Advanced Training
entries: At 6th level, choose one barrier technique you have already enhanced through this school. It gains one additional Ainu school enhancement from the same list, following the normal restrictions.

subclassFeature.name: Ainu School Mastery
entries: At 10th level, choose one more barrier technique to receive three Ainu school enhancements, following the normal Ainu school rules.

subclassFeature.name: Sendai Jujutsu High
entries: You have come from a school that values defensive techniques. As a result, you are naturally more evasive and durable.
Your Cursed Martial Arts techniques now only cost 1 cursed energy, and you may increase one Cursed Energy Enhancement by a tier.
In addition, you gain one of the following benefits:

items: {@b Durable Body.} You gain a damage reduction equal to your Constitution modifier.

{@b Cursed Patient Defense.} You gain advantage on Charisma saving throws when you use the Dodge action granted by this feature. You keep the benefits even if your speed drops to 0, and gain advantage in either Strength or Constitution saving throws if your speed is 0 when you activate this Dodge action.
{@b Sturdier Frame.} You gain a +2 bonus to your armor class.

subclassFeature.name: Sendai School Advanced Training
entries: At 6th level, you gain one additional benefit from the Sendai school list.

subclassFeature.name: Sendai School Mastery
entries: At 10th level, you gain another additional benefit from the Sendai school list.

background.name: Sorcerer Family

items.name: Skill Proficiencies
items.entry: Choose two from {@skill Athletics}, {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Deception}, {@skill Performance}, {@skill Persuasion}, {@skill Religion}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies
items.entry: One artisan's tools of your choice

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: A simple weapon, a clan's uniform, and a pouch containing 10 gp

entries: Sorcerer Families are clans of jujutsu sorcerers that pass down inherited cursed techniques through generations. These attributes make sorcerer clans prominent figures in the jujutsu world. This is especially true for the Big Three Sorcerer Families who hold the most prestige and influence due to their bloodlines' innate talent for jujutsu. The three great sorcerer families of the jujutsu world are the Zenin Family, the Gojo Family, and the Kamo Family. You have been born into one of the three great sorcerer families, having been raised by them since you were young.

entries.caption: Clan
rows: Inumaki Clan
Kamo Clan
Gojo Clan
Zenin Clan

entries.name: Feature: Inumaki Clan - Jujutsu Outlaw
entries: You were born in a family that is considered outlaw by the jujutsu world, with you knowing that they are trying to eradicate the sorcerers from their family. If you have any levels in the jujutsu sorcerer class, you will be treated badly by your clan and will hardly receive their support.

entries.name: Feature: Kamo Clan - Pure Bloodline
entries: You were born in the family that values its blood purity, making you learn to avoid the external jujutsu world but also giving you more knowledge about it. You have an easier time searching for or remembering information that involves the history of the jujutsu world, and due to your knowledge and wisdom the higher ups will be more compliant to help you.

entries.name: Feature: Gojo Clan - The Strongest Clan
entries: Due to being born in the family where the strongest sorcerers are born, your voice has maximum authority on others. Fellow Sorcerers will listen to your ideas more easily, and will not oppose most of your actions. You also can stop conflicts more easily, since your mere presence already settles things down. However, the higher ups will dislike you despite being willing to hear your ideas.

entries.name: Feature: Zenin Clan - Power Above All Else
entries: You were born in the family where power is the sole thing that defines your value, weakness is seen as shameful and will be ridiculed. You may potentially have access to resources that could improve the quality of your training due to the clan's prestige and status. You will also receive better treatment from your fellow Zenin clan members depending on your power status.

Additionally, a significant portion of the Special Grade Cursed Tools will be in possession of your clan, such as the Split Soul Katana and the Inverted Spear of Heaven. Depending on the urgency and your status within the clan, you may either acquire them or steal them. These tools are strictly unknown to the rest of the world, and only blood members know where they are stored.

entries.name: Alternate Feature: Exile
entries: You have been exiled from your clan due to a terrible crime, breaking a rule, or simply because you decided to leave and never come back. Whatever were your reasons, one thing is certain, you want nothing to do with your clan anymore. Due to your newfound neutrality since you left your clan, the other clans have become more acceptant of you. You may get information, ask for help, and even trade easier with any clans outside of your own.

rows: I judge people by their strength, not only as a person but also as a fighter.
Give me the pay, and I'll do it.
I must spread positivity so the world can become a better place for those I care about to live in.
I just take it easy, there's no need to rush things.
I'm impulsive and tend to act more on my feelings than my thoughts.
I'll fight for it if I think it's right.
Rules are absolute, I won't disobey them.
I want to turn against my clan.
Judgement. I'm going to help the people I believe are good. (Neutral)
Freedom. I'd rather die than just be what they want me to be. (Chaotic)
Loyal. I will obey my clan's orders until the end. (Lawful)
Protector. Protecting those I care about is the most important thing for me. (Good)
Visionary. I will turn this world upside down, no matter the means. (Evil)
Dutiful. If the mission is given, I must accomplish it. (Any)
My friends are my world.
I will get my revenge on those who wronged me at my clan.
My clan is sacred to me, I must defend its members with my life.
I have unsolved business with my sibling and we ended up distancing from each other.
I couldn't care less about bonds, I live for myself and myself only.
I have few people who I can truly trust, and I value them a lot.
I don't see you as human if you're not a sorcerer, you're not a sorcerer if you're not from my clan.
I won't mind if any of the other clans go out of business, I only care about mine.
I believe our bloodline must be kept pure, which makes me not want to fall in love with outsiders and disapprove of those who do so.
No one is deserving of becoming the next head of the clan but me.
If you're not strong I think you're as good as trash.
I am extremely proud of my bloodline, and can get physical if someone disrespects it.

background.name: Sorcerer Student

items.name: Skill Proficiencies
items.entry: Choose two from {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Athletics}, {@skill History}, {@skill Perception}, {@skill Religion}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Stealth}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies
items.entry: Choose one type of artisan's tools or Talismans

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: A Jujutsu high uniform (commoner's clothes) and a pouch containing 5 gp

entries: Jujutsu Sorcerers are individuals secretly trained and employed by the government in Japan to defend humanity from cursed spirits. The race of humans capable of using jujutsu are colloquially known as Sorcerers, and the jujutsu prefix is often used to signify their alignment to Jujutsu High. Evil jujutsu sorcerers are known as Curse Users while humans who cannot use jujutsu are called Non-Sorcerers. All jujutsu sorcerers are trained in their youth at one of the two jujutsu educational facilities in either Tokyo or Kyoto. There they are taught about jujutsu and how to use their abilities to exorcise cursed spirits.

entries.name: Feature: Student Support
entries: You are just a student in jujutsu high, which makes teachers and the higher ups give you support. Jujutsu high is more inclined to help you than others, providing basic things such as grade 4 cursed tools, a teacher to supervise your progress, and send you into missions. Depending on your teacher, you'll gain additional information or be sent into even better missions.

entries.name: Alternate Feature: Professional Sorcerer
entries: After graduation, you decided to follow your path as a sorcerer and work professionally. At the end of every month, you gain gp based on your grade plus missions completed (determined by the GM).

rows: 10 gp
20 gp
Semi 2
30 gp
40 gp
Semi 1
50 gp
100 gp
Special Grade
I'm only here to do my job, anything else is overtime.
The higher ups are above me, and I'll follow their orders to the end.
I hate when others help me, since I know I'm capable.
I'm quiet and tend to stay on the sidelines.
I'm very energetic and live in the moment, enjoying being in the presence of those who do the same.
I have a happy personality as a way to move on from my pains.
I'm petty, and tend to look down on others to make myself feel better.
I'm calm and collected, knowing when to act.
Cursed. I have a "curse" within me, which I must overcome. (Any)
Dutiful. I must fulfill my role, as it is my duty to do so. (Lawful)
Caring. I want to die surrounded by people I care about. (Good)
Vengeful. I want to take revenge on those who wronged me. (Evil)
Determined. I will do anything to achieve my goal, no matter what I have to sacrifice. (Chaotic)
Selfless. I'm not a hero, but I'll lend a hand. (Neutral)
I love my friends, and wish to keep them safe!
I have family back home, and I'm out here for them.
I'm very close with my sibling, and we'll do anything for each other.
I have a Best Friend, for which I would die for!
I value my superiors, having a deep respect for them.
I couldn't let go of a loved one, and now they follow me as my "curse".
I wish I was born with better powers, and tend to see myself as inferior to others.
I take my tastes in the opposite gender very seriously, and I will beat you down if you disagree.
I tend to think of myself as someone superior, thinking that I'm the strongest.
I care more about my paycheck than the wellbeing of those around me.
I tend to be a bit of an airhead, not caring much about my surroundings.
I live in the moment, not caring enough to think about things.

background.name: Reincarnated

_.special: one martial or simple weapon of your choice

items.name: Skill Proficiencies
items.entry: {@skill History} and one more skill of your choice

items.name: Tool Proficiencies
items.entry: One artisan's tool of your choice or Talismans

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: One martial or simple weapon of your choice

entries: Reincarnated sorcerers used to live a millennia ago, being able to live through the ages by becoming cursed objects on their own or with exterior help. After finally becoming a vessel, they are now reincarnated into the modern world with new objectives and goals to accomplish.

entries.name: Feature: Golden Age of Jujutsu
entries: Before being resurrected, you have lived in the Heian Era, the golden age of jujutsu. This gave you massive knowledge about jujutsu and its arts. It is much easier for you to gain knowledge in jujutsu sorcery and to find out information about other sorcerers, taking only one fourth of the time to learn something jujutsu related such as cursed energy, seals, as it normally would compared to a normal sorcerer.

entries.name: Alternate Feature: New Objective
entries: You came back to this world for a single purpose, and you shall do it no matter what. Talk with your GM about what this purpose is, and how it affects your character story. Your GM can and should use your objective as a plot hook of sorts.

rows: Nothing gets me going more than a good fight!
All I want is some treasure.
I don't care about anything as long as I win.
I like to see the chaos unfold.
I have no reason for doing the things I do, I just do them.
Staying alive is my main goal.
I want to make sure my dream comes true.
I'll protect those who cannot be protected from the monsters brought back to this era.
Wonder. I wish to see what happens after my objective is complete. (Neutral)
Fighter. I want to fight a certain someone. (Chaotic)
Disciplined. I shall follow my principles even in this new era. (Lawful)
Savior. I revived just to stop the evil that will soon surface this era. (Good)
Destroyer. I will wreak havoc on this era. (Evil)
Redemption. I wish for another chance. (Any)
I was part of one of the great sorcerer families in the past.
I always felt and still feel alone due to how strong I am.
I have a sibling who also got resurrected.
I'm very fond of some people I have met in my new life.
I have a special someone, which has been revived alongside me.
I have been resurrected alongside someone I hate.
I'm too afraid of dying again, meaning I don't fight to win but to survive.
I want to cause pain and suffering to the descendants of my enemies.
I'm too obsessed with someone, to the point I revived for a chance to make them mine.
My fighting spirit is so great that I won't stop until I'm knocked unconscious or die.
I like to exploit the weaknesses of others.
I'll sacrifice others to achieve my goals.

background.name: New Shadow Style Student

_.special: a finesse martial weapon of your choice

items.name: Skill Proficiencies
items.entry: Choose two from {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Athletics}, {@skill History}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies
items.entry: Choose one type of artisan's tools or Talismans

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: A Jujutsu high uniform (commoner's clothes), a finesse martial weapon of your choice, and a pouch containing 5 gp

entries: The New Shadow Style is a fighting style that combines swordsmanship with the use of anti-domain techniques. It was created in the Heian Era by Sadatsuna Ashiya as a way for weaker sorcerers to fight against the stronger ones, with its main technique, a domain countermeasure called Simple Domain, being called the "domain for the weak". For the purposes of preventing outsiders and non-followers of the style to learn about it, the techniques were never documented, instead being passed down from person to person, which has led to many of its techniques being lost to time, as well as new other ones being introduced by different people. Being a student of the New Shadow School makes the basis for a solid and capable sorcerer.

entries.name: Feature: Dedicated Apprentice
entries: You started your career as a sorcerer diving in the ways of the New Shadow Style. Whenever you obtain New Shadow Style: Simple Domain, you'll start with 5 refinement. Additionally, you have advantage on History checks made to recall information about the New Shadow Style and individuals closely related to it.

rows: I am reasonable and level headed enough to not fight someone way stronger than me.
I am secretive about the details of my techniques so that they don't leak to the wrong people.
I care deeply about the people around me.
I am overly dedicated to studying the style.
I actually dislike learning this style.
I am not confident in my abilities.
I prefer to fight alongside others.
I want to use my abilities for the good of someone else.
Mentor-Like. I wish to teach a new generation of followers. (Lawful)
Protective. I'll use my skills to protect others. (Good)
Selfish. My learnings serve myself above any others. (Neutral)
Untrustworthy. I have hidden intentions to study this style. (Evil)
Uncontrollable. None shall tell me how to use my skills, not my peers nor my masters. (Chaotic)
Aspiring. The style is only a tool for further goals. (Any)
I want to create a new technique of my own.
I want to rediscover a technique lost to time.
I betrayed my old teacher and colleagues.
I come from a lineage of students of this school.
I have nothing in my life but this school.
I would die for my master.
I believe myself to be inherently more skilled than my colleagues.
I believe my skills make me able to defend myself without needing to rely on anyone else.
My lack of determination halts my skills.
I'm not good at working with others, even if I wanted to.
I have a tendency to distract myself easily, and sometimes I end up distracting others too.
I think of myself as useless due to my shortcomings.

background.name: Negative Manifestation

items.name: Skill Proficiencies
items.entry: Choose two from {@skill Athletics}, {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Perception}, {@skill Stealth}, {@skill Survival}

entries: Those who were created by negative emotions were negative manifested, also called cursed spirits. Curses are created when cursed energy leaks from (non-sorcerer) humans as a result of their harboring of negative emotions. Cursed energy thus gathers in one spot, building up like sediment until a cursed spirit takes form.

entries.caption: Form of Manifestation
rows: Imaginary Vengeful Cursed Spirit
Disease Cursed Spirit
Vengeful Cursed Spirit
Cursed Womb

entries.name: Feature: Imaginary Vengeful Cursed Spirit - Goal of Self
entries: You were manifested by the accumulated fear of the masses for something, that something is your entire being and you shall fight for it. You'll have a goal in mind that involves the fear you were created from. For example, a curse made from the fear of nature would want to preserve the same.

entries.name: Variant Feature: Natural Spirit
entries: You were manifested from negative emotions towards a sacred concept, such as nature. Due to that, you are different than most spirits, being able to be Neutral or even your own kind of Good. This also makes your cursed energy different and harder to detect. Whenever you have your cursed energy tracked, you count as having expertise in stealth and gain advantage on all rolls related to being tracked. Additionally, if you hide within your concept, such as the woods, your cursed energy becomes completely untrackable.

entries.name: Feature: Disease Cursed Spirit - Plague Knowledge
entries: You were manifested by the fear caused by a disease, this made you gain great knowledge about how diseases work. You have an easier time discovering and learning information about diseases.

entries.name: Feature: Vengeful Cursed Spirit - Back From the Darkness
entries: Instead of being born by the negative emotions generated by others, you were created by your own desire to get revenge. You are moved by said revenge, following any methods necessary to achieve it.

entries.name: Feature: Cursed Womb - Inoffensive Appearance
entries: You were manifested, however you weren't fully formed yet. Those around you will speculate and doubt your strength due to this factor, however those who know about the jujutsu world shall know that you can give birth to a new stronger cursed spirit.

rows: I think curses are inherently superior to humans.
I prey on the weak.
I think the power I hold is unstoppable.
I act as my curse nature tells me to.
I enjoy devouring humans.
I want to remain alone.
I believe curses should stick together.
I fight for the things I care about, no matter what they are.
Survival. I live to survive, anything else. (Neutral)
Curse. I will live as a true curse. (Chaotic)
Destruction. I'll destroy everything and everyone in my way. (Evil)
Humanity. I'll make curses become the new humans. (Any)
I take care of other curses.
I only live for myself.
I'll protect the things I value.
I have human relationships just for toying with them.
I think humans should grovel to me.
I like killing for fun.
I'm cowardly.
I follow my instincts no matter what they are.

background.name: Curse User

items.name: Skill Proficiencies
items.entry: Choose two from {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Athletics}, {@skill Survival}, {@skill Sleight of Hand}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Stealth}

items.name: Tool Proficiencies
items.entry: Choose one type of artisan's tools

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: A set of clothing (commoner's clothes) and a pouch containing 10 gp

entries: Curse Users are sorcerers who use jujutsu for evil and malicious deeds such as killing, they usually align with themselves or others with their ideals. Naturally, they have existed as long as jujutsu sorcerers have, and were also prominent threats during the Heian Era alongside cursed spirits.

entries.name: Feature: Cursed Underworld
entries: You are in frequent contact with people in the criminal underworld of Jujutsu Society that you or your group roam through. These people might be associated with the criminal underworld, or corrupt members of Jujutsu Society. This connection comes in the form of a contact in any city you visit, a person and/or fellow curse user who provides information about the people, things, places, and any other additional information that they may reveal to you.

entries.name: Alternate Feature: Turning a New Leaf
entries: After being a Curse User for as long as you have, you've elected to turn away from this life for whatever reason, be it love, agency, or otherwise, you've left it all behind. You may be wanted by others for the secrets you've learned over your time, and certain Higher-ups will be interested in your discoveries, you will know a certain amount of information about the criminal underworld that you may or may not reveal. (determined by the GM).

rows: I speak with an air of superiority, believing myself above all others.
My calm demeanor hides a deep, simmering anger at the world.
I find twisted joy in manipulating others, often just to pass the time.
I despise weakness, especially in myself, and will push beyond my limits to prove my strength.
Loyalty means everything to me, but betrayal is repaid a hundredfold.
I see the world as inherently unfair and align myself with chaos to level the playing field.
I approach every situation with detached curiosity, treating people like pawns in a game.
I am haunted by my own past, and I lash out at others to cope with my inner torment.
Power. Strength is the only currency that matters. (Evil)
Freedom. I refuse to be controlled by anyone or anything. (Chaotic)
Domination. Only the strong should decide the fate of the weak. (Evil)
Revolution. The world is broken, and I will break it further to rebuild it. (Chaotic)
Companionship. Even in darkness, I seek the solace of those who understand me. (Neutral)
Knowledge. True power lies in uncovering the hidden truths of existence. (Any)
I owe everything to a single person who showed me kindness when no one else would.
My family or clan rejected me, and I will never forgive them.
A rival bested me once, and I am obsessed with defeating them.
My ability defines who I am, and I'll protect it at all costs.
I fight for a cause, though my methods might be questionable.
I am bound by an oath that I cannot break, no matter how much it hurts me.
I am reckless, often rushing into danger without considering the consequences.
My arrogance blinds me to my own weaknesses.
I struggle to trust anyone, even my closest allies.
My emotions, particularly anger or sorrow, often cloud my judgment.
I take pleasure in the suffering of others, making it hard to form meaningful connections.
I am obsessed with a single goal, often ignoring everything else to achieve it.

item.name: Slaughter Demon
entries: When attacking with this {@item dagger|PHB} you will deal an additional {@damage d4} + your Charisma modifier of force damage.

item.name: Cursed Glasses
entries: While wearing these glasses, you will gain a bonus to your {@skill Perception} and {@skill Investigation} skills equal to your Charisma modifier. In addition, if you cannot see cursed spirits you may see them while wearing the glasses.

item.name: Cursed Revolver
entries: When attacking with this {@item revolver} you will deal an additional {@damage 2d8} + your Charisma modifier of force damage.

item.name: Cursed Spear
entries: When attacking with this {@item spear|PHB} you will deal an additional {@dice d6}({@dice d8} versatile) + your Charisma modifier of force damage.

item.name: Cursed Katana
entries: When attacking with the Cursed Katana you will deal an additional {@damage d8} + your Charisma modifier of force damage. This weapon is of the Finesse variant.

item.name: Cursed Tonfa
entries: When attacking with these tonfa you will deal an additional {@dice d4}({@dice d6} Standard Grip) + your Charisma modifier of force damage.

item.name: Cursed Bolt Action Rifle
entries: When attacking with this rifle you will deal an additional {@damage 2d8} + your Charisma modifier of force damage.

item.name: Cursed Shortbow
entries: When attacking with this bow you will deal an additional {@damage 1d6} + your Charisma modifier of force damage.

item.name: Dark Sword
entries: When attacking with the Dark Sword you will gain a +1 to attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@damage d8} + your Charisma modifier of force damage.

item.name: Cursed Glaive
entries: When attacking with the Cursed {@item Glaive|PHB} you will gain a +1 to attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@damage 1d12} + your Charisma modifier of force damage.

item.name: Cursed Hand Sword
entries: When attacking with the Cursed Hand Sword you will gain a +2 to attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@damage d10} + your Charisma modifier of force damage. In addition to this you can choose to remotely control it to move and attack as a bonus action. The weapon can move up to 25 feet in a single turn and can only go a maximum of 75 feet away from the user. When attacking remotely with this weapon you use your Charisma modifier for it's attack rolls, and deal {@damage 2d10} force damage while not adding your ability modifier to damage.

item.name: Cursed Battle Axe
entries: When attacking with the Cursed Battle Axe you will gain a +3 to attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@damage 2d10} + your Charisma modifier of force damage.

item.name: Dragon-Bone
entries: When attacking with Dragon-Bone you gain an additional +3 to attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@dice 2d6}({@dice 2d8} versatile) + your Charisma modifier force damage. Additionally this weapon has the ability of storing the force behind your attack. Each time you make an attack with this weapon against a creature record 1/3rd (rounded down) of the damage which is stored into the blade. Additionally, every time you attack a creature and they use a feature that spends cursed energy to defend against your attack, you may store a {@dice d6} of force damage. You can only store damage and cursed energy up to 3 times before having to release it as part of making an attack with the weapon. When released, your next attack will deal additional slashing damage equal to the accumulated damage stored up. This attack cannot have its energy stored, and all stored energy is lost if you miss the attack. The energy is stored until you complete a long rest.
The maximum amount of flat damage that the Dragon-Bone can store is determined by your proficiency bonus multiplied by 10.

item.name: Steel Arm
entries: When attacking with unarmed strikes you will gain a +3 to attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@damage 2d8} + your Charisma modifier of force damage.

The arm also has 100 hit points and is resistant to bludgeoning, slashing, and piercing damage. As a reaction to an incoming melee attack, ranged attack, or line or cone Dexterity saving throw, you may use the arm to take the damage instead. If the damage depletes the health of the arm, it shatters and the extra damage goes to you. As a bonus action, you can refill the health of the arm, spending cursed energy to heal it. For every cursed energy spent it regains 10 hit points, up to its original 100 hit points.

item.name: Claws of Calamity
entries: When attacking with unarmed strikes you will gain a +3 to attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@damage 2d12} + your Charisma modifier of force damage. Additionally, your unarmed strike damage becomes slashing damage while using the claws.

The arm can also help defend against attacks that are targeting your hands. When using the Limbs System variant rule, if a creature tries to target your hand or arm and deals damage to them, the Claws will block and take the damage instead. Each claw has 100 hit points, and as a bonus action, you can refill the health of the arm, spending cursed energy to heal it. For every cursed energy spent it regains 10 hit points, up to its original 100 hit points.

item.name: Nyoi Staff
entries: This {@item staff|PHB} acts as a {@item quarterstaff|PHB} with the additional reach property. When attacking with the Nyoi {@item Staff|PHB} you gain an additional +3 to your attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@dice 1d12} ({@dice 2d6} versatile) + your Charisma modifier force damage.

item.name: Blunt Sword
entries: You have a +4 to attack and damage rolls with this weapon, and it deals an extra {@damage 2d6} + your Charisma modifier in force damage. This cursed tool can be used to use the Ratio Technique for free 5 times per encounter as per in the Technique Imbued Cursed Tools rule.

item.name: Serpent Eyes and Fang
entries: This cursed tool is a megaphone imbued with the Cursed Speech technique, it allows the wielder to use the famous cursed speech technique. As a bonus action while wielding Serpent Eyes and Fang, you can use any of the cursed words on the cursed speech as long as you have Cursed Energy for it. You will suffer the same consequences a normal cursed speech user would. After you have spent 20 Cursed Energy, the item evaporates.

item.name: Playful Cloud
entries: This weapon must be wielded with two hands, and has a {=bonusWeapon} to its attack and damage rolls with this weapon, and it deals an extra {@damage 2d8} + your Charisma modifier added four times in force damage. When using playful cloud as part of an attack action you may make an additional strike with the weapon. In addition, due to the weapon being created to use your own might and power, you can add your Strength modifier four times, instead of once, to the damage rolled.

item.name: Chain of a Thousand Miles
entries: The Chain of a Thousand miles possesses a virtually infinite length so long as at least one end cannot be seen. This chain can be attached to other weapons and extend the reach of said weapon as far as your Strength can allow. For every point your Strength modifier has the chain can extend a weapon's range by 20 feet. So for example if your strength modifier is a +4, the weapon's range can be extended by up to 80 feet.

However, this has a downside. Whenever a non-attack related feature of the weapon would be used during time until the start of your next turn after using the chain, you have to compare your Strength modifier with the attack bonus or save DC - 8 of the attack or feature which would necessitate such a feature, if any. If your Strength modifier is lower, you can't pull the weapon back in time to use the feature.

item.name: Inverted Spear of Heaven
entries: When attacking with the Inverted {@item Spear|PHB} of Heaven you will receive a +4 bonus to attack and damage rolls and additional {@damage 1d12} + your Charisma modifier force damage. This weapon cannot be affected by a cursed technique feature (such as Blazing Courage for example).

entries.name: Forced Release
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using the Inverted {@item Spear|PHB} of Heaven they are forced to release any active Innate Techniques. Any active durations, effects, or sustained abilities will be ended. This also applies to the attack roll you hit that triggers this effect. You can use this when attacking an active innate technique feature or spell to dispel it upon contact. (For example, making an attack roll against a Blue, dispelling it upon hitting it.

Once per round, you can also use this as a reaction to being affected by an innate technique cursed energy attack roll or saving throw that you can physically attack using the Inverted {@item Spear|PHB} of Heaven. Make an attack roll with the Inverted {@item Spear|PHB} of Heaven against the attack roll or save DC. If your attack roll is higher than the cursed energy attack roll, or 3 or more higher than the save DC, you successfully negate and end the effect.

You must be aware of the technique in order to use this feature. A creature with the Inverted {@item Spear|PHB} of Heaven lodged into their body cannot use any Innate Technique features until it is removed.

item.name: Black Rope
entries: This tool has a +4 bonus to attack and damage rolls, and it deals an additional {@damage 1d12} + your Charisma modifier force damage on a hit. This weapon starts with a total of 200 hit points. While wielding it you gain an additional reaction exclusively for defending yourself with the rope. As a reaction to being hit by an attack imbued with cursed energy you're able to use the rope in order to have the rope take the damage in your place. The rope has resistance to all damage imbued with cursed energy. Additionally as an action you can attempt to restrain an enemy with the rope who can either make a Dexterity saving throw to avoid it, or a Strength save to break out of it, against a DC equal to (8 + your Dexterity or Strength modifier + proficiency bonus). Upon being caught by the rope the target will be considered {@condition restrained} and unable to use cursed techniques. They can attempt to break free from the rope at the start of each of their turns by making a Strength saving throw against the DC, escaping on a success and remaining trapped on upon failure. You may also have this effect when grappling a creature while wielding the Black Rope. Attacks from the rope will ignore defensive cursed techniques, and whenever they do the rope takes damage equal to the cursed energy spent on the defensive technique.

Finally on a short or long rest you can imbue cursed energy into the rope to restore its lost hit points. On a short rest you can imbue as much Cursed Energy as your Charisma modifier, and on a long rest you can imbue as much as you'd like. The rope heals 15 hp per Cursed Energy spent.

item.name: Split Soul Katana
entries: When attacking with the Soul Split Katana you gain an additional {=bonusWeapon} to your attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@dice 2d8}({@dice 2d10} versatile) + your Charisma modifier force damage. In addition, this powerful cursed tool has additional effects based on your ability to perceive the Soul.
Object Soul Awareness If you possess Object Outline Awareness then the sword ignores temporary hit points, damage reductions, resistances, and immunities and is considered Minor Soul Harming.

Soul Outline Awareness If you possess Object Outline Awareness and Soul Outline Awareness, then the sword ignores temporary hit points, damage reductions, resistances, and immunities and is considered Major Soul Harming.

item.name: Festering Life Sword
entries: When attacking with the Festering Life Sword you gain an additional +4 to your attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@damage 2d6} + your charisma modifier force damage. In addition, you can launch an egg into someone as a part of an attack, once per turn. Doing this deals an extra {@damage 6d8} force damage as the hatches out of the target and creates a Larva. Leaving this larva alone for 1 full turn gives it time to evolve into a Nutshell Shikigami. To prevent this evolution, you must kill the larva. There are 6 eggs in the sword. You regain all eggs on a long rest unless you are an Earthen Insect Trance user. If you are one, you can spend 15 cursed energy to refill all 6.

item.name: Supreme Martial Solution
entries: When attacking with the Supreme Martial Solution you gain an additional +4 to your attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@damage 1d12} + your Charisma modifier lightning damage. When wielding this weapon, you may spend 10 cursed energy to send out a blast of lightning as a ranged attack roll at a range of 60/300. On a hit, the blast will deal {@damage 15d12} lightning damage. This attack can be used once per turn, and may replace one of your melee attacks.

item.name: Prosthetic Vibraslap
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the Boogie Woogie Innate Technique
entries: When you become attuned to the Prosthetic Vibraslap you choose one of your hands to become permanently lost and unable to be regained for the duration of the attunement, replaced by the Prosthetic Vibraslap. While you are attuned to the Prosthetic Vibraslap the amount of reactions you have only for using Boogie Woogie is doubled.

item.name: Hiten
entries: When attacking with Hiten you gain a +4 to your attack and damage rolls, and deal an additional {@dice 2d6}({@dice 2d8} Versatile) + your Charisma modifier force damage. When holding Hiten, you can spend 5 cursed energy to summon a puff of cloud within 60 feet of you. You or someone else can stand in the puff of cloud and gain a flying speed equal to the riders walking speed, but this lasts for a number of rounds equal to the holder's Charisma modifier. The action cost is a bonus action to summon and a free action to mount.

entries.name: Supreme Martial Solution
entries: If someone were to have both Supreme Martial Solution and Hiten can create a special reaction between the two cursed tools. If the lightning specifically targets Hiten's cloud, due to the compatible cursed energy the cloud absorbs the electricity then erupts in a massive electrical explosion. Every person within 60 feet must make a Dexterity saving throw and taking {@damage 30d12} lightning damage in a failure. Half as much on a success. This can only be done once per long rest as the cursed energy will not synergize for a period of time.

optionalfeature.name: Limitless
entries: The Limitless technique is the most powerful Innate Technique in the jujutsu world. This technique uses jujutsu to bring the convergence and divergence of an infinite series into reality, allowing the user to freely manipulate and distort space. Only those who possess the Six Eyes can fully master the Limitless due to the complex cursed energy manipulation required to wield it.
When you choose this innate technique, you gain access to the following features as you level up:

items: {@optfeature Infinity|jujutsukaisen} (1st level)
{@optfeature Blue|jujutsukaisen} (3rd level)
{@optfeature Teleport Technique|jujutsukaisen} (6th level)
{@optfeature Blue Infused Strikes|jujutsukaisen} (10th level)
{@optfeature Delayed Explosions|jujutsukaisen} (14th level, Tokyo School Feature)
{@optfeature Simultaneous Casting|jujutsukaisen} (20th level)
