entries: Additionally, if you obtain the Cursed Technique Reversal cursed feat, you gain access to {@optfeature Cursed Technique Reversal: Red|jujutsukaisen}.

optionalfeature.name: Infinity
entries: Starting at 1st level, you gain the ability to use your manipulation over space to protect yourself more effectively. As an action or Reaction to being targeted by an attack or effect that would cause you damage that you can see for 3 Cursed Energy, you can cause the space around you to divide into infinite fractions for 1 minute. Whenever you are targeted by a weapon, grapple, melee cursed energy attack roll, ranged cursed energy attack roll or any saving throws from sources outside your body, you may use a Reaction to spend 1 Cursed Energy and roll a {@dice d20}. On an 11 or higher, any attacks made against you automatically fail to hit or harm you in any physical way. At 6th level, you must roll a 7 or higher, and at 11th level, you must roll a 4 or higher. Additionally, you gain a number of additional Reactions equal to your Charisma modifier + your proficiency bonus that can be used exclusively for this feature. You must Concentrate on this feature in order for it to remain active.

When you take the Dodge action while Infinity is activated, you may use your Infinity Reactions to defend a number of creatures within your reach equal to your number of arms with Infinity alongside yourself.

Regardless of level, this feature only works on things that travel and can be stopped. Abilities that do not physically travel will not be stopped by Infinity. For example, Eye contact, mind control, basilisk stare, making things explode with your mind, etc.

entries.name: Infinity Pressure
entries: At 5th level, you have learned to imbue more cursed energy into your infinity, reinforcing it against Domain Amplification. As a bonus action while Infinity is active or while you activate it, you may spend Cursed Energy up to half of your level(rounded up) to increase its density and sheer force. Every creature that has Domain Amplification active, that starts their turn within 5ft. of you must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are pushed back 5 ft. and are knocked prone. On a success, they are not pushed back and they do not become prone. If knocked against a wall of stone or into a solid surface (consult your GM), their saving throw becomes against taking 1d12 force damage for every cursed energy spent, and being restrained, as they are crushed against the wall, and for every damage the creature suffers the object or wall behind it suffers the same damage in either situation on failure. You must spend a number of Cursed Energy equal to the amount spent on activation at the beginning of your turns to keep this form of Infinity active. If a creature has spent equal to or more cursed energy than you on Domain Amplification, they are unaffected by Infinity Pressure.
While infinity pressure is active, your speed is reduced to 10ft, and you cannot make melee attack rolls.

In addition, if a creature is using Domain Amplification in order to negate infinity, infinity will not be nullified if you have spent a greater amount of cursed energy on infinity pressure than they have spent on domain amplification. You cannot use Maximum Output to bypass domain amplification. You may use infinity pressure as a reaction to a creature hitting you with a melee attack roll while they have domain amplification is active.

entries.name: Semi-Perfect Infinity
entries: At 11th level, Infinity works against poisons.

entries.name: Perfect Infinity
entries: At 17th level, you may spend 6 additional Cursed Energy when using this feature to automatically roll a 20.

optionalfeature.name: Blue
entries: At 3rd level, as an amplification of limitless, you may directly manipulate space. As an action for 5 Cursed Energy, you may cause space to be erased within 30 ft of you causing a medium sized blue sphere with a 10 ft. circle attraction radius to draw everything towards it for 1 minute. All creatures within this area must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, they take {@dice 6d8} force damage, are restrained until the beginning of their next turn, and are pulled to an open space closest to the sphere. On a success, they take half as much damage and are not restrained or pulled. A creature can retry this saving throw at the beginning of each of their turns. If they succeed, they take half the damage and exit the sphere. If they fail, they remain there without being able to move and retake the damage. Any creatures that enter the sphere's range or start their turns in the sphere's range must also make the Strength saving throw, but a creature can only be subject to the saving throw once per round. This does not affect the repeated save at the beginning of a creature's turn. You can dispel this technique as a free action. You may only have a single Blue active at a time.

At 5th level this technique's damage increases to {@dice 7d8}, then again at 11th level {@dice 9d8}, 17th level {@dice 12d8}, and 20th level {@dice 16d8}. Objects within the area that aren't being worn or carried also take this damage.

At 13th level, when you use your blue technique in its Maximum Output form you may spend a bonus action to move the sphere a number of ft. equal to your proficiency bonus times 10. All creatures currently stuck in the sphere will be moved as well, and any creatures caught in the range of the sphere moving must do the saving throw. A creature can still only be subjected to the saving throw once per round.

entries.name: Blue Redirection
entries: Whenever a projectile or a ranged attack roll with a line area passes through Blue's area of effect, it is attracted to the center of the sphere. If said technique or attack would deal less damage than Blue, it is pulled in and stopped completely, missing its target in the case of an attack and being cut short in case of a line effect. If it is a projectile, it also takes Blue's damage. In the case of a line effect, it now changes its trajectory, with its area starting from the first point where it entered Blue's area of effect and ending at the center of the sphere. If any other creatures are in that area, they are affected by the line effect as normal. If the attack or line effect would deal more damage than Blue, it ignores this and works as normal, but its damage is reduced by Blue's damage.

entries.name: Pulse Blue
entries: You can choose to fire off your blue in a quick burst, weaponizing its attraction effect for damage rather than utility. As an action for 3 Cursed Energy, you can cast blue, instead firing it as a ranged cursed energy attack roll against a creature within 60 ft of you. This use of blue does half of blue's damage, and does not pull creatures or linger after it has been cast.

entries.name: Blue Manipulation
entries: At 5th level, you have learned how to use blue to manipulate space more precisely. As an action for an amount of cursed energy depending on the size, you can choose an object or creature within 50 ft., moving it a number of feet equal to your proficiency bonus times 10. If it collides with an object, they both take {@dice 1d12} bludgeoning damage. If it collides with a creature or structure, they both take Collision Damage based on the object's size, making a Dexterity saving throw, taking half as much damage on a success. Any adjustments used to enhance Blue besides effects that increase your damage dice also apply to this (including range increases and Cursed Energy cost reductions).

entries.caption: Blue Manipulation Costs
rows: Tiny
{@dice 1d4} + your Charisma modifier bludgeoning damage
Small
{@dice 2d6} + your Charisma modifier bludgeoning damage
Medium
{@dice 2d8} + your Charisma modifier added twice bludgeoning damage
Large
{@dice 2d10} + Charisma modifier added twice bludgeoning damage
Huge
{@dice 2d12} + Charisma modifier added three times bludgeoning damage
Gargantuan
{@dice 4d12} + Charisma modifier added three times bludgeoning damage

entries: Depending on the nature of the object or structure you are moving, your DM may allow you to rip things planted or rooted to something, such as pulling a specific portion of a bridge.

{@bold Moving a Creature.} You can pull a creature, using the chart above for their size. Make a contested check against the creature, with you using your Cursed Energy Modifier, and the creature using Strength. If you succeed, the creature is pulled up to the normal range. On a failure, they are not moved. A creature can choose to fail this check. A creature counts as their size for the purposes of Collision Damage.

After using Blue Manipulation on your turn, you can immediately use it again as a bonus action. You can make these instances of Blue Manipulation occur at the same time, so as to make objects or creatures collide.
If you have Simultaneous Casting you can spend up to your Charisma modifier in additional cursed energy when using Blue Manipulation, gaining a bonus to the check equal to the amount spent.
You can use Blue Manipulation out of combat to freely manipulate tiny objects at no cursed energy cost.

optionalfeature.name: Teleport Technique
entries: At 6th level, you have learned how to manipulate the space-time around you and how to bend it. When you take the movement action, you can spend 1 cursed energy to teleport to the location, causing you to not provoke attacks of opportunity. The first attack roll you do after taking the teleport action is made with advantage unless the target has a higher passive perception than your cursed energy DC.

As a reaction for 5 Cursed Energy when you are targeted by an attack, melee cursed energy attack roll, ranged cursed energy attack roll, or any saving throws, you may teleport up to an empty space up to your movement speed in feet, potentially causing the attack or effect to miss if you become out of range or area. If it was an attack roll, you must make a Dexterity check against the attack roll, successfully escaping it on a success. If it was a saving throw or effect, make a Dexterity check against the Save DC of the effect or saving throw, on a success avoiding the effect. If you fail the Dexterity check, you will still be subject to the effect, teleporting after it occurs. You can only use one of these teleport features per round.

When you move and for 6 Cursed Energy, you can use blue as a way to propel you into the air, giving you flying (hover) speed equal to your walking speed for 1 minute. After this minute ends, you can spend another 1 Cursed Energy to keep this active for an additional minute, and so on. You can also grant this benefit to the other creatures you're sharing Infinity with or that you're currently holding.
When using this feature there must be an unobstructed path to where you wish to teleport, even if you could normally not travel such a path.

entries.name: Blue Augmented Speed
entries: At 11th level, you learn to manipulate the space around you by utilizing Blue to increase your speed. By spending Cursed Energy up to your Charisma modifier as a Bonus Action, you can increase your movement speed by 10 ft per energy spent. This lasts for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

entries.name: Space Manipulation
entries: At 14th level, due to your constant practice with the teleport technique, you have learned how to move long distances. You can now cast teleport as an action for 10 Cursed Energy by utilizing your teleport technique, and instead of only needing to be on the same plane of existence it needs to be within an amount of miles equal to 10 times your jujutsu sorcerer level. If you have the Six Eyes background feat, you always get the exact location you want to go and the range is extended to simply being on the same planet. This application of teleportation still requires a clear path, such as the open sky or an opening of a building.

optionalfeature.name: Blue Infused Strikes
entries: At 10th level, you have learned how to imbue blue into your strikes. Whenever you hit an unarmed strike, you can spend up to 6 cursed energy, dealing an additional {@dice d8} force damage per energy spent. This damage cannot be increased by features from the Tokyo school nor can it benefit from the Improved Cursed Energy Output feat.

optionalfeature.name: Delayed Explosions
entries: At 14th level, you have learned how to delay your Innate Technique to create surprise explosions. Whenever you use your Blue (or Red if you have the reversal) technique, you may reduce their size and make them not explode, delaying them. All hostile creatures must make a Perception check against your cursed energy DC. If any feature raises the delayed technique's DC or imposes disadvantage on the targeted creatures, it also affects this check.

On a failure, they don't notice it and think it already exploded, vanished, or missed. You may choose for the technique to activate as normal, dealing half as much damage and only afterwards enter its delayed state. If you choose to do that with Blue, it does not keep its lingering effect. While in this state, once per turn you may move the delayed technique during your turn, no action required, through the air, up to your movement speed. A technique can be delayed like this for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). You may spend a bonus action to make them explode, doubling their effect's range, and imposing disadvantage on the saving throw. If the creature succeeds in the Perception check, it may make its saving throw as normal. While in their delayed state, your Blue and Red have an Armor Class equal to your Cursed Energy DC. It automatically fails all saving throws, and it is immune to psychic and poison damage. If they take damage in this form, they will explode prematurely.

entries.name: Explosive Purple
entries: If you possess the Hollow Purple feat, you can utilize your Red and Blue to create an explosive version of your Hollow Purple, not bound by any specific target. If you make a delayed red or blue and a blue or red enter the same space, you may use a free action to have them merge, causing them to become an Explosive Purple, which you can then detonate as a bonus action. When you cause it to explode, a massive purple that erases everything will form. Every creature within a 200 ft circle centered on the Explosive Purple must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take Hollow Purple's damage. On a success, they take half as much damage.
Any enhancements placed on Hollow Purple affect Explosive Purple as well.

optionalfeature.name: Simultaneous Casting
entries: Finally, at 20th level, you have mastered the art of utilizing Blue. When you cast Blue you may pay its cost an additional amount of times up to half your Charisma modifier (rounded down), creating an additional Blue within 5 ft. of the initial blue for each additional time you pay the cost. The additional blues will deal the damage of the original and will not restrain the target. Alternatively, you can shoot them like projectiles, making a ranged cursed energy attack roll against a target within 60 feet of you, doing your blue's damage on a hit. These attacks can replace any number of attacks in the attack action.
You may not use Delayed Explosions, Maximum Output, Chanting, or any damage-increasing features or effects, including those from Domain Expansions or Tokyo School, on a Blue cast in this way.

optionalfeature.name: Cursed Technique Reversal: Red
entries: You may invert Limitless using Reverse Cursed Technique, creating a blast that expands space. As an action for 10 Cursed Energy, choose a point within 40 feet. All creatures within the range of the explosion centered on that point must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are knocked back 10 × your Charisma modifier feet (minimum 10 feet), take {@dice 16d8} Force damage, and are knocked prone. On a success, the damage is halved and they are not knocked back nor prone. The explosion range can be a radius from 5 ft. up to 30 ft. (your choice). You may only have a single Red active at a time.

Alternatively, you may make a ranged cursed energy attack roll against a creature up to 120 feet away, shooting a more precise version of red that targets only said creature. On a hit, the creature takes Red's damage, and must make the Strength saving throw as normal to be knocked back.
At 17th level, it increases to {@dice 24d8} and again at 20th level to {@dice 32d8}.
This technique deals twice as much damage to objects and structures. Objects within the area that aren't being worn or carried also take this damage.

optionalfeature.name: Ten Shadows Technique
entries: The Ten Shadows Technique is the prized inherited technique of the Zenin clan. This technique allows the user to use shadows as an intermediary to summon ten different shikigami.
When you choose this innate technique, you gain access to the following features as you level up:

items: {@optfeature Shikigami Summoning|jujutsukaisen} (1st level)
{@optfeature Shadow Manipulation|jujutsukaisen} (3rd level)
{@optfeature The Well's Unknown Abyss|jujutsukaisen} (6th level)
{@optfeature Shadow Manipulation Expansion|jujutsukaisen} (10th level)
{@optfeature Incomplete Shadows|jujutsukaisen} (14th level, Tokyo School Feature)
{@optfeature Shikigami Gigantification|jujutsukaisen} (20th level)

entries: Additionally, if you obtain the Cursed Technique Reversal cursed feat, you gain access to {@optfeature Light Manipulation|jujutsukaisen}.

optionalfeature.name: Shikigami Summoning
entries: Starting at 1st level, you have the prized technique of the Zenin clan, the ten shadows earned to summon shikigami from the shadows. As an action for 1 cursed energy you may summon up to 2 Divine Dogs (S-2) anywhere within 5 feet of you. Any shikigami summoned as part of this technique last until they are reduced to 0 hit points, you dismiss them at will, or you take a short or long rest. They regain all lost hit points at the end of a long rest.
Divine Dogs count as 1 shikigami for summoning and controlling purposes.
To control and command your shikigami, use the Summon Commanding Rules and you may command a number of summons equal to your Charisma modifier.
Additionally, the 10 Shadows Technique comes with a trial, in order to gain other shikigami, you must subjugate the Shikigami.

entries.name: Trial
entries: As you level, you will gain access to your other shikigami, and you must face them in combat alone to gain control over them. As a full round action(which is completed at the beginning of your next turn), you may summon any shikigami that are on the table below within 5 feet of you. If you haven't battled against the untamed shikigami and won, it will be under the GM's control, act on their own turn and be hostile to you. If you have battled against the untamed shikigami alone (does not include using your tamed ten shadows shikigami) and reduce it to 0 hit points, you will now be able to summon the shikigami under your control, tamed. The distance which you can summon an untamed shikigami cannot be increased.

You can choose any creatures within 15 feet of you or the untamed shikigami to become part of the trial until either they or the untamed shikigami are dead. If you are reduced to 0 hit points or are killed while any creature other than the untamed shikigami is within the trial, you will enter a state of stasis until the ritual is properly complete, when your death will be completed. If the ritual is interrupted before it is properly concluded, such as an outsider killing the untamed shikigami before all other creatures in the ritual have been killed, then you will instead begin rolling death saves when the ritual concludes, if applicable. Additionally, if you are reduced to 0 hit points, the untamed shikigami becomes hostile to all other creatures within the trial.

entries.caption: Shikigami Summoning Costs
rows: 2 cursed energy
Gama (S-2)
Level 1
4 cursed energy
Nue (S-2)
Level 3
3 cursed energy
Rabbit Escape (S-2)
Level 5
5 cursed energy
Great Serpent (S-1)
Level 6
6 cursed energy
Round Deer (S-2)
Level 8
7 cursed energy
Max Elephant (S-2)
Level 10
8 cursed energy
Mourn Tiger (S-2)
Level 12
9 cursed energy
Piercing Ox (S-2)
Level 14
10 cursed energy
Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sila Divine General Mahoraga (S-2, V-1)
Level 17

entries: Additionally, should any one of your tamed shikigami die, they can no longer be summoned ever again. Should this happen, they are now eligible for Totality.

entries.name: Summoning Techniques
entries: When you defeat an untamed shikigami in battle, you gain a new special form to summon it.
You can summon the shikigami as an action.
The distance you can summon your shikigami is 30 feet.

{@bold Rabbit Escape.} You may summon Rabbit Escape for 3 cursed energy as a Reaction to an attack made against you and add your Charisma modifier to your AC against that attack. If this causes the attack to miss, you reappear in any place within your movement speed range. This movement does not generate opportunity attacks.

{@bold Round Deer.} You may summon Round Deer as a reaction to being afflicted by a cursed technique or a cursed energy fueled feature, causing it to appear within 5 feet of you, and using its Neutralize Energy action(if applicable). Doing so uses Round Deer's action for the round.
To use the other special summoning techniques, you must choose a Ten Shadows Feat.

entries.name: Totality
entries: Additionally, if one of your summons is killed, you can use a bonus action to grant one of your summons its Totality form. You cannot use totality on a Shikigami that is already summoned and you cannot have more than 2 shikigami in a totality, which is the living shikigami and the deceased shikigami. When you make a totality, the creature gains the strengths of the deceased shikigami. They will gain the following changes:

items: Their Strength, Dexterity, Constitution and Charisma scores become those of the creature who has the higher score. Then they will increase by the modifier of the creature with the smaller scores.(Ex: Nue has 14 Strength while divine dog has 17, their totality would have 19 Strength.)
When you combine two shikigami of the same size, the totalities size increases by 1
Their AC will become that of the creature with the higher AC.
When making a totality, the base shikigami gains the skill and saving throw proficiency's of the shikigami used in the totality.
They receive the amount of hit die of both creatures combined, with the size of the hit die being determined as normal.
Their movement speed is added together.(Ex: Two creatures with 30 movement speed would have 60 movement speed in totality form)

They will receive all features, traits, actions, bonus actions, reactions, legendary actions, skill, saving throw proficiencies, damage resistances, damage immunities, condition immunities, and senses from both creatures.
Their size will become the size of the bigger creature.

Their CR will become that of the creature who has a higher CR. Then it will increase by half the CR of the creature with the smaller CR rounded up.(Ex: Nue's CR is 4, and Great Serpent's CR is 5, their totality's CR would be 7)

After calculating the ability scores, the difference in the ability modifier from the original user of a damaging feature and the totality is added to the amount of dice. This value can not increase the damage of a feature to over double its original value in dice. You apply this before Stronger shadows. For save attack damage, the stat corresponding to the save is used. (Ex: Mourn Tiger has a +7 in Dexterity and Nue has a +3. Mourn Tiger's Dexterity Modifier increases by +1, meaning you add 1 additional dice to the Totality's attack to the damage dice that use Dexterity as the ability modifier, changing Mourn Tiger's bite attack from a {@dice 2d10+7} into a {@dice 3d10+8}.) This increase only applies to direct attack rolls and saving throws.(excluding Natural Weight) This value is halved(rounded down to a minimum of 1) for attacks that can be used through a multi-attacking feature.(Ex: Nue's Wings and Talon)

entries: You can not make a Totality using Rabbit Escape or Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sila Divine General Mahoraga. You only apply Stronger Shadows to the shikigami after they are in the totality form, so the calculations above are made using their stats without Stronger Shadows. When summoning a totality, you may choose to instead summon the base shikigami being the living shikigami before being made into a totality.

entries.name: Stronger Shadows
entries: Your shikigami grow alongside you as you become stronger. Your Shikigami can gain Stronger Shadows counters in the following ways:
{@bold Shikigami Reserve:} Your tamed shikigami gain a number of Stronger Shadows counters equal to 1/20th of your cursed energy maximum (Rounded Down.) These counters are applied passively.

{@bold Shikigami Output:} When you summon a tamed shikigami, you may spend additional cursed energy up to half of your Charisma modifier (rounded down), to apply stronger shadows counters to the shikigami, for every 2 cursed energy spent the shikigami will gain a Stronger Shadows counter. Stronger Shadows counters applied this way will last for a number of hours equal to your Charisma modifier, and while applied to a shikigami, you cannot apply more Stronger Shadows counters to the shikigami in this way, unless you remove the Stronger Counters counters applied this way (no action required).

At 11th level, you can spend up to your Charisma modifier. At 17th level, you can spend up to your Charisma modifier plus half of your Charisma modifier (rounded down). At 20th level, you can spend up to your Charisma modifier added twice.
No matter from which source, a shikigami can only possess a maximum of 5 stronger shadows counters at any time, with the exception of Divine Dog, which can have 10.
For each stronger shadows counter applied, the shikigami will gain the following benefits:

items: {@bold Overall Increase.} They gain a +1 bonus to their Strength, Dexterity, Constitution and Charisma scores. Their AC is not increased by ability scores increasing.
{@bold Vitality Increase.} They gain an additional hit die.
{@bold Speed Increase.} They gain 5 additional feet of movement speed.
{@bold Attack Power Increase.} Their attack rolls damage die increases by one die.
{@bold Power Increase.} Their dice for features that force creatures to make a saving throw increases one die.
{@bold Defense Increase.} Their Armor Class increases by 1. This does not apply to Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sila Divine General Mahoraga.

optionalfeature.name: Shadow Manipulation
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you've learned to make use of your ability to manipulate shadows, allowing you to form an extra dimensional space to hide your things and slightly manipulate the shadow of other creatures. You gain the following features:

entries.name: Shadow Storage
entries: You may store objects in an extra-dimensional space within your shadow. You may physically place something into your shadow, costing no cursed energy, however you must spend 1 cursed energy to interact with an object in this space, potentially removing it. All items on your shadow still count against your carrying capacity. Your shikigami count as objects for the purpose of this feature. Additionally, you may trap hostile creatures within your shadow storage. As a Bonus Action for 2 cursed energy, you may force a creature standing on darkness to make a Dexterity (Acrobatics) check against your Cursed Energy DC, they make this check with Disadvantage if they are completely covered in darkness or if they are inside a veil. If the creature is inside your domain then they automatically fail this check, unless they possess the ability to stand or run on liquids. On a success, they don't fall into your shadow storage. On a failure, they are trapped inside your shadow storage. Their weight counts against your carrying capacity. They can hold their breath for a number of minutes equal to their Constitution modifier (minimum of 1) before beginning to suffocate. If a creature has failed the Dexterity (Acrobatics) check, they can attempt a Strength or a Dexterity (their choice) saving throw at the end of their turns, freeing themselves on a success and appearing in an unoccupied space within 5ft.

entries.name: Shadow Ridge
entries: Once per round, as a reaction for 1 Cursed Energy against a melee attack roll, you can force a creature within 10ft of you to make said attack with disadvantage as their legs fall into their own shadow momentarily.

optionalfeature.name: The Well's Unknown Abyss
entries: At 6th level, you can combine two of your shikigami into a single fusion, sacrificing their individual strength for a versatile but weaker form. As an action, you can spend 6 Cursed Energy to fuse a base shikigami with an enhancer shikigami, granting the fused shikigami the shikigami Alteration feature of the enhancer. The fusion ends if it drops to 0 hit points or if you dismiss it as a bonus action. When fused, the shikigami follows these rules:

items: Designate one shikigami as the base and another as the enhancer.
The fused shikigami inherits all traits of both shikigami and gains the Shikigami Alteration feature from the enhancer.
It gains no immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities from either shikigami.
Its hit points are half of the base shikigami's hit points.
Its speed is half of base Shikigami's speeds rounded down in increments of 5 feet.
Its ability scores are half of the base shikigami's ability scores.
The fused shikigami's size matches the base shikigami.
The fused shikigami's AC matches the base shikigami.
The fused shikigami gains one condition immunity of your choice from either shikigami.
The fusion's CR equals the base shikigami's CR plus half of the enhancer's CR.

The fused shikigami's size can be reduced, and its quantity increased. For every size reduction, the number of shikigami increases by two, up to a limit of times equal to half the summoner's proficiency bonus (rounded up). For example, if the base shikigami is Large, the summoner can reduce its size to Medium and summon 3 fusion shikigami, or further reduce it to Small to summon 5 fusion shikigami. The multiple shikigami created this way will only count as 1 shikigami for the purposes of summoning, controlling, commanding and any reactions it has.

When the size is reduced and quantity is increased, the CR, STR and CON stats, as well as the hit points of the base shikigami and cursed energy are split evenly among the summoned shikigami. This split applies only to the fusion and does not affect the original shikigami's individual stats when unfused.

If the fused shikigami is split into multiple shikigami, the damage of all damage dealing features will be evenly split between each fusion shikigami. The dice tier of these damage dealing features is also reduced by each step the base shikigami's size is reduced.

entries: This ability cannot be used to fuse Rabbit Escape or Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sila Divine General Mahoraga. If the fused shikigami is destroyed, you may resummon the original shikigami individually as long as the base shikigami was not destroyed. You may only have one fusion. You only apply Stronger Shadows to the shikigami after they are in the fused form, so the calculations above are made using their stats without Stronger Shadows. Shikigami Gigantification cannot be used with The Well's Unknown Abyss in any step.

entries.caption: Shikigami Alteration Feature
rows: Divine Dogs
Gives Pack Tactics feature
Gama
Gives the Support Carry, Tongue, Swallow and Tongue Grab features
Great Serpent
Gives Shadow Burrow feature, 30 ft. burrow speed and 30 ft. swim speed
Nue
Gives 80 ft fly speed
Round Deer
Grants the Reverse Cursed Technique feature, the fusion will use its scores and CR to determine its RCT pool and cursed energy pool
Piercing Ox
It will grant the Battering Ram feature and the Gore attack, the fusion will use its scores to determine the attack and damage bonuses.
Mourn Tiger
It will grant the Sneak Attack feature
Max Elephant
Increases hit dice by 20

optionalfeature.name: Shadow Manipulation Expansion
entries: At 10th level, you got better at manipulating shadows, becoming able to even manipulate to the shadows of others and even use shadows in esoteric ways never before seen, you can use the following techniques:

entries.name: Shadow Merge
entries: You get better at manipulating shadows, becoming able to connect to the shadows of others and even summoning your creatures from behind them. As a free action or during a short or long rest, you can connect to the shadow of a creature within 120ft of you. As a bonus action for 3 cursed energy, you may teleport behind this creature's shadow from up to 120 feet away. Also, if you have any of your shikigami summoned you can spend 3 additional cursed energy to make one of them be teleported alongside you.

As a bonus action for 3 cursed energy, you can sink into your or another creature whose shadow you're connected to. While inside the shadow, you are considered invisible and under total cover until you take any type of action, which makes you leave the shadow within 5 feet of the creature. You are also removed from the shadow if the creature is knocked unconscious. You can stay under a creature's shadow for up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) hours.

In case you utilize this feature on a hostile creature, the creature makes a Wisdom (Perception) check against your cursed energy DC. On a success they know you are behind them, on a failure they will be surprised. If you were completely hidden before teleporting to said creature's shadow, it makes the Perception check with disadvantage.

You can connect to the shadows of a number of creatures up to your proficiency bonus. Whenever you try to connect to a new shadow after reaching the maximum, you will choose which shadow to lose your connection to.

entries.name: Shadow Summon
entries: You've learnt how to impede your opponents through their own shadows. When you spend cursed energy to summon your Shikigami, you can spend an extra 6 Cursed Energy to summon your Shikigami through the shadow of the creature you're in the shadow of. When summoned this way, you can command your Shikigami to take one action it is capable of. If it is an attack roll, the one action has advantage. And if it forces a saving throw, the target has disadvantage.
Additionally, you can now summon your Shikigami as a bonus action.

optionalfeature.name: Incomplete Shadows
entries: At 14th level, you have learned how to utilize the special property of your shikigami, their form of shadow. You have derived a special summoning technique to keep their form incomplete. When you summon a ten shadows shikigami, you can summon them in an unstable form, causing their maximum hit points to be halved and any damage it deals to be halved(rounded down) while in this form. In addition, while in this form their movement speed is reduced to 10ft, and their size can be increased by up to two sizes. While in their incomplete form, the action cost to command them is increased by 1. This form lasts a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier, or until you choose to end it at any time (no action required). While in this unstable form any hit points lost are only lost until the unstable form ends, and if the shikigami dies in this unstable form, you retain the ability to summon it again. When incomplete shadows ends you may choose to immediately summon the shikigami in its normal form at the same location for no cursed energy cost. This cannot be used on Divergent Sila Divine General Mahoraga.

optionalfeature.name: Shikigami Gigantification
entries: Finally at 20th level, you have developed a technique that allows you to enhance the size of your shikigami. As part of summoning a shikigami created by your cursed technique and using Shikigami Output, for every 5 cursed energy spent, you may increase the shikigami's size by one, to a maximum of Gargantuan. If they are already Gargantuan, you can only increase their size once, making their width and height doubles. When a shikigami is increased this way, they gain the following:

items: Their hit die increase by two per size increase.
Their attack roll damage dice increase by one per size increase.
Their saving throw damage dice if any increase by two per size increase.
Their Constitution score increases by two per size increase (to a maximum of 30).

entries: You cannot use this extension technique on Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sila Divine General Mahoraga or Totalities that have more than two shikigami. This lasts as long as the Stronger Shadows counters from Shikigami Output last, or until you end it at will (no action required).

optionalfeature.name: Light Manipulation
entries: With great mastery over Reversed Cursed Technique, you have found the reversal of your technique. You have learnt how to channel light rather than shadow, illuminating your or another creature's shadow. As a bonus action for 4 cursed energy, you may target a creature's shadow who you can see, causing it to erupt in blinding flash of light. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a success, nothing happens. On a failure, the creature is blinded until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Summon Dispelling
entries: You can also manipulate the light in such a way as to interfere with the summons of others. As a Bonus Action, you can invert the shadow of a summon within 120 feet which you are connected to, for a number of cursed energy equal to half of the summons's CR or level(rounded down) You may force its summoner to make a Charisma saving throw. On a success, nothing happens. On a failure, the Summon becomes incapacitated and cannot move until the end of your next turn. If the summoner fails the saving throw by 5 or more, the summon is instead de-summoned, and cannot be summoned again for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier.

entries.name: Light Ridge
entries: As a reaction to a melee attack roll for 2 cursed energy, if the attacker is within 10 feet of you, you may force them to make a Constitution Saving throw, on a success, nothing happens. On a failure, the attack misses, and the attacker is blinded until the end of the turn. A creature is immune to this feature if they do not rely on sight, such as with blindsight.

Using Light Manipulation causes your shadow storage to become unstable. One random object, if any, currently inside your shadow storage will be ejected out, ending up within 5 feet of the shadow on which Light Manipulation was used.

At 10th level, you can now use Light Manipulation to target an entire area of shadow, inverting it into a blinding flash of Light. As a bonus action for 6 cursed energy, you may cause a creature's Shadow that you are connected to and can see, or your own shadow, to flash in large radius. All creatures within a 20 feet radius must make a constitution saving throw. On a success, nothing happens, on a failure becoming blinded for one minute. A creature may repeat the saving throw at the beginning of their turns, ending the effects on a success.
Using Light manipulation in this way causes all items within your Shadow Storage to be forced out, ending up within 5 feet of the shadow on which Light Manipulation was used.
You cannot be blinded by Light Manipulation.

optionalfeature.name: Hollow Purple
entries: You have also learned how to mix blue and red, creating a purple bolt of cursed energy that destroys everything in its path. As a rooting distracting action (which cannot be reduced),(S-2) you begin concentrating as if concentrating on a spell. If you successfully maintain concentration until the beginning of your next turn, as an action (S-1) for 20 Cursed Energy and 100 Hit Points from your Reverse Cursed Technique pool, you may end your concentration, and force every creature in a 15-foot wide 200-foot long line to make a Dexterity saving throw at disadvantage. On a failure, they take {@damage 30d12} force damage. On a successful save, they take half damage and do not take the additional effects. Objects within the area that aren’t being worn or carried also take this damage. At 17th level, this deals {@damage 50d12} force damage. At 20th level, this deals {@damage 60d12} force damage.
This counts as an extension technique for the purposes of other features.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: Kenta has 41 cursed energy that he can use to fuel his abilities. All of his cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: Kenta regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of his turns.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: Kenta reduces all non-magic damage he takes but psychic by 3. This also affects magical damage if he is using cursed armor.

trait.name: Maximum Output
entries: Kenta may use Maximum Output on any of the following techniques: Infinity, Blue, Blue Manipulation, and Hollow Purple.

trait.name: Teleport Technique
entries: When Kenta takes the movement action, he can spend 1 cursed energy to teleport to the location, causing him to not provoke opportunity attacks. The first attack roll he makes after taking the teleport action is made with advantage unless the target has a higher passive perception than 18. He can only use one teleport per round. He can also use blue as a way to propel himself into the air, giving him flying speed equal to his walking speed for 1 minute. After this minute ends, he can spend another 1 Cursed Energy to keep this active for an additional minute, and so on.

trait.name: Cursed Enhanced Body
entries: As long as Kenta has 1 cursed energy or more, he may add 3 to his attack and damage rolls with unarmed strikes or weapons he is proficient with.

trait.name: Reverse Cursed Technique
entries: Kenta has a healing pool of 450 that he can use with his reverse cursed energy. If he is suffering from a physical effect injury such as a concussion or something similar, one of these effects can be healed for every 10 hit points healed with reverse cursed technique. Kenta can use his RCT until his healing pool ends before getting burnout, during which he cannot use RCT until his healing pool recovers. At the beginning of his turns while in a combat where his life is at risk, he regains 5 hit points of his healing pool back. His healing pool automatically goes back to full at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Falling Blossom Emotion (Damage Threshold 70)
entries: When Kenta is affected by a sure-hit effect of a Domain Expansion, he may use his Falling Blossom Emotion as a reaction. He reduces the damage of the sure-hit effect by 70.

action.name: Extra Attack
entries: Kenta can attack twice, instead of once, when he takes the Attack action during his turn.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d12 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Cursed Strike (1-6 Cursed Energy)
entries: When Kenta hits with an unarmed strike, he may spend up to 6 cursed energy to deal an additional {@damage 1d6} force damage per cursed energy spent.

action.name: Blue Infused Strikes (1-6 Cursed Energy)
entries: When Kenta hits with an unarmed strike, he may spend up to 6 cursed energy to deal an additional {@damage 1d8} force damage per cursed energy spent.

action.name: Blue (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Kenta causes gravity to bend within 30 ft. of him, causing a medium-sized blue sphere with a 10 ft. circle attraction radius to draw everything towards it for 1 minute. All creatures within this area must make a {@dc 18} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they take {@damage 12d8} force damage, are {@condition restrained} until the beginning of their next turn, and are pulled to an open space closest to the center of the sphere. On a success, they take half as much damage and are not {@condition restrained} or pulled. A creature can retry this saving throw at the beginning of each of their turns. If they succeed, they take half the damage and exit the sphere. If they fail, they remain there without being able to move and retake the damage. Any creatures that enter the sphere's range must also make the Strength saving throw. Kenta can dispel this technique as a free action. He may only have a single Blue active at a time. When Kenta uses this technique in its Maximum Output form, he may spend a bonus action to move the sphere up to 60 feet from its original location. All creatures currently in the sphere are moved with it, and any creatures caught in the sphere's new range must make the saving throw.

action.name: Pulse Blue (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Kenta can choose to fire off your blue in a quick burst, weaponizing its attraction effect for damage rather than utility. As an action for 3 Cursed Energy, you can cast blue, instead firing it as a ranged cursed energy attack roll against a creature within 60 ft of you. This use of blue does half of blue's damage, and does not pull creatures or linger after it has been cast.

action.name: Blue Manipulation
entries: Kenta have learned how to use blue to manipulate space more precisely. As an action for an amount of cursed energy depending on the size, Kenta can choose an object or creature within 50 ft., moving it a number of feet equal to his proficiency bonus times 10. If it collides with an object, they both take {@damage 1d12} bludgeoning damage. If it collides with a creature or structure, they both take Collision Damage based on the object's size, making a Dexterity saving throw, taking half as much damage on a success. Any adjustments used to enhance Blue besides effects that increase your damage dice also apply to this (including range increases and Cursed Energy cost reductions). The size categories and their corresponding costs and damage are as follows:

rows: Tiny
1 Cursed Energy
{@damage 1d4} + Kenta's Charisma modifier bludgeoning damage
Small
3 Cursed Energy
{@damage 2d6} + Kenta's Charisma modifier bludgeoning damage
Medium
5 Cursed Energy
{@damage 2d8} + Kenta's Charisma modifier added twice bludgeoning damage
Large
8 Cursed Energy
{@damage 2d10} + Kenta's Charisma modifier added twice bludgeoning damage
Huge
10 Cursed Energy
{@damage 2d12} + Kenta's Charisma modifier added three times bludgeoning damage
Gargantuan
12 Cursed Energy
{@damage 4d12} + Kenta's Charisma modifier added three times bludgeoning damage

action.name: Red (10 Cursed Energy)
entries: {@atk r} {@hit 11} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}{@damage 12d10} force damage, and the target is knocked back up to 60 feet and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a hit, the targeted creature also becomes the center of Red's explosion.

action.name: Hollow Purple (20 Cursed Energy)
entries: Kenta mixes blue and red, creating a purple bolt of cursed energy that destroys everything in its path. As a rooting distracting action, Kenta begins {@status concentration||concentrating}. If he successfully maintains {@status concentration} until the beginning of his next turn, he may as an action spend 20 Cursed Energy and 100 hit points from his Reverse Cursed Technique pool, forcing every creature in a 15-foot wide, 200-foot long line to make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw at disadvantage. On a failure, they take {@damage 50d12} force damage. On a successful save, they take half damage. Objects within the area that are not being worn or carried also take this damage.

action.name: Exorcise
entries: Kenta automatically destroys a cursed spirit within 30 ft. of him that he can see that has a CR of 2 or lower.

bonus.name: Martial Arts
entries: After using the Attack action, Kenta can make one unarmed strike.

bonus.name: Blue Augmented Speed (1-5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Kenta spends up to 5 cursed energy to increase his movement speed by 10 feet per cursed energy spent. This lasts for a number of rounds equal to his Charisma modifier.

bonus.name: Cursed Armor (1-5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Kenta spends up to 5 cursed energy to give himself temporary hit points equal to the cursed energy spent times 10 for 1 minute. He can only benefit from this feature again after 1 minute or having his temporary hit points reduced to 0.

bonus.name: Cursed Blast of Blows (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: After Kenta uses the Attack action, he can spend 3 Cursed Energy to perform two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Kenta can spend 3 Cursed Energy to take the Dodge action as a bonus action on his turn.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Kenta can spend 3 Cursed Energy to take the Disengage or Dash action as a bonus action on his turn, and his jump distance is doubled until the end of his turn.

bonus.name: Infinity Pressure (1-9 Cursed Energy)
entries: While Infinity is active or while Kenta activates it, he may spend up to 9 cursed energy to increase its density. Every creature with Domain Amplification active that starts its turn within 5 ft. of him must make a {@dc 18} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are pushed back 5 ft. and are knocked {@condition prone}. If knocked against a solid surface, their saving throw becomes against taking {@damage 1d12} force damage per cursed energy spent and being {@condition restrained}. Kenta must spend cursed energy equal to the initial amount spent at the beginning of his turns to keep this active.

bonus.name: Reverse Cursed Technique
entries: Kenta spends any amount of cursed energy, regaining 10 hit points for each energy he spends, and removing the same amount of healing from his healing pool.

reaction.name: Infinity (3 Cursed Energy to activate; 1 Cursed Energy per intercept)
entries: When being targeted by an attack or effect that would cause damage that he can see, Kenta activates Infinity, encasing himself in a field for 1 minute. Whenever he is targeted by a weapon, grapple, or cursed energy attack roll, he spends 1 Cursed Energy and rolls a {@dice d20}. On a 4 or higher, the attack automatically fails. Kenta may spend 6 additional Cursed Energy to automatically roll a 20. When Kenta takes the Dodge action while Infinity is activated, he may use Infinity to defend creatures within his reach alongside himself. This feature only works on things that travel and can be stopped. Abilities that do not physically travel will not be stopped by Infinity.

reaction.name: Teleport Technique (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: When Kenta is targeted by an attack or saving throw, he may teleport to an empty space up to his movement speed in feet, potentially causing the effect to miss.

reaction.name: Cursed Enhanced Body (1-6 Cursed Energy)
entries: Whenever Kenta makes a Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, or Wisdom saving throw, he may add the number of cursed energy spent as a bonus to the saving throw.

reaction.name: Reverse Cursed Technique
entries: When Kenta receives damage, he may use his Reverse Cursed Technique.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: Todo has 56 cursed energy that he can use to fuel his abilities. All of his cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: Todo regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of his turns while in combat. This combat must be one where his life is at risk.

trait.name: Unarmored Movement
entries: Todo can move and run along vertical surfaces and across liquids on his turn without falling during the move.

trait.name: Durable Cursed Armor (1-5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Todo can give himself temporary hit points equal to the cursed energy spent times 10 for 1 minute. Todo can only benefit from this feature again after 1 minute or having his temporary hit points reduced to 0. Additionally, he gains 10 damage reduction while his cursed armor is active.

trait.name: Stronger Cursed Strike (1-6 Cursed Energy)
entries: Todo can, when making an Unarmed Strike, add {@damage 1d8} force damage to his attack per Cursed Energy spent. He can also use this in response to rolling a natural 20 on an attack roll with an unarmed strike instead of beforehand. On a miss, the energy used remains until he hits a target or until 1 minute passes. Creatures of Todo's choice within 5 feet of the impact take a number of {@dice d12} equal to half of the cursed energy spent (rounded down). Objects take twice the damage from this effect. Every cursed energy Todo spends on this feature counts as if he spends 2, though he still cannot count as spending more than his limit.

trait.name: Curse-Empowered Strikes
entries: Todo deals an additional 13 ({@damage 2d12}) force damage whenever he attacks with his Unarmed Strikes and weapons he's proficient with (already included). Additionally, his attacks are considered magical.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Tracker
entries: Todo may make a {@skill Perception} check, adding his Intelligence modifier, to see if there are any remains of cursed energy in the place he's at, and if he succeeds he know exactly who the energy is from. In case Todo does not know who the person is, then he can only track down where the cursed energy trail leads him to. The DC is equal to the cursed energy DC of the creature he's trying to track. Tracking residual cursed energy may not always lead all the way to the person, with the DM determining how the residual trail goes. Usage of a cursed technique or cursed energy fueled feature will often leave much more residuals than normal. Todo knows when any creature he can see spends cursed energy and how much they spend when doing so. Additionally, as a free action Todo can focus on a creature for 1 minute, seeing the "sparks" of cursed energy that always happen before cursed technique usage. For that minute when that creature forces him to make a save using their cursed technique or makes a cursed energy attack roll against Todo using their cursed technique they must roll {@skill Stealth}, using Charisma instead of Dexterity, against Todo's Perception. If he wins he gets advantage on the saving throw, or the attack roll is made with disadvantage. Todo can focus on 3 creatures with this feature at once.

trait.name: Negative Flow
entries: Whenever Todo is under the effects of the conditions {@condition frightened}, berserk/bloodlusted, are under half his maximum hit points (370), missing a limb lost in this encounter, or in an encounter that's total CR is higher than his Level; Todo will gain 2 temporary cursed energy at the start of his turn which cannot go above 1/4th of his cursed energy maximum (14). The DM can determine that an encounter is not suitable for Negative Flow to be able to be used.

trait.name: Object Infusion
entries: Todo has become more adept at imbuing cursed energy not just into special tools, but mundane objects for crafty use.

The amount of cursed energy spent on infusing the objects is based on the size of the object itself. Todo is able to infuse a nonmagical tiny object that fits into his hand for almost no cursed energy as a free action. After Todo infuses 10 of these tiny objects per long rest, it will cost 1 cursed energy. For example, infusing 9 pebbles costs nothing, infusing 10 will then cost 1 cursed energy, infusing 15 will cost none after the initial 1 spent at 10, and infusing 20 will cost another 1 for a total of 2 spent. For nonmagical objects above tiny, the action cost and energy cost are as follows: Size	Action Cost	Cursed Energy Cost Small	Bonus Action	2 Cursed Energy Medium	Action	4 Cursed Energy An object stays imbued for 1 minute. For the duration, it counts as an improvised weapon and has double the hit points it had before it was infused. If Todo wants to throw a tiny object they have imbued, the normal range is 10 feet times his Strength modifier or Dexterity modifier, and the long range is double the normal range. Additionally, Todo can expend a lot of cursed energy to cause the object to become volatile. When imbuing an object, Todo can instead spend double the normal amount of cursed energy to make the object reactive. If used on a tiny object, it costs 1 cursed energy per object. An object imbued in this way will explode when disturbed, like if it is moved or attacked (determined by the dm). Tiny, small, and medium objects heavily obscure an area equal to their size when they explode for 1 round. Only nonmagical objects can be infused in this way.

trait.name: Accursed Adrenaline (1/Long rest)
entries: At the beginning of his turn, if Todo is at or below half his hit points (370) and in a combat against an enemy whose CR is equal to or above his level, he can decide to enter a state ramping up his body with adrenaline and cursed energy. For one minute, he gains an extra action that may only be used to take the attack(one melee weapon attack only), dash, disengage, dodge, or use an object action, and either his Strength or Dexterity score increases by 2, as does its maximum. Lastly, once per turn, Todo may reduce the cost of Cursed Strikes by 2, to a minimum of 1. At the end of this period, Todo needs to make a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw, gaining 1 level of {@condition exhaustion} on a failure.

trait.name: Clapping Like Your Life Depends on It
entries: Todo may use Boogie Woogie once per turn as a free action.

trait.name: Clapping With Your Soul
entries: When Todo switches places with an ally who was previously in range of an enemy right before the switch, his next attack roll has advantage, the same applies for the ally if Todo were in the range. Both him and his ally count for this if they both were previously in range. If Todo is in range of an ally and he switches with an enemy, Todo may also get advantage if he is now next to an enemy, the same applies for the ally if they were in Todo's position, and his ally gains advantage vs the enemy switched for the next attack.

trait.name: Total Sync
entries: At the end of a long rest, choose one ally to become in sync with. Whenever Todo switches places with this ally, it can use its reaction to take the attack action.

trait.name: Taido Martial Arts
entries: Todo can use the Cursed Blast of Blows, Cursed Patient Defense or Cursed Wind Step Cursed Martial Arts without expending Cursed Energy once per turn.

trait.name: Sweep Kick (2 Cursed Energy)
entries: Once per round when Todo makes an unarmed strike, he may attempt a sweep kick. On a hit the target must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity saving throw with disadvantage. On a failure, they are knocked {@condition prone}. If they are already {@condition prone}, they take 5 extra damage.

trait.name: Cursed Blast
entries: Once per round Todo can raise the limit of one Cursed Strike to 12.

trait.name: Cursed Rain of Blows (1-3 Cursed Energy)
entries: As a free action after he has taken Cursed Blast of Blows on this turn, Todo may make a number of unarmed strikes equal to the amount spent. Todo may use this feature without expending Cursed Energy once per long rest.

trait.name: Strong Throw
entries: In place of an attack, Todo can choose a creature or object of a size up to 1 size category larger than him within his unarmed strike reach, and make an {@skill Athletics} check contested by their {@skill Athletics} or {@skill Acrobatics} (their choice) or a set DC for objects, 5 for tiny, 7 for small, 10 for medium, 15 for large, 20 for huge, and 25 for gargantuan. On a success, Todo can throw the creature or object up to 60 feet, dealing Xd6 bludgeoning damage to them and anyone in the path they're thrown in who fails a Dexterity saving throw, where X is 10 times the creature or objects size category number above small (minimum of 1), so 1 for medium, 2 for large, 3 for huge, and 4 for gargantuan. The d6s become d8s if the target is large, d10s if they're huge, and d12s if they're gargantuan.

trait.name: Cursed Stomp
entries: At the beginning of Todo's turn, he may reduce his movement speed to 20ft until the end of the turn, and delegate all of his unarmed strikes for the duration to be downward stomping kicks. For the duration, whenever Todo makes an unarmed strike, it will now be a stomping kick attack, if he is attacking a creature that is {@condition prone}, his critical hit radius will be reduced by 1. However, whenever Todo misses an unarmed strike attack roll, he will instead stomp the ground (if applicable), causing his unarmed strike to hit the ground and expending any features on his unarmed strike that would be normally expended (Such as Cursed Strikes), and all creatures within a 5ft radius except for him must make a {@dc 19} Constitution saving throw, on a success they are unaffected, on a failure they will take half of Todo's unarmed strikes damage.

trait.name: Colossal Physique
entries: Todo gains the following benefits: Todo will count as two sizes larger for the purposes of grappling, shoving, disarming and the weight he can carry, push, drag, or lift.
Whenever Todo makes a Strength check, instead of rolling the {@dice d20}, he can choose to automatically roll a 10 and use it for the check.
Any attack roll Todo makes which uses Strength deals twice as much damage to objects and structures.

Once per round as part of a successful melee weapon attack, Todo can force the creature hit to make a {@dc 19} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are knocked directly back 50 feet. If the creature would collide with an unsecured object or structure then the creature and the unsecured object or structure receive {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage per every 10 feet the creature traveled before colliding with the surface. If it was another creature, they instead both take damage equal to half of Todo's melee attack's damage roll instead (rounded down). If Todo's melee attack was a critical hit and he uses this feature, the creature will have disadvantage on their saving throw and are knocked back twice the distance they would be if this feature was used on a regular attack.
Whenever Todo makes a Strength check or saving throw, he can give yourself advantage on the roll. He can do this 5 times, regaining all uses after taking a long rest.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: Todo has 2 reactions per round.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: Todo will reduce all damage he takes but psychic by 5. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this, unless he's under the effects of cursed armor.

trait.name: Human Wall
entries: Todo gains the following benefits: Whenever Todo makes a Constitution check or saving throw, he can give himself advantage on the roll. He can do this 5 times, regaining all uses after taking a long rest.

Whenever Todo is under the effects of levels of {@condition exhaustion}, at the beginning of his turn, he can enter a state in which he's unaffected by the detriments of the {@condition exhaustion} condition. This state lasts 5 rounds. Once Todo uses this feature, he must complete a long rest before using it again.
Slam Master.

trait.name: Brute Slam
entries: Once per turn as part of an unarmed strike, Todo may make a grapple attempt against a creature. If he succeeds, he may pick up the creature and slam them against a solid surface within 10 ft. of him, such as the ground or a wall, dealing another roll of his martial arts die in damage ({@dice 1d12}). The surface takes twice as much damage as the creature, and if it breaks the creature takes another {@damage 2d6} damage of an appropriate type.

trait.name: Heavy Slam
entries: Whenever Todo takes the Attack action against a creature he is currently grappling with, he may forgo an attack to violently slam the creature into the ground. Todo makes a contested Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check against the creature who contests via Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check (Their choice). On a successful check, Todo's opponent is driven into the earth beneath him ending his grapple early, taking bludgeoning damage equal to two rolls of his martial arts dice ({@dice 2d12}) and being knocked {@condition prone}. On a failure, the creature escapes from Todo's iron grasp, causing his grapple to end early and the creature may move an additional 5 ft. away from him as part of their successful check. If Todo scores a 20 on the Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check whilst using this feature and it was a success, the creature takes bludgeoning damage equal to four rolls of his martial arts damage dice ({@dice 4d12}) and gains disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks, skill checks and Strength or Dexterity saving throws until the start of Todo's next turn.
Cursed Martial Artist.

Todo's unarmed strikes, when benefitting from the Cursed Strike enhancement, have their critical hit range increased by 1 (Ex. If he would score a critical hit on a roll of 20 with his unarmed strikes, he can now score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 so long as he uses Cursed Strike). Additionally, whenever Todo scores a critical hit with an unarmed strike, he can roll one additional unarmed strike damage die when determining the extra damage for the critical hit.

If Todo has taken the Attack action on his turn and only used unarmed strikes, when he makes any unarmed strike which benefits from Cursed Strike as a bonus action, the enhancement's cursed energy cost is reduced by 2 (to a minimum of 1).

trait.name: Cursed Enhanced Body
entries: As long as Todo has 1 cursed energy or more, he may add 2 to his attack and damage rolls with unarmed strikes or weapons he is proficient with (already applied).

trait.name: Cursed Energy Reinforcement
entries: Todo gains the following benefits so long as he has at least 1 cursed energy: When Todo makes a Strength, Dexterity or Constitution ability check, he may add 5 to it.

When calculating his jump distance and height, Todo may add his Intelligence score when calculating distance and modifier when calculating height. Additionally Todo's jump distance and height are doubled.
Todo may add his Intelligence score when determining his carrying capacity. Todo's carrying capacity (including maximum load and maximum lift) is also doubled.

Whenever Todo is calculating his AC through Unarmored Defense, he may add half of his Intelligence modifier to the final result (already applied). This second instance of adding his Intelligence score counts as if he were using two separate ability scores for the purposes of other features and rules.
The Curse-Empowered Strikes class feature now inflicts an additional die of force damage (already applied).

trait.name: Improved Cursed Energy Output
entries: As apart of any feature which uses cursed energy, Todo can expend extra cursed energy on it equal to half of the feature's original cost to amplify its output. This cannot be used on Cursed Energy Enhancements.

Amplifying a feature this way grants one of the following benefits: The feature's number of damage dice increases by 2. If the feature does not deal damage dice, it's damage bonus increases by a quarter of the original amount.
The feature's range increases by 30 feet. This range increase does not work with touch-based techniques.
The feature's bonus to hit is increased by +2 The feature's DC increases by +2 The feature's duration increases by 6 rounds.

trait.name: Cursed Enhancement
entries: Todo has also increased general cursed energy output and potency of his techniques. Whenever he uses a damage dealing feature that utilizes cursed energy its damage dice will be increased by one tier, ({@dice d6}->{@dice d8}->{@dice d10}). This does not apply to Cursed Energy Enhancements.

trait.name: Flawless Fundamentals
entries: Todo reduces the cost of all Cursed Energy Enhancements, Cursed Enhanced Body, all Taijutsu Feats and Cursed Martial Arts by 12 (to a minimum of 1).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Todo can make 3 attacks.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@dice 4d6 + 7}) bludgeoning and 13 ({@damage 2d12}) force damage.

action.name: Exorcise
entries: Todo automatically destroys a cursed spirit within 30 ft. of him that he can see that has a CR of 3 or lower.

action.name: We Are The Exception! (2/Short rest)
entries: Todo can perform a speech, inspiring his companions and restoring their resolve to fight. Todo chooses up to 6 allied creatures (which can include himself), making them gain 25 temporary hit points and removing any negative mental conditions (example: {@condition frightened}). All uses are restored on a short or long rest.

action.name: Basic Barrier (1-5 Cursed Energy, S-1)
entries: Todo creates a dome starting from himself with a 5ft. radius and a 10ft. height. For each cursed energy spent, the barrier's maximum radius and height both increase by 10ft, but Todo may choose to make it smaller if he wishes. This barrier has 10 hit points times the amount of cursed energy spent, it has resistance to all damage from the inside, is vulnerable to all damage from the outside and is immune to Poison and Psychic damage. This barrier has 19 AC and automatically fails saving throws. This barrier lasts 5 minutes. Todo can deactivate your basic barrier at will (no action required).

action.name: Curtain (5 Cursed Energy, S-1, V-1)
entries: Emerge from the darkness, blacker than darkness. Purify that which is impure.

Todo covers the sky and the ground within a 2165-foot radius with a sphere of darkness. Upon activation, a dark, almost liquid-like matter drips down from the sky to form the outer barrier wall in the shape of a sphere that extends underground, appearing as a dome from the surface. The curtain changes the sky becomes dark despite the time of day, provoking any cursed spirits out of hiding. Those without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class cannot see anything inside the barrier, merely seeing as if nothing but the vegetation was there. Creatures who can see into the ethereal plane or have levels in the jujutsu sorcerer path can see the black sphere.
The curtain lasts for 50 minutes. Todo's curtain has 50 hit points. This barrier has 19 AC and automatically fails saving throws. It is also immune to Poison and Psychic damage.
Todo may choose to make your curtain smaller in radius, however it cannot be smaller than a 100-foot radius.

action.name: New Shadow Style Simple Domain (5 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: Todo surrounds himself with a 5 ft. barrier for 1 minute, though said barrier does not impede movement. The circle has 180 hit points. While inside of a Domain Expansion, the sure-hit feature and sure-hit effect is negated against creatures inside the simple domain but at the start of Todo's turn the simple domain will take {@damage 3d12} force damage per 100 refinement the domain has (minimum of {@dice 3d12}). If the simple domain is reduced to 0 hit points, this technique ends. Any innate techniques or spells that target Todo, creatures inside his simple domain, or targets an area that overlaps with his simple domain, will have its damage dice reduced by 12. If it does not deal damage, it will instead have its Cursed Save DC reduced by 6. As an action while his simple domain is active, Todo can reduce his movement speed to 0 until the beginning of his next turn, and cause his simple domain to regain 50 hit points. When Todo casts his simple domain, he can choose for it to be of a larger radius around him than normal. He can choose for it to be a 10ft radius around you or a 15 ft. radius around him. For every 5 ft. Todo increases it beyond 5 ft., it will lose 50 hit points. Todo's refinement for his Simple Domain is 30.

bonus.name: Martial Arts
entries: When Todo uses the Attack action with an unarmed strike on his turn, he can make one unarmed strike or attack with a one-handed weapon.

bonus.name: Cursed Blast of Blows (1 Cursed Energy)
entries: Immediately after taking the Attack action on his turn, Todo can perform three unarmed strikes.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense (1 Cursed Energy)
entries: Todo can take the Dodge action. He also gains advantage in Strength and Constitution saving throws while benefitting from the dogde.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step (1 Cursed Energy)
entries: Todo can take the Disengage or Dash action on his turn, and his jump distance is doubled until the end of his turn. When Todo takes the Dash action granted by this feature, he instead gains extra movement equal to twice his speed. Additionally, when he takes the Disengage action granted by this feature, he gains extra movement equal to his speed for the current turn.

bonus.name: Explosive Blow (8, 10 or 14 Cursed Energy)
entries: Todo concentrates on channeling a very condensed portion of his cursed energy into either his limbs or a weapon for 8 cursed energy. For 1 minute after he performs this bonus action, the next melee attack he lands adds half of his attacks' damage dice (rounded down) as extra damage onto the damage roll for his attack. If Todo receives damage from any source, he must make a Constitution saving throw against a DC of 10 or half the damage taken, whichever is higher, to keep {@status concentration||concentrating}. Alternatively, as a bonus action when Todo lands a melee attack, he may expend 10 cursed energy to add half of his damage dice (rounded down) to the damage roll of his attack.
For 14 cursed energy as opposed to 8 or 10 cursed energy, the melee attack that Todo uses with this feature will instead add his attacks' damage dice to the damage roll as opposed to only half.

On a successful melee attack that benefits from Explosive Blow, the creature is knocked back a number of feet equal to either Todo's attacks' ability modifier or Intelligence modifier times 10 in a direction of his choice. If the creature collides with an unsecured object or structure then the creature and the unsecured object or structure receives {@damage 1d10} bludgeoning damage per every 5 feet the creature had left to travel after colliding with the surface (To a maximum of {@dice 10d10}). If it was another creature, they take damage equal to half of Todo's attack's damage roll instead.

bonus.name: Boogie Woogie (1 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: Todo can swap the places of 2 creatures or objects within 60 feet of him that has Cursed Energy by clapping.

For an object to count as a cursed object, it must have been altered by a cursed tool's feature or imbued with cursed energy. A person with cursed energy is anyone who does not have levels in the Heavenly Restriction class or is holding a Cursed Tool.

If this feature would target a New Shadow Style: Simple Domain the whole simple domain is swapped, along with all the creatures and objects inside. If this would put a creature or object in an occupied space or a space too small for it that creature or object is instead swapped to the nearest unoccupied space. A creature under the effects of Amplification will still be swapped.

If a swapped creature was the original target of an attack, the attacker may redirect their attack to another valid target within range. However, they only have knowledge of the placements of objects and creatures before the swap, not after. If a swapped creature or object now occupies the attack's new space, they are targeted instead.

Whenever Todo claps (once per round per creature with no action required), even if he does not use his technique, the affected creature must adjust their attack mid-action if their intended target was swapped. If they were targeting Todo directly and he clapped, they must succeed on a {@dc 19} Charisma saving throw to successfully re-aim at their new target; otherwise, they strike at their original target's prior location. Clapping without using his cursed technique does not force a saving throw on creatures actively tracking Todo's cursed energy via the Cursed Energy Tracker feature.

When using this technique, you must log what was swapped and where with the GM (or other players if applicable) before the attacker declares their adjusted attack. Both choices are then revealed simultaneously, and the action is resolved accordingly. This technique cannot cause a creature to hit itself with a melee attack, nor can it extend a melee attack beyond its reach.

reaction.name: Boogie Woogie (1 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: As a reaction to a significant stimulus that he can perceive, as determined by the DM, Todo uses his Boogie Woogie technique. Todo has 10 additional reaction that may only be used for this feature.

reaction.name: Glancing Blows (1-5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Once per turn when Todo takes bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage from an attack or effect he can see, he can reduce it by 4 times the amount spent, to a minimum of 1. If Todo reduces the damage to 1 in this way, he can make an unarmed strike at advantage against the attacker if they're within his reach as part of the same reaction.

reaction.name: Human Wall (5/Long rest)
entries: Upon hitting 0 hit points but not being outright killed, Todo can spend a hit die to fall to the amount rolled in hit points.

reaction.name: Cursed Martial Artist
entries: As a reaction when a creature fails a melee attack roll against him, Todo can make one unarmed strike against them. On a successful hit, Todo may add 5 to the damage roll.

reaction.name: New Shadow Style Simple Domain (5 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: As a reaction against a cursed energy attack roll or saving throw, spell, domain expansion opening, or being affected by the sure hit effect of a Domain Expansion Todo expands his Simple Domain. Todo cannot react to the effects of a Domain Expansion with this feature the first time he would get the opportunity after his simple domain ends or is broken.

reaction.name: Cursed Enhanced Body (1-5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Whenever Todo makes a Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Wisdom or Charisma saving throw, he may add the number of energy spent as a bonus to the saving throw. Todo has an additional reaction that may only be used for this feature.

trait.name: Quantity over Quality
entries: For every 10 hit points the swarm loses, a random cursed spirit dies (can be the DM's choice).

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: You are entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning you cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see you. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see you.

trait.name: Phasing
entries: Due to being composed of a smaller amount of cursed energy, the curse may walk through objects and structures as if they weren't there.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Medium Cursed Spirit. The swarm cannot regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The Swarm's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Crowd Control Vulnerability
entries: The swarm has disadvantage in saving throws against effects that cover an area of effect, such as a cone, circle or line. Additionally, any damage caused to the swarm by such effects is tripled if the entire area is covered by the swarm.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The swarm makes two Overwhelming Pressure attacks.

action.name: Spacing Issues
entries: As the swarm is too big, not all curses composing it can reach a single target, so the swarm causes less damage the smaller a creature is.
