A creature takes 1 dice less damage for every size they're smaller than Medium, minimum 1. A creature takes 1 dice extra of damage for every size they are larger than Medium.

action.name: Overwhelming Pressure
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, targets must be within 5 ft. of the swarm or in its space, hits as many targets as possible. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d10 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: Jogo has 128 cursed energy he can spend. All of the cursed energy, when used, is unavailable until he takes a long rest.

trait.name: Improved Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: Jogo regains 4 cursed energy at the beginning of his turn, up to his maximum.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: Jogo has advantage on saving throws against disease and being {@condition poisoned}.

trait.name: A Part Of Nature
entries: At the start of his turn, Jogo may choose to deal 15 fire damage to a creature he's grappling for no action required. Additionally, if a creature is grappling him, it takes the same damage he'd deal to them at the start of their turns.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: Jogo is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning they cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see Jogo . Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see Jogo.

trait.name: Hard to Kill (1/Long rest)
entries: If Jogo is reduced to 0 hit points, he can choose to drop to 1 hit point instead and are considered stable. This feature does not work if the damage that reduced Jogo to 0 hit points was Radiant.

trait.name: Your Fears Become Real
entries: Whenever Jogo rolls a skill check related to fire-based natural disasters, he does so with advantage.

trait.name: Sadism
entries: Jogo has immense sadism when beating his victims. Whenever he deal damage to someone who is {@condition frightened} of him, half of the total damage will be converted in temporary hit points to himself. These temporary hit points cannot go above 22.

trait.name: Cursed Strike (1-7 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo can, when making an Unarmed Strike in order to add {@damage 1d8} fire damage to his attack per Cursed Energy spent. He can also use this in response to rolling a natural 20 on an attack roll with an unarmed strike instead of beforehand. On a miss, the energy used remains until he hits a target or until 1 minute passes.

trait.name: Cursed Armor (1-8 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo can give himself temporary hit points equal to the cursed energy spent times 10 for 1 minute. Jogo can only benefit from this feature again after 1 minute or having his temporary hit points reduced to 0.

trait.name: Curse-Empowered Strikes
entries: Jogo's unarmed strikes are considered magical for the purposes of overcoming resistances, and deal {@dice 1d12} extra fire damage (already included).

trait.name: Speed Supremacy/50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: Once per turn, when Jogo takes an action, bonus action, or reaction, or uses his movement, he can blind his enemies in a quick burst of speed. The feature used will require twice as many reactions to react to. If a creature also possesses this feature, they can expend their use of it for the round to make reactions function as normal. If a feature or action allows him to make multiple attacks or force multiple saving throws, only one of them will receive the benefit of speed supremacy. Additionally, Jogo has 3 reactions per round.

trait.name: Purple Flames
entries: Each time Jogo uses a feature granted by his Innate Technique; he will gain 1 point of Purple Flames. Jogo can have up to 6 purple flames, which are lost at the end of a short or long rest. Whenever a creature is hit or fails a saving throw against taking damage by his Innate Technique being in their purple version, they are engulfed in flames, taking 28 additional fire damage.

trait.name: Untraceable Speed
entries: If an action or feature increases any of Jogo's speeds, it is further increased by 40 feet.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: Jojo will reduce all damage he takes but psychic or thunder by 4. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this unless she uses Cursed Armor.

trait.name: Cursed Enhanced Body
entries: As long as Jogo has 1 cursed energy or more, he may add 4 to his attack and damage rolls with unarmed strikes and weapons he is proficient with (already applied).

trait.name: Improved Cursed Energy Output
entries: As a part of any feature which uses cursed energy, Jogo can expend extra cursed energy on it equal to half of the feature's original cost to amplify its output. Amplifying a feature this way grants one of the following benefits: The feature's number of damage dice increases by 4. If the feature does not deal damage dice, it's damage bonus increases by a quarter of the original amount.
The feature's range increases by 50 feet. This range increase does not work with touch-based techniques.
The feature's bonus to hit is increased by +2 The feature's DC increases by +2 The feature's duration increases by 11 rounds.
If a feature benefits from Maximum Output that is already benefiting from this feature, it's total cursed energy cost is reduced by 10, to a minimum of 1.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Jogo makes 3 attacks

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 19} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@dice 1d12 + 12}) bludgeoning and 6 ({@damage 1d12}) fire damage.

action.name: Disaster Flames (1-27 Cursed Energy)
entries: When Jogo takes the Attack action, he can replace one of those attacks with a melee cursed energy attack roll or ranged cursed energy attack roll (both with {@hit 16} to hit) against a creature within 100 feet of him. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} fire damage per cursed energy spent and must make a {@dc 24} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they receive First Degree burns. On a success, they receive no additional conditions. If they fail by 5 or more, they receive Second Degree burns. All creatures of his choice within a 10-foot cone behind the target are also targeted.
Jogo can replace multiple attacks with Disaster Flames, but he must spend appropriate cursed energy for each attack. This cannot replace attacks from features such as Cursed Blast of Blows.

action.name: Purple Hands
entries: When Jogo uses this technique, he can spend 2 purple flame points to increase the damage die by one tier ({@dice 1d8} -> {@dice 1d10}). Additionally, if the target fails the {@dc 24} Constitution saving throw, they receive Second Degree burns. If they fail by 5 or more, they receive Third Degree burns. The cone is also increased to 30 feet.

action.name: Erupting Volcanoes (1-9 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo can create a number of volcanos equal to the amount spent on any surface within 120 ft. of him, with each volcano occupying a 5 ft. radius circle and existing until the beginning of his next turn.

action.name: Magma Blasts
entries: As part of the same action in which the volcanos are cast, Jogo may force every creature in a 30 ft. long, 10 ft. wide line originating from each volcano to make a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw as the volcanoes erupt. On a failure, they take 88 ({@damage 16d10}) fire damage, and become {@condition restrained} by the magma for 1 minute, and must make a {@dc 23} Constitution saving throw. On success, they take half as much damage and are not {@condition restrained}. Alternatively Jogo may choose to have volcanoes not fire immediately upon their casting, in which case he may have them fire as a reaction to a creature entering the area of effect that magma blast would occupy. The volcanoes disappear right after firing. On a failure, they receive Second Degree burns. On a success, they are not burned. If they fail by 5 or more, they receive Third Degree burns.
The creature may make a {@dc 24} Strength saving throw at the beginning of their turns, ending the condition early on success.
Any area targeted by this feature becomes difficult terrain for 1 minute, and any creatures that end their turn on it take 88 ({@damage 16d10}) fire damage.
Creatures on the area of effect of more than one volcano will make only one save, but the DC of the damage will increase by 1 for each volcano hitting the creature.

action.name: Purple Volcanos
entries: When Jogo uses this technique, he can spend 2 purple flame points to increase the range of the Magma Blast to 60 feet, and its width to 20 feet. Additionally, the creatures have disadvantage on the Dexterity saving throw caused by the magma blast.

action.name: Ember Insects (1-18 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo may create a number of Ember Insects equal to the amount of Cursed Energy spent within 10 feet of him for 1 minute.

action.name: Purple Insects
entries: When Jogo uses this technique, he can spend 3 Purple Flames points to cause the insects to gain a +2 in all of their ability scores, and increase their explosion damage die to {@dice 8d4}.

action.name: Hot Comet (10 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo chooses a point within 60 feet of him. All creatures in a 20-foot square centered at that point must make a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 93 ({@damage 17d10}) fire damage and 93 ({@damage 17d10}) bludgeoning damage and are knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, they take half as much damage and are not knocked {@condition prone}.
This technique deals twice as much damage to objects and structures. All objects in the area take maximum damage.
The area affected becomes difficult terrain.

action.name: Purple Comet
entries: When Jogo uses this technique, he can spend 4 purple flame points to increase its range to 120 feet and its square to 40 feet. The creatures have disadvantage on the Dexterity saving throw.

action.name: Lava Hands (10 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo creates up to two lava hands within 120 feet of him for 1 minute.
The Lava Hand is a gargantuan object that has AC 20 and 290 hit points. If it drops to 0 hit points, the technique ends. It has a Strength of 26 (+8) and a Dexterity of 10 (+0).

When Jogo utilizes this technique he can move the hands up to 60 feet and then cause one of the following effects with it: Clenched Fist Forceful Hand Grasping Hand Interposing Hand Jogo is {@status concentration||concentrating} for the duration of this technique. The technique ends if the hands are reduced to 0 hit points, the time ends or he loses {@status concentration}.

action.name: Maximum Meteor (20 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo may choose a spot within 100 ft to summon a meteor, which is a 60-foot sphere. Jogo can move this sphere up to 60 feet in any direction as a bonus action, and creatures that overlap with the sphere for the first time during that movement are dragged by the meteor to the end of its path.

Creatures other than Jogo who come into contact with the meteor must make a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw with advantage. On success, they take half the damage. On a failure, they or take 195 ({@dice 30d12}) fire and 195 ({@damage 30d12}) bludgeoning damage and must make a {@dc 24} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they receive Second Degree burns. On a success, they receive First Degree burns. If they fail by 5 or more, they receive Third Degree burns. Objects which overlap with the sphere take this same damage.
The sphere lasts for 1 minute, until it touches the ground, or until Jogo loses {@status concentration} as if {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell.
This technique deals twice the maximum damage possible to objects and structures.

action.name: Purple Meteor
entries: When Jogo uses this feature, he may spend 6 Purple Flame points to double this feature's radius. Creatures have disadvantage on the Dexterity saving throw.

action.name: Volcanic Head
entries: Jogo can use the volcanoes on his head to spew magma. He spews magma in 3 separate 30-foot-long-5-foot-wide lines, one pointing upwards, on pointing left and one pointing right. Every creature caught in one these lines must make a Dexterity saving throw as per the rules of Magma Blasts.

action.name: Basic Barrier (1-8 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo can create a dome starting from himself with a 5ft. radius and a 10ft. height. For each cursed energy spent, the barrier's maximum radius and height both increase by 10ft, but Jogo may choose to make it smaller if he wishes. This barrier has 10 hit points times the amount of cursed energy spent, it has resistance to all damage from the inside, is vulnerable to all damage from the outside and is immune to Poison and Psychic damage. This barrier has an AC of 24 and automatically fails saving throws. This barrier lasts for 10 minutes, and Jogo can deactivate his basic barrier as will (no action required).

action.name: Curtain (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Emerge from the darkness, blacker than darkness. Purify that which is impure.

Jogo covers the sky and the ground within a 2165-foot radius with a sphere of darkness. Upon activation, a dark, almost liquid-like matter drips down from the sky to form the outer barrier wall in the shape of a sphere that extends underground, appearing as a dome from the surface. The curtain changes the sky becomes dark despite the time of day, provoking any cursed spirits out of hiding. Those without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class cannot see anything inside the barrier, merely seeing as if nothing but the vegetation was there. Creatures who can see into the ethereal plane or have levels in the jujutsu sorcerer path can see the black sphere.
Jogo's curtain lasts for 90 minutes, and the curtain has 90 Hit Points. This barrier has an AC of 24 and automatically fails saving throws. It is also immune to Poison and Psychic damage.
Jogo may choose to make their curtain smaller in radius, however it cannot be smaller than a 100-foot radius.

action.name: Domain Expansion: Coffin of the Iron Mountain (50 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo opens his Domain Expansion, trapping all creatures within 30 feet inside it.

action.name: Domain Amplification (1-10 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo can cover his body to "wear" his domain in order to neutralize cursed techniques for 1 minute. Once activated, he automatically dampens any defensive technique, offensive technique, or spell that affects him, and he can bypass barriers such as infinity and defensive innate techniques. While active, Jogo gains a damage reduction to Innate Techniques or Spells equal to the amount spent times 10. In addition, conditions and effects from Innate Techniques and Spells are negated against him, unless they possess an original cost (before reductions) higher than the amount spent on Amplification, or are under Maximum Output. Jogo can dismiss Domain Amplification as a bonus action. Even if he dismisses it, for the minute he can reactivate it as a bonus action without spending the cursed energy again. Domain Amplification works on contact with Jogo's body, and as such ranged attacks and his features that do not make physical contact will not gain the benefits of Domain Amplification. Jogo cannot use Domain Amplification while his Domain Expansion is active.

When Jogo activates amplification, and while it is active, he cannot use his Innate Technique, and any active durations or effects will be stopped and interrupted. Jogo may instead choose to maintain active durations and effects from his innate technique, instead attempting to 'pause' it while amplification is active. At the beginning of Jogo's turns while amplification is active, he must make a {@dc 20} Charisma saving throw. On a success, his technique is 'paused' successfully, rather than being interrupted. On a failure, the technique is ended and interrupted as normal.

bonus.name: Martial Arts
entries: When Jogo uses the Attack action with an unarmed strike on his turn, he can make one unarmed strike or attack with a one-handed weapon.

bonus.name: Cursed Blast of Blows (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Immediately after taking the Attack action on his turn, Jogo can perform two unarmed strikes.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo can take the Dodge action.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Jogo can take the Disengage or Dash action on his turn, and his jump distance is doubled until the end of his turn.

bonus.name: Maximum Output
entries: Jogo can spend Cursed Energy equal to twice the original cost of one of his cursed techniques to amplify its cursed energy output, making it gain two of the following benefits the next time its cast: The technique's range is increased by half its original range rounded to the highest 5. For example, a technique with 10ft. of range would become a 15ft. range technique. This range increase does not work with touch based techniques.

The technique's damage dice is increased by half of its original dice rounded up. For example, a technique with {@dice 10d10} die of damage would add {@dice 5d10} die of damage to the total damage die.
The technique's bonus to hit is increased by half of its original bonus rounded up. For example, a technique with a {@hit 7} to hit would add a {@hit 4} to hit in its maximum output form.
The technique's DC is increased by 3.
The technique's duration is increased by half of its original duration. For example, a technique that lasts a minute would last 1 minute and 5 rounds.
Jogo cannot choose the same benefit twice.

bonus.name: Clenched Fist (Lava Hands active)
entries: The hand strikes one creature or object within 5 feet of it. Make a melee weapon attack for the hand using the hand's game statistics. On a hit, the target takes 27 ({@damage 8d8}) fire damage.

bonus.name: Forceful Hand (Lava Hands active)
entries: The hand attempts to push a creature within 5 feet of it in a direction Jogo chooses. Make a check with the hand's Strength contested by the Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check of the target. If the target is Medium or smaller, Jogo has advantage on the check. If he succeeds, the hand pushes the target up to 50 feet. The hand moves with the target to remain within 5 feet of it.

bonus.name: Grasping Hand (Lava Hands active)
entries: The hand attempts to grapple a Huge or smaller creature within 5 feet of it. Jogo uses the hand's Strength score to resolve the grapple. If the target is Medium or smaller, he has advantage on the check. While the hand is grappling the target, Jogo can use a bonus action to have the hand crush it. When he does so, the target takes 36 ({@damage 5d10 + 9}) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Interposing Hand (Lava Hands active)
entries: The hand interposes itself between Jogo and a creature he chooses until he gives the hand a different command. The hand moves to stay between Jogo and the target, providing him with half cover against the target. The target can't move through the hand's space if its Strength score is less than or equal to the hand's Strength score. If its Strength score is higher than the hand's Strength score, the target can move toward Jogo through the hand's space, but that space is difficult terrain for the target and they take 22 fire damage at the beginning of their turns.

bonus.name: Untraceable Speed
entries: Jogo may perform the Dash action or make a Skill Check he is proficient in.

bonus.name: Advanced Regeneration
entries: Jogo can spend any amount of Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 15 times the amount spent.

reaction.name: Untraceable Speed
entries: Jogo can use one of the following reactions: When passing a saving throw or when an attack roll is being made against him, Jogo may move up to half of his speed in a direction of his choice. This movement cannot be used to exit domains. If this movement would cause him to leave the area targeted by an attack roll he's reacting to he may roll a {@skill Acrobatics} check to attempt to fully dodge. He rolls against the attack roll, causing the attack to miss regardless of his armor class if his {@skill Acrobatics} check is higher than the attack roll. On a failure, the attack happens as normal, though he still moves as normal. This roll automatically fails if the attack was a critical hit, unless he's 10 or more levels above the attacker's level, or CR if they don't have a level. If he fails this roll by no more than 3 against an attack targeting one of his limbs with the Limb System then though he can't fully dodge he may change which limb is targeted. In the case of a saving throw he is unaffected by the saving throw if he moves out of the area of effect.
Jogo can make one melee attack if he moved into range of a creature or a creature enters his range. He can only do this once per creature per round.

reaction.name: Advanced Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, Jogo uses his Advanced Regeneration.

reaction.name: Cursed Enhanced Body (1-9 Cursed Energy)
entries: Whenever Jogo makes a Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence or Wisdom saving throw, he may add the number of energy spent as a bonus to the saving throw.

legendary.name: Retreat
entries: Jogo may move up to his movement speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Regenerate
entries: Jogo uses Advanced Regeneration.

legendary.name: Cursed Technique (2 Actions)
entries: Jogo may use one of his Cursed Techniques.

legendary.name: Domain Expansion:
entries: Coffin of the Iron Mountain (3 Actions). Jogo opens his Domain Expansion.

trait.name: Cursed Glasses
entries: Maki wears cursed glasses that allow her to see creatures with the {@condition invisible} force feature. Additionally, Maki gains a +3 on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}).

trait.name: Stamina Points
entries: Maki has 7 stamina points she can use to fuel her abilities. All of her stamina is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: Maki reduces all non-magical damage (except for thunder or psychic damage) by 3.

trait.name: Improved Unarmored Defense
entries: Maki gains a +1 bonus to her AC while not wearing any kind of armor or shield (already included).

trait.name: Taijutsu Sorcery
entries: Once per turn, Maki can make an unarmed strike with advantage. If she already had advantage on a unarmed strike, she raises the critical hit range for the attack by 1.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Cursed Spear
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d10 + 4}) piercing damage + 8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) necrotic damage.

bonus.name: Martial Arts
entries: When Maki takes the Attack action with an unarmed strike or a martial weapon on her turn, she can make one unarmed strike.

bonus.name: Barrage of Strikes (1 Stamina Point)
entries: Maki makes three unarmed strikes against a creature within range.

bonus.name: Quick Sweep (1 Stamina Point)
entries: Before making an unarmed strike, Make forces the targeted creature to make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, the creature is knocked {@condition prone}.

reaction.name: Taijutsu Sorcery
entries: Whenever Maki takes the Attack action on her turn, she may take the Dodge action as a reaction however this Dodge action only causes melee attacks against her to have disadvantage. If a creature rolls a natural 1 on a melee attack against her whilst she has performed this variation of the Dodge action, Maki can make one attack against that creature if it is within range once per round.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The corpse has 50 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. It's cursed energy can be restored by Yaga as an action.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The Corpse's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Level 2 Sensor System (Grade 1)
entries: This sensor system gives the corpse a +1 in any skill checks using it. It has audio, visual, and olfactory senses. Due to this component being made from grade 1 materials the corpse gains a +4 instead.

trait.name: Dexterity Upgrade
entries: This upgrade increases the corpse's Dexterity ability score by +2.

trait.name: Cursed Strikes
entries: The corpse can spend up to 6 cursed energy when making an Unarmed Strike in order to add {@damage 1d8} necrotic damage to its attack per Cursed Energy spent. The corpse can also use this in response to rolling a natural 20 on an attack roll with an unarmed strike instead of beforehand. On a miss, the energy used remains until it hits a target or until 1 minute passes.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The corpse makes 2 attacks.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d4 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

monster.name: Divine Dog Totality
languages: understands the languages of it's master but can't speak.

trait.name: Keen Senses
entries: The Divine Dog has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing, sight, or smell.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The Divine Dog Totality is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Cursed Weapons
entries: The Divine Dog's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The Divine Dog has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the Divine Dog's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Divine Dog makes three attacks: One with its bite and two with its claws.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) piercing damage and the target must succeed on a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 6}) slashing damage.

reaction.name: Dog's Shield
entries: When a creature makes an attack against the dog's master, the dog grants a +2 bonus to the master's AC if the dog is within 5 feet of the master.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: Satoru has 109 cursed energy that he can use to fuel his abilities. All of his cursed energy is replenished at the end of a short or long rest.

trait.name: Enhanced Cursed Energy Regeneration
entries: Satoru regains 5 cursed energy at the beginning of his turns while in combat. This must be a combat where his life is at risk He will also gain 1 Cursed Energy per minute outside of combat and doing light activity.

trait.name: Unarmored Movement
entries: Satoru can move and run along vertical surfaces and across liquids on his turn without falling during the move.

trait.name: Cursed Armor (1-11 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru can give himself temporary hit points equal to the cursed energy spent times 20 for 1 minute. Satoru can only benefit from this feature again after 1 minute or having his temporary hit points reduced to 0. Satoru gains 20 damage reduction while his Cursed Armor is active. While Satoru's Cursed Armor is active, he regains 18 temporary hit points at the beginning of his turns. These temporary hit points cannot go above the maximum amount of armor he can have. Satoru's Cursed Armor will provide him with resistances to two damage types of his choice whilst he has temporary hit points from Cursed Armor. These damage resistances may be changed on a long rest or once per round as a bonus action or reaction to Satoru using Cursed Armor. Every cursed energy Satoru spends on this feature counts as if he spends 2, though he still cannot count as spending more than his limit.

trait.name: Cursed Strike (1-10 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru can, when making an Unarmed Strike, add {@damage 2d10} force damage to his attack per Cursed Energy spent, as well as dealing 16 additional force damage. Satoru can expend 3 additional cursed energy to increase the additional force damage his cursed strike deals to 22. Satoru can also use this in response to rolling a natural 20 on an attack roll with an unarmed strike instead of beforehand. On a miss, the energy used remains until he hits a target or until 1 minute passes.
Satoru can use this feature 9 times per round.

trait.name: Curse-Empowered Strikes
entries: Satoru deals an additional 13 ({@damage 2d12}) force damage whenever he attacks with his Unarmed Strikes and weapons he's proficient with (already included). Additionally, his attacks are considered magical.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Tracker
entries: Satoru may make a {@skill Perception} check, adding his Charisma modifier, to see if there are any remains of cursed energy in the place he's at, and if he succeeds he know exactly who the energy is from. In case Satoru does not know who the person is, then he can only track down where the cursed energy trail leads him to. The DC is equal to the cursed energy DC of the creature he's trying to track. Tracking residual cursed energy may not always lead all the way to the person, with the DM determining how the residual trail goes. Usage of a cursed technique or cursed energy fueled feature will often leave much more residuals than normal. Satoru knows when any creature he can see spends cursed energy and how much they spend when doing so.

Alternatively, Satoru can detect any remains of Cursed Energy in a 240 ft. radius, detecting it even if he can't see it directly. Additionally, as a free action Satoru can focus on a creature for 1 minute, seeing the "sparks" of cursed energy that always happen before cursed technique usage. For that minute when that creature forces him to make a save using their cursed technique or makes a cursed energy attack roll against Satoru using their cursed technique they must roll {@skill Stealth}, using Charisma instead of Dexterity, against Satoru's Perception. If he wins he gets advantage on the saving throw, or the attack roll is made with disadvantage. Satoru can focus on 4 creatures with this feature at once.

trait.name: Negative Flow
entries: Whenever Satoru is under the effects of the conditions {@condition frightened}, berserk/bloodlusted, are under half his maximum hit points (765), missing a limb lost in this encounter, or in an encounter that's total CR is higher than his Level; Satoru will gain 5 temporary cursed energy at the start of his turn which cannot go above 1/4th of his cursed energy maximum (27). The DM can determine that an encounter is not suitable for Negative Flow to be able to be used.

trait.name: Focused Rage
entries: During his turn, while using Negative Flow and being under any of the circumstances listed in the feature, Satoru can begin {@status concentration||concentrating} to increase the temporary cursed energy gained at the start of his turn to 10. When the circumstances listed end Satoru can no longer concentrate on this feature. (This feature can be concentrated on even while under the berserk/bloodlusted condition) Speed Supremacy/50 Meters In 3 Seconds! Once per turn, when Satoru takes an action, bonus action, or reaction, or uses his movement, he can blind his enemies in a quick burst of speed. The feature used will require twice as many reactions to react to. If a creature also possesses this feature, they can expend their use of it for the round to make reactions function as normal. If a feature or action allows him to make multiple attacks or force multiple saving throws, only one of them will receive the benefit of speed supremacy. Additionally, Satoru has 3 reactions per round.

trait.name: Teleport Technique (1 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: When Satoru takes the movement action, he can teleport to the location, causing him to not provoke attacks of opportunity. The first attack roll Satoru does after taking the teleport action is made with advantage unless the target has a higher passive perception than 27.
When using this feature there must be an unobstructed path to where Satoru wishes to teleport, even if he could normally not travel such a path. Satoru can only use this feature once per round.

trait.name: Flight (6 Cursed Energy)
entries: When Satoru moves he can use blue as a way to propel himself into the air, giving him a flying (hover) speed equal to his walking speed for 1 minute. After this minute ends, Satoru can spend another 1 Cursed Energy to keep this active for an additional minute, and so on. Satoru can also grant this benefit to the other creatures he's sharing Infinity with or that he's currently holding.

trait.name: Blue Infused Strikes (12 Cursed Energy)
entries: Whenever Satoru hits an unarmed strike he can choose to make it count as a critical hit for ts damage calculation. Additionally, once per round Satoru can spend 6 more cursed energy as a part of a Blue Infused Strike to force a target within 15 feet to make a {@dc 27} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are pulled into his reach. On a success, they are not moved.

trait.name: Delayed Explosions (S-1)
entries: Whenever Satoru uses Blue or Red, he may reduce their size and make them not explode, delaying them. All hostile creatures must make a {@skill Perception} check against his cursed energy DC. If any feature raises the delayed technique's DC or imposes disadvantage on the targeted creatures, it also affects this check. On a failure, they don't notice it and think it already exploded, vanished, or missed. Satoru may choose for the technique to activate as normal, dealing half as much damage and only afterwards enter its delayed state. If he chooses to do that with Blue, it does not keep its lingering effect. While in this state, once per turn Satoru may move the delayed technique during his turn, no action required, through the air, up to his movement speed. A technique can be delayed like this for up to 10 rounds. Satoru may spend a bonus action to make them explode, doubling their effect's range, and imposing disadvantage on the saving throw. If the creature succeeds in the {@skill Perception} check, it may make its saving throw as normal. While in their delayed state, Satoru's Blue and Red have an Armor Class equal to his Cursed Energy DC. It automatically fails all saving throws, and it is immune psychic and poison damage. If they take damage in this form, they will explode prematurely.

trait.name: Simultaneous Casting (S-1)
entries: When Satoru casts Blue he may pay its cost up to 5 additional times, creating an additional Blue within 5ft. of the initial blue for each additional time he pays the cost. The additional blues will deal the damage of the original and will not restrain the target. Alternatively, Satoru can shoot them like projectiles following the rules for cursed projectiles but they do his blues damage, and these attacks can replace any number of attacks on his turn.
Satoru may not use Delayed Explosions, Maximum Output, Chanting, or any damage- increasing features or effects, including those from Domain Expansions or Tokyo School, on a Blue cast in this way.

trait.name: Explosive Purple
entries: If Satoru makes a delayed red or blue and a blue or red enter the same space, he may use a free action to have them merge, causing them to become an Explosive Purple, which he can then detonate as a bonus action. When Satoru causes it to explode, a massive purple that erases everything will form. Every creature within a 200 ft circle centered on the Explosive Purple must make a {@dc 27} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take Hollow Purple's damage. On a success, they take half as much damage.
Any enhancements placed on Hollow Purple affect Explosive Purple as well.

trait.name: Taido Martial Arts
entries: Satoru can use the Cursed Blast of Blows, Cursed Patient Defense or Cursed Wind Step Cursed Martial Arts without expending Cursed Energy once per turn.

trait.name: Jab
entries: Once per round, when Satoru make an unarmed strike, he may perform a jab. This attack deals half damage but forces the target to react, giving him a strategic advantage.
If the jab hits, Satoru's next melee attack against the same target before the end of his next turn is made with advantage.
If the jab misses, Satoru's next melee attack is made normally, but the target does not gain an opportunity attack against him if they normally would.

trait.name: Liver Shot
entries: Once per round when Satoru hits a creature with an unarmed strike, he may force them to make a {@dc 27} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they have disadvantage on their next melee attack or Dexterity-based ability check.

trait.name: Cursed Blast
entries: Once per round Satoru can raise the limit of one Cursed Strike to 18.

trait.name: Cursed Rain of Blows (1-3 Cursed Energy)
entries: As a free action after he has taken Cursed Blast of Blows on this turn, Satoru may make a number of unarmed strikes equal to the amount spent. Satoru may use this feature without expending Cursed Energy once per long rest.

trait.name: Strong Throw (Cursed Energy Varies)
entries: In place of an attack, for 3 Cursed Energy, Satoru can choose a creature or object of a size up to 1 size category larger than him within his unarmed strike reach, and make an {@skill Athletics} check contested by their {@skill Athletics} or {@skill Acrobatics} (their choice) or a set DC for objects, 5 for tiny, 7 for small, 10 for medium, 15 for large, 20 for huge, and 25 for gargantuan. On a success, Satoru can throw the creature or object up to 60 feet, dealing Xd6 bludgeoning damage to them and anyone in the path they're thrown in who fails a Dexterity saving throw, where X is 10 times the creature or objects size category number above small (minimum of 1), so 1 for medium, 2 for large, 3 for huge, and 4 for gargantuan. The d6s become d8s if the target is large, d10s if they're huge, and d12s if they're gargantuan. The cost also increases, to 4 cursed energy if the target is large, 5 if they're huge, and 6 if they're gargantuan.

trait.name: Running Drop Kick (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Once per round when Satoru makes an unarmed strike with an attack on his attack action, he may force the creature to make a {@dc 27} Strength saving throw. On a success, they are unaffected. On a failure, they take a {@damage d6} bludgeoning damage for every 20 feet moved immediately before the unarmed strike, and are pushed directly back a number of feet equal to 5 feet for every 20 feet moved immediately before the unarmed strike. A creature automatically succeeds the saving throw if they did not take any damage from the unarmed strike. The damage from this feature cannot exceed 10 damage dice.

trait.name: Perfected Martial Combat
entries: Whenever Satoru uses a Cursed Strike, any cursed energy spent into them, will count as if he spent quadruple the amount. This still cannot add more dice than his limit.

trait.name: Stored Energy
entries: During a short or long rest, Satoru can spend one of his hit dice to gain temporary cursed energy equal to the result rolled + 13. These temporary cursed energy last until the end of his next short or long rest. They are not restored by the Enhanced Cursed Energy Regeneration feature.

trait.name: Otherworldly Efficiency
entries: Every time Satoru spends cursed energy in a feature it counts as if he has spent twice as much, however the maximum amount he can reach remains the same. Additionally, he reduces the amount he spends on any features that use cursed energy by 9 (to a minimum of 1).

trait.name: Master of Manipulation
entries: When Satoru uses any features that uses cursed energy he may choose for their action cost to be reduced by 1 (full turn action->action->bonus action->free action->he gains one additional free action just for using said feature). If this would reduce the action cost of a reaction he gains 1 additional reaction only for using said reaction. Satoru may only gain an additional Reaction once per round per feature. Additionally, he can spend up to 11 cursed energy whenever he's using a cursed energy feature that's not a cursed technique to increase its DC by the amount spent.

trait.name: Battle Rhythm
entries: Once per turn, when Satoru misses one of his Melee or Ranged Weapon Attacks, he may re-roll the attack, adding his Strength or Dexterity modifier a second time to this re-roll. If the initial attack had advantage or disadvantage, both dice are re-rolled when this feature is used.

trait.name: Amazing Student, Terrible Teacher
entries: Satoru has disadvantage in any rolls related to teach something to a creature, while on the other hand he has advantage in any rolls related to learning something. This only applies to things the DM would determine he has proper advantage at grasping, so complex techniques may not benefit from this feature.

trait.name: Information Overload
entries: Due to the amount of information his eyes capture, if Satoru's not wearing any light object to protect his eyes, he will receive 1 level of {@condition exhaustion} every minute, to a maximum of 3 levels. Additionally, for every {@condition exhaustion} level he gains like this, the damage die tier of his techniques decreases by 1, and the DC his technique suffers a penalty of -1. If Satoru wears eye protection again, this timer resets after a minute of covering his eyes rather than immediately. Outside of combat, he will only gain one level of {@condition exhaustion} every hour his eyes are uncovered, with the timer and {@condition exhaustion} resetting if he wears eye protection for 10 minutes. Finally, Satoru can remove these {@condition exhaustion} levels for every 50 hit points he heals with it, and he can do so even if he has not taken damage.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Economy
entries: Due to the six eyes, the amount of cursed energy Satoru spends on his techniques is ridiculously low when compared to an average sorcerer. Every feature that uses Cursed Energy will instead cost 0.1 times its original cost in Cursed Energy (Ex:If a feature costs 5 Cursed Energy, you'd spend 0.5 Cursed Energy.). This reduction does not mean that he can spend more cursed energy on a feature than normal. While wearing eye protection, this reduction is decreased to 0.5 energy spent (rounded down).

trait.name: Perceptive Eyes
entries: While not wearing eye protection, Satoru gains advantage on {@skill Perception} checks.

trait.name: Technique Reader
entries: As a free action towards an enemy within Satoru's Truesight, he can look into a creature and receive the following information: Their Jujutsu Sorcerer or spellcasting class level, spells they currently have, their cursed energy, whether they have Unique Cursed Energy and it's properties, remaining spell slots and domain refinement if any. In addition, if they have an innate technique, Satoru will learn their Innate Technique's target concept and the capabilities of its Lapse technique, as well as any technique improvements to the lapse they currently possess. If the Lapse technique involves summoning creatures he only gains general knowledge of the summons' abilities. While wearing eye protection, Satoru can't use this feature.

trait.name: Colossal Physique
entries: Satoru gains the following benefits: Satoru will count as two sizes larger for the purposes of grappling, shoving, disarming and the weight he can carry, push, drag, or lift.
Whenever Satoru makes a Strength check, instead of rolling the {@dice d20}, he can choose to automatically roll a 10 and use it for the check.
Any attack roll Satoru makes which uses Strength deals twice as much damage to objects and structures.

Once per round as part of a successful melee weapon attack, Satoru can force the creature hit to make a {@dc 25} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are knocked directly back 80 feet. If the creature would collide with an unsecured object or structure then the creature and the unsecured object or structure receive {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage per every 10 feet the creature traveled before colliding with the surface. If it was another creature, they instead both take damage equal to half of Satoru's melee attack's damage roll instead (rounded down). If Satoru's melee attack was a critical hit and he uses this feature, the creature will have disadvantage on their saving throw and are knocked back twice the distance they would be if this feature was used on a regular attack.
Whenever Satoru makes a Strength check or saving throw, he can give yourself advantage on the roll. He can do this 10 times, regaining all uses after taking a long rest.

trait.name: Untraceable Speed
entries: Satoru will gain the following benefits: If an action or feature increases any of Satoru's speeds, it is further increased by 50 feet (already applied).

Whenever Satoru makes a Dexterity check, instead of rolling the {@dice d20}, he can choose to automatically roll a 10 and use it for the check. Satoru cannot use this for the reaction dodge ability provided by Untraceable Speed.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: Satoru will reduce all damage he takes but psychic by 10. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this, unless he's under the effects of cursed armor.
Acrobatic Fighter.
Satoru may calculate his jump distance and height with his Dexterity score.
Satoru's jump distance and height is doubled.

Whenever Satoru makes an unarmed strike while within 5 ft. of a solid surface or another object like a pole, he may use it to leverage a powerful attack against his opponent, dealing an additional damage die of his unarmed strike. If there are not any solid surfaces, he may use half his movement speed to drop to the ground on his hands, extending his legs up and making a powerful kick.
Once per turn, whenever he fall onto a creature, make a {@dc 10} {@skill Acrobatics} check. On a success, Satoru may make one unarmed strike against that creature.
Cursed Martial Artist.

Satoru's unarmed strikes, when benefiting from the Cursed Strike enhancement, have their critical hit range increased by 1 (Ex. If he would score a critical hit on a roll of 20 with his unarmed strikes, he can now score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 so long as he uses Cursed Strike). Additionally, whenever Satoru scores a critical hit with an unarmed strike, he can roll one additional unarmed strike damage die when determining the extra damage for the critical hit.

If Satoru has taken the Attack action on his turn and only used unarmed strikes, when he makes any unarmed strike which benefits from Cursed Strike as a bonus action, the enhancement's cursed energy cost is reduced by 2 (to a minimum of 1).

trait.name: Taijutsu Sorcery
entries: Once per round, Satoru can make an unarmed strike or martial weapon attack with advantage (this does not give you an extra attack). If Satoru already had advantage on a unarmed strike or martial weapon attack, he raises the critical hit range for the attack by 1 using this feature.

trait.name: Refined Sorcerer
entries: All methods of refining Satoru's simple domain will provide 5 additional refinement points.

trait.name: Cursed Enhanced Body
entries: As long as Satoru has 1 cursed energy or more, he may add 5 to his attack and damage rolls with unarmed strikes or weapons he is proficient with (already applied).

trait.name: Cursed Energy Reinforcement
entries: Satoru gains the following benefits so long as he has at least 1 cursed energy: When Satoru makes a Strength, Dexterity or Constitution ability check, he may add 10 to it.

When calculating his jump distance and height, Satoru may add his Charisma score when calculating distance and modifier when calculating height. Additionally Satoru's jump distance and height are doubled.
Satoru may add his Charisma score when determining his carrying capacity. Satoru's carrying capacity (including maximum load and maximum lift) is also doubled.

Whenever Satoru is calculating his AC through Unarmored Defense, he may add half of his Charisma modifier to the final result (already applied). This second instance of adding his Charisma score counts as if he were using two separate ability scores for the purposes of other features and rules.
The Curse-Empowered Strikes class feature now inflicts an additional die of necrotic damage (already applied).

trait.name: Improved Cursed Energy Output
entries: As apart of any feature which uses cursed energy, Satoru can expend extra cursed energy on it equal to half of the feature's original cost to amplify its output. This cannot be used on Cursed Energy Enhancements.

Amplifying a feature this way grants one of the following benefits: The feature's number of damage dice increases by 5. If the feature does not deal damage dice, it's damage bonus increases by a quarter of the original amount.
The feature's range increases by 55 feet. This range increase does not work with touch-based techniques.
The feature's bonus to hit is increased by +2 The feature's DC increases by +2 The feature's duration increases by 11 rounds.

trait.name: Cursed Enhancement
entries: Satoru has also increased general cursed energy output and potency of his techniques. Whenever he uses a damage dealing feature that utilizes cursed energy its damage dice will be increased by one tier, (already applied). This does not apply to Cursed Energy Enhancements.

If a feature benefits from either Maximum Output or Chanting that is already benefiting from this feat, it's total cursed energy cost is reduced by 15, to a minimum of 1. This cursed energy reduction does not apply for the purposes of Flawless Fundamentals. This does not apply to Cursed Enhancements.

trait.name: Divine Cursed Energy Output
entries: As apart of any feature that uses cursed energy, Satoru can expend 8 cursed energy to drastically alter its functions as part of the same action he uses the feature with. If the feature was a Cursed Energy Attack, the target of Satoru's attack or attack rolls has their AC treated as if it had been reduced by 5. If the feature was a Cursed Energy save, he may change the saving throw to another depending on its original save. The changes are the following below: Original Saving Throw	Changed Saving Throw Strength	Dexterity Dexterity	Constitution Constitution	Strength Intelligence	Wisdom Wisdom	Intelligence Finally, whenever Satoru uses a cursed energy fueled feature and amplifies its output using Improved Cursed Energy Output then he can apply two benefits as opposed to one by expending cursed energy equal to the feature's cost (A minimum of 1).

trait.name: Flawless Fundamentals
entries: Satoru reduces the cost of all cursed energy features by 18. If one of Satoru's class features is reduced by double its original cost, the feature's cost instead becomes 0. This feature's cost can be treated as 0 up to 22 times per long rest.

trait.name: Reverse Cursed Technique Pool
entries: Satoru has a healing pool of 4500 that he can use utilizing his Reverse Cursed Technique. Satoru can heal this way until the pool ends, when he enters burnout. Satoru's reverse cursed technique pool replenishes at end of a long rest. Additionally, Satoru may try to force his reverse cursed technique to come out despite being in burnout, forcing himself to make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a success, he regains or heals half as much as he normally would. At the beginning of his turns while in a combat where Satoru's life is at risk, he regains 15 hit points of his healing pool back.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (5/Day)
entries: If Satoru fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Satoru makes 5 attacks.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 24} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}43 ({@dice 8d6 + 15}) bludgeoning 13 ({@damage 2d12}) force damage.

action.name: Exorcise
entries: Satoru can automatically destroy a cursed spirit within 30 feet of himself with a CR of 4 or lower that he can see.

action.name: Infinity (10 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru can cause the space around him to divide into infinite fractions for 1 minute. Whenever he is targeted by a weapon, grapple, spell, melee cursed energy attack roll, ranged cursed energy attack roll or any saving throws from sources outside his body, he may roll a {@dice d20}. On a 4 or higher, any attacks or spells made against him automatically fail to hit or harm him in any physical way.
When Satoru takes the Dodge action while Infinity is activated, he may use his Infinity to defend 2 creatures within his reach with Infinity alongside himself.

This feature only works on things that travel and can be stopped. Abilities that do not physically travel will not be stopped by Infinity. For example, Eye contact, mind control, basilisk stare, making things explode with your mind, etc.

action.name: Blue (12 Cursed Energy, S-1)
entries: Satoru may cause space to be erased within 30 ft of him causing a medium sized blue sphere with a 10 ft. circle attraction radius to draw everything towards it for 1 minute. All creatures within this area must make a {@dc 27} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they take 130 ({@damage 20d12}) force damage, are {@condition restrained} until the beginning of their next turn, and are pulled to an open space closest to the sphere. On a success, they take half as much damage and are not {@condition restrained} or pulled. A creature can retry this saving throw at the beginning of each of their turns. If they succeed, they take half the damage and exit the sphere. If they fail, they remain there without being able to move and retake the damage. Any creatures that enter the sphere's range or start their turns in the sphere's range must also make the Strength saving throw, but a creature can only be subject to the saving throw once per round. This does not affect the repeated save at the beginning of a creatures turn. Satoru can dispel this technique as a free action. Satoru may only have a single Blue active at a time.
Objects within the area that aren't being worn or carried also take this damage.

When Satoru uses his blue technique in its Maximum Output form he may spend a bonus action to move the sphere a 90 ft. All creatures currently stuck in the sphere will be moved as well, and any creatures caught in the range of the sphere moving must do the saving throw. A creature can still only be subjected to the saving throw once per round.

action.name: Blue Redirection
entries: Whenever a projectile, ranged attack roll, or spell/technique with a line area passes through Blue's area of effect, it is attracted to the center of the sphere. If said technique or attack would deal less damage than Blue, it is pulled in and stopped completely, missing its target in the case of an attack and being cut short in case of a line effect. If it is a projectile, it also takes Blue's damage. In the case of a line effect, it now changes its trajectory, with its area starting from the first point where it entered Blue's area of effect and ending at the center of the sphere. If any other creatures are in that area, they are affected by the line effect as normal. If the attack or line effect would deal more damage than Blue, it ignores this and works as normal, but its damage is reduced by Blue's damage.

action.name: Pulse Blue (7 Cursed Energy, S-1)
entries: Satoru can cast blue, instead firing it as a ranged cursed energy attack roll against a creature within 60ft of him. This use of blue does half of blue's damage, and does not pull creatures or linger after it has been cast.

action.name: Blue Manipulation (Cursed Energy Varies, S-1)
entries: Satoru can choose an object or creature within 50 ft., moving it up to 90 feet. If it collides with an object, they both take {@damage 1d12} bludgeoning damage. If it collides with a creature or structure, they both take Collision Damage based on the object's size, making a {@dc 27} Dexterity saving throw, taking half as much damage on a success. Any adjustments used to enhance Blue besides effects that increase Satoru's damage dice also apply to this (including range increases and Cursed Energy cost reductions).
Object Size	Cursed Energy Cost	Collision Damage Tiny	1 Cursed Energy	{@damage 1d4 + 10} bludgeoning damage.
Small	3 Cursed Energy	{@damage 2d6 + 10} bludgeoning damage.
Medium	5 Cursed Energy	{@damage 2d8 + 20} bludgeoning damage.
Large	8 Cursed Energy	{@damage 2d10 + 20} bludgeoning damage.
Huge	10 Cursed Energy	{@damage 2d12 + 30} bludgeoning damage.
Gargantuan	12 Cursed Energy	{@damage 4d12 + 30} bludgeoning damage.
Depending on the nature of the object or structure Satoru is moving, the DM may allow him to rip things planted or rooted to something, such as pulling a specific portion of a bridge.

action.name: Moving a Creature
entries: Satoru can pull a creature, using the chart above for their size. Make a contested check against the creature, with Satoru using his Cursed Energy Modifier, and the creature using Strength. If Satoru succeeds, the creature is pulled up to the normal range. On a failure, they are not moved. A creature can choose to fail this check.
A creature counts as their size for the purposes of Collision Damage.

After using Blue Manipulation on his turn, Satoru can immediately use it again as a bonus action. Satoru can make these instances of Blue Manipulation occur at the same time, so as to make objects or creatures collide.
Satoru can spend up to 11 additional cursed energy when using Blue Manipulation, gaining a bonus to the check equal to the amount spent.
Satoru can use Blue Manipulation out of combat to freely manipulate tiny objects at no cursed energy cost.

action.name: Spacial Manipulation (10 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: Satoru can cast {@spell teleport} by utilizing his teleport technique, and instead of only needing to be on the same plane of existence it needs to be on the same planet. Satoru always gets the exact location he wants to go. This application of teleportation still requires a clear path, such as the open sky or an opening of a building.

action.name: Red (24 Cursed Energy, S-1)
entries: Choose a point within 40 feet. All creatures within the range of the explosion centered on that point must make a {@dc 27} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are knocked back 100 feet, take 260 ({@damage 40d12}) Force damage, and are knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, the damage is halved and they are not knocked back nor {@condition prone}. The explosion range can be a radius from 5 ft. up to 30 ft. (Satoru's choice). Satoru may only have a single Red active at a time.

Alternatively, Satoru may make a ranged cursed energy attack roll against a creature up to 120 feet away, shooting a more precise version of red that targets only said creature. On a hit, the creature takes Red's damage, and must make the Strength saving throw as normal to be knocked back.
This technique deals twice as much damage to objects and structures. Objects within the area that aren't being worn or carried also take this damage.

action.name: Hollow Purple (S-2)
entries: As a rooting distracting action (which cannot be reduced), Satoru begins {@status concentration||concentrating} as if {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell. If Satoru successfully maintains {@status concentration} until the beginning of his next turn, as an action (V-1) he may pay the cost of both his Red and Blue, and end his {@status concentration}, forcing every creature in a 15-foot wide 200-foot long line to make a {@dc 27} Dexterity saving throw at disadvantage. On a failure, they take force damage equal to the combined dice of Satoru's Red and Blue, increased by 27 dice. On a successful save, they take half damage. Objects within the area that aren't being worn or carried also take this damage.

This counts as an extension technique for the purposes of other features. When Satoru uses Hollow Purple, any Tokyo enhancements or Technique Specialist enhancements to Red or Blue can be used as if he were using the technique normally, adding to the total damage of Hollow Purple.

action.name: Cursed Energy Ray (1-22 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru can launch a cursed energy ball in a line of 30ft. Once it reaches the end of the line, it will explode in a 15ft. circle, making every creature in that range make a {@dc 27} Strength, Dexterity or Constitution saving throw (Satoru's choice). On a failure, they take ({@dice 2d6 + 2d6} per cursed energy spent) force damage, and half as much on a success. Objects and structures take twice as much damage from this feature. When Satoru uses the maximum amount of cursed energy on this feature, it becomes a cursed energy nuke. It's range becomes a 60ft line and explodes in a 30 ft cylinder, dealing an additional 40 damage and deals twelve times as much damage to objects and structures (instead of twice as much).

action.name: Cursed Energy Pressure (1-11 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru forces all creatures within 5 ft. times the amount of cursed energy spent to make a {@dc 27} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they take ({@dice 4d12 + 1d12} per cursed energy spent) force damage and are knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, they take half as much and are not knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Basic Barrier (1-5 Cursed Energy, S-1)
entries: Satoru creates a dome starting from himself with a 5ft. radius and a 10ft. height. For each cursed energy spent, the barrier's maximum radius and height both increase by 10ft, but Satoru may choose to make it smaller if he wishes. This barrier has 10 hit points times the amount of cursed energy spent, it has resistance to all damage from the inside, is vulnerable to all damage from the outside and is immune to Poison and Psychic damage. This barrier has 27 AC and automatically fails saving throws. This barrier lasts 5 minutes. Satoru can deactivate his basic barrier at will (no action required).

action.name: Curtain (5 Cursed Energy, S-1, V-1)
entries: Emerge from the darkness, blacker than darkness. Purify that which is impure.

Satoru covers the sky and the ground within a 2165-foot radius with a sphere of darkness. Upon activation, a dark, almost liquid-like matter drips down from the sky to form the outer barrier wall in the shape of a sphere that extends underground, appearing as a dome from the surface. The curtain changes the sky becomes dark despite the time of day, provoking any cursed spirits out of hiding. Those without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class cannot see anything inside the barrier, merely seeing as if nothing but the vegetation was there. Creatures who can see into the ethereal plane or have levels in the jujutsu sorcerer path can see the black sphere.
The curtain lasts for 100 minutes. Satoru's curtain has 150 hit points. This barrier has 27 AC and automatically fails saving throws. It is also immune to Poison and Psychic damage.
Satoru may choose to make your curtain smaller in radius, however it cannot be smaller than a 100-foot radius.

action.name: New Shadow Style: Simple Domain (5 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: Satoru surrounds himself with a 5 ft. barrier for 1 minute, though said barrier does not impede movement. The circle has 180 hit points. While inside of a Domain Expansion, the sure-hit feature and sure-hit effect is negated against creatures inside the simple domain but at the start of Satoru's turn the simple domain will take {@damage 3d12} force damage per 100 refinement the domain has (minimum of {@dice 3d12}). If the simple domain is reduced to 0 hit points, this technique ends. Any innate techniques or spells that target Satoru, creatures inside his simple domain, or targets an area that overlaps with his simple domain, will have its damage dice reduced by 12. If it does not deal damage, it will instead have its Cursed Save DC reduced by 6. As an action while his simple domain is active, Satoru can reduce his movement speed to 0 until the beginning of his next turn, and cause his simple domain to regain 50 hit points. Satoru's refinement for his Simple Domain is 20.

action.name: Domain Expansion: Unlimited Void (31 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru opens his Domain Expansion, trapping all creatures within 50 feet inside it.

action.name: Defensive Falling Blossom Emotion (10 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: Satoru can shroud himself in cursed energy for 1 minute. The sure-hit feature of the Domain Expansion is negated. This prevents any Cursed Energy attack rolls from automatically hitting him and prevents him from automatically failing any Cursed Energy saves caused by the Domain Expansion. Satoru gains 220 special damage reduction that applies to the sure hit effect for the duration of this technique. If the damage from the Domain Expansion does not exceed the damage reduction, its damage will be negated and any effects it would apply are negated as well. This feature cannot prevent the effects or damage of anything non-physical, such as a mental attack which does psychic damage.
Satoru must maintain {@status concentration} on Falling Blossom Emotion to keep it active.

action.name: Offensive Falling Blossom Emotion (5 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: Satoru can surround himself with cursed energy and prepare to make counterattacks for the next minute. When he receives a melee attack roll, he can use his reaction to make one melee weapon attack with an advantage. On a hit, the attack is considered as a critical hit and will deal its damage as force damage. While maintaining {@status concentration} on Falling Blossom Emotion, Satoru may continue to counterattack against melee attack rolls as long as he has the reactions.
Satoru must maintain {@status concentration} on Falling Blossom Emotion to keep it active.

action.name: Mastered Reverse Cursed Technique
entries: Satoru spends any amount of Cursed Energy, regaining 30 hit points for each energy he spends, and removing the same amount of healing from his healing pool. For each 10 hit points healed he may also end the {@condition poisoned} condition on himself.

bonus.name: Martial Arts
entries: When Satoru uses the Attack action with an unarmed strike on his turn, he can make one unarmed strike or attack with a one-handed weapon.

bonus.name: Cursed Blast of Blows (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Immediately after taking the Attack action on his turn, Satoru can perform two unarmed strikes.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru can take the Dodge action.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru can take the Disengage or Dash action on his turn, and his jump distance is doubled until the end of his turn.

bonus.name: Maximum Output
entries: Satoru must spend Cursed Energy equal to the original cost of one of his lapse, extension, or maximum techniques twice. The first time he pays this cost, any reductions that apply to the technique are used; the second time, none are applied. Doing this will amplify its cursed energy output, making it gain two of the following benefits the next time it's cast before the end of his next turn: The technique's range and area of effect are increased by half its original range, rounded to the highest 5. For example, a technique with 10ft. of range would become a 15ft. range technique. This range increase does not work with touch-based techniques.

The technique's damage dice is increased by half of its original dice, or damage bonus if the technique is a flat damage, rounded up. For example, a technique with {@dice 10d10} die of damage would add {@dice 5d10} die of damage to the total damage die.
The technique's bonus to hit is increased by 5.
The technique's DC is increased by 5.
The technique's duration is increased by half of its original duration. For example, a technique that lasts a minute would last 1 minute and 5 rounds.
Satoru cannot choose the same benefit twice.
Maximum Output lasts on a technique (once it has been casted) for 5 rounds.

bonus.name: Infinity Pressure (1-16 Cursed Energy)
entries: While Infinity is active or while Gojo activates it, he may increase its density and sheer force. Every creature that has Domain Amplification active, that starts their turn within 5ft. of Satoru must make a {@dc 27} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are pushed back 5 ft. and are knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, they are not pushed back and they do not become {@condition prone}. If knocked against a wall of stone or into a solid surface (consult the GM), their saving throw becomes against taking {@damage 2d10} force damage for every cursed energy spent, and being {@condition restrained}, as they are crushed against the wall, and for every damage the creature suffers the object or wall behind it suffers the same damage in either situation on failure. Gojo must spend a number of Cursed Energy equal to the amount spent on activation at the beginning of his turns to keep this form of Infinity active. If a creature has spent equal to or more cursed energy than Satoru on Domain Amplification, they are unaffected by Infinity Pressure.
While infinity pressure is active, Satoru's movement speed is reduced to 10ft, and he cannot make melee attack rolls.

In addition, if a creature is using Domain Amplification in order to negate infinity, infinity will not be nullified if Satoru has spent a greater amount of cursed energy on infinity pressure than they have spent on domain amplification. Satoru cannot use Maximum Output to bypass domain amplification. Satoru may use infinity pressure as a reaction to a creature hitting him with a melee attack roll while they have domain amplification is active.

bonus.name: Blue Augmented Speed (1-31 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru can increase his movement speed by 15 ft per energy spent. This lasts for an amount of rounds equal to half the energy spent.

bonus.name: Double Palm Strike (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru may deliver a double palm strike to an enemy's gut, staggering them for a brief period. Make an unarmed strike against a creature within range. On a hit, they are pushed back 10ft and must make a {@dc 27} Constitution saving throw. On a success, they are unaffected. On a failure, their movement speed is reduced by 90 feet, until the end of their next turn, and they cannot benefit from any increases to their speed for the duration.

bonus.name: Technique Analyzer
entries: As a bonus action within 1 minute of seeing an Extension, Reversal, or Maximum technique be used while Satoru's eyes were uncovered, he may make an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check against its cursed energy DC. On a failure, he must wait to see the feature used again. On a success, he will learn the feature and its capabilities.

bonus.name: Illuminated Vision (6 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru can expand his range of truesight to 120 feet for 1 minute in initiative, or 1 hour out of initiative.

bonus.name: Untraceable Speed
entries: Satoru may take the dash action or make a skill check he is proficient in.

bonus.name: Taijutsu Sorcery
entries: Whenever Satoru takes the Attack action, he can make one unarmed strike, shove, grapple, or weapon attack as a bonus action.

bonus.name: Afterimage Assault (1-26 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru may create a number of 1 hit point clones that are identical to him up to half of the cursed energy spent. The clones have 20 AC. These clones can be used to grant flanking and can only be up to a quarter of Satoru's movement speed away from himself, and they move when he moves. Once per round, Satoru may swap with one of these clones as a bonus action or reaction to being targeted by an attack or being forced to make a saving throw.
These clones last for up to 1 minute, and Satoru must use at least half of his movement speed a turn to keep the clones active.

A creature can distinguish these clones from the original by making a {@dc 27} Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check. They have advantage on this check if they have the Cursed Energy Tracker option from the Advanced Energy Usage class feature, and are focusing on Satoru with it.

bonus.name: Domain Expansion: Unlimited Void (31 Cursed Energy)
entries: Satoru opens his Domain Expansion, trapping all creatures within 50 feet inside it.

bonus.name: Mastered Reverse Cursed Technique
entries: Satoru may use his Mastered Reverse Cursed Technique.

bonus.name: Burnout Recovery (8 Cursed Energy)
entries: Whilst under the effects of Technique Burnout, Satoru can damage his brain with cursed energy, roughly where his innate technique is most predominantly etched, dealing 195 ({@damage 30d12}) force damage to himself, which cannot be reduced in any way. Once Satoru has healed the damage taken by using Reverse Cursed Technique, starting at the beginning of the next round, he will regain his Cursed Technique and remove the {@condition exhaustion} gained from burning out.

After Satoru has used this feature, the next time he attempts to use his Domain Expansion or something with equal strain on his brain, Satoru must succeed on a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw. On a success, he will be able to carry out the action, such as properly expanding his Domain Expansion. On a failure, Satoru will suffer serious stress to his brain, being unable to expand his domain or completing the action, and taking 90 ({@damage 20d8}) force damage, which cannot be reduced in any way.

The Constitution saving throw DC increases by 5 for every success and returns to its original DC at the end of a long rest. Once the DC reaches 40, Satoru becomes unable to pass the Constitution saving throw, reaching his limit.

reaction.name: Teleport Technique (6 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: When Satoru is targeted by an attack, spell, melee cursed energy attack roll, ranged cursed energy attack roll, or any saving throws, he may teleport up to an empty space up to his movement speed in feet, potentially causing the attack or effect to miss if he becomes out of range or area. If it was an attack roll, Satoru must make a Dexterity check against the attack roll, successfully escaping it on a success. If it was a saving throw or effect, make a Dexterity check against the Save DC of the effect or saving throw, on a success avoiding the effect. If Satoru fails the Dexterity check, he will still be subject to the effect, teleporting after it occurs. Satoru can only use this feature once per round.

reaction.name: Technique Analyzer
entries: As a reaction to seeing an Extension, Reversal, or Maximum technique be used while Satoru's eyes were uncovered, he may make an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check against its cursed energy DC. On a failure, he must wait to see the feature used again. On a success, he will learn the feature and its capabilities.

reaction.name: Untraceable Speed
entries: Satoru may make one of the following reactions: As a reaction to passing a saving throw or an attack roll being made against him, Satoru may move up to half of his speed in a direction of his choice. This movement cannot be used to exit domains. If this movement would cause Satoru to leave the area targeted by an attack roll he's reacting to he may roll an {@skill Acrobatics} check to attempt to fully dodge. Satoru rolls against the attack roll, causing the attack to miss regardless of his armor class if his {@skill Acrobatics} check is higher than the attack roll. On a failure, the attack happens as normal, though he still moves as normal. This roll automatically fails if the attack was a critical hit, unless he's 10 or more levels above the attacker's level, or CR if they don't have a level. If Satoru fails this roll by no more than 3 against an attack targeting one of his limbs with the Limb System then though he can't fully dodge he may change which limb is targeted. In the case of a saving throw Satoru is unaffected by the saving throw if he moves out of the area of effect.
Satoru can make an opportunity attack as a reaction if he moved into range of a creature or a creature enters his range. Satoru can only do this once per creature per round.

reaction.name: Cursed Martial Artist
entries: As a reaction when a creature fails a melee attack roll against him, Satoru can make one unarmed strike against them. On a successful hit, Satoru may add 10 to the damage roll.

reaction.name: Taijutsu Sorcery
entries: Whenever Satoru takes the Attack action on his turn, he may take the Dodge action as a reaction to an attack roll made against him, however, this Dodge action only causes melee attacks against him from the triggering attacker to gain disadvantage. If a creature rolls a natural 1 on a melee attack against him whilst he has performed this variation of the Dodge action, Satoru can make one attack against that creature if it is within range once per round.

reaction.name: New Shadow Style: Simple Domain (5 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: As a reaction against a cursed energy attack roll or saving throw, spell, domain expansion opening, or being affected by the sure hit effect of a Domain Expansion Satoru expands his Simple Domain. Satoru cannot react to the effects of a Domain Expansion with this feature the first time he would get the opportunity after his simple domain ends or is broken.

reaction.name: Cursed Enhanced Body (1-11 Cursed Energy)
entries: Whenever Satoru makes a Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence or Wisdom saving throw, he may add the number of energy spent as a bonus to the saving throw. Satoru has an additional reaction that may only be used for this feature.

reaction.name: Defensive Falling Blossom Emotion (10 Cursed Energy, S-2)
entries: When being affected by a sure hit effect of a Domain Expansion, or seeing a Domain Expansion open, Satoru may use his Defensive Falling Blossom Emotion.

reaction.name: Mastered Reverse Cursed Technique
entries: When Satoru takes damage, he may use his Mastered Reverse Cursed Technique. Satoru has 5 additional reaction that may only be used for this feature.

legendary.name: Evasive Attack
entries: Satoru moves up to half his movement and can make one Unarmed Strike.

legendary.name: Reverse Cursed Technique
entries: Satoru uses his reverse cursed technique.

legendary.name: Blue or Red (2 Actions Required)
entries: Satoru uses Blue or Red.

legendary.name: Chanting (3 Actions Required)
entries: Satoru can start chanting.

legendary.name: Domain Expansion (3 Actions Required)
entries: Satoru uses his Domain Expansion.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The curse has 26 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: The curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns.

trait.name: Cursed Attack
entries: The curse's attacks are considered magical.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it is {@condition invisible}. Creatures with levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who can see into the Ethereal Plane can see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see you.

trait.name: Sadism
entries: Whenever the Chiko deals damage to someone who is {@condition frightened} of it, half of the total damage will be converted in temporary hit points to themselves. These temporary hit points cannot go above its level/CR.

trait.name: Tailed Advancement
entries: Chiko range in power with the most powerful Chiko ever being Tamomo no Mae Incarnate.
