As they grow older and more in power they gain another tail and whenever they gain another tail their Strength, Dexterity, and Charisma scores all increase by 1, as well as they gain another level in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class. An example of how they would gain tails is through another kitsune, age, or possibly a cursed object.

trait.name: Phasing
entries: Due to being composed of a smaller amount of cursed energy, the curse may walk through objects and structures as if they weren't there(They lose this if they are no longer considered Grade 3).

trait.name: Cursed Strikes
entries: Whenever the Chiko makes an unarmed strike, it can spend up to 3 cursed energy to increase its damage. On a hit, it deals {@dice 1d8} extra necrotic damage per energy spent. This may also be used as a response to the curse hitting a Nat 20. This energy persists until the curse hits a target or 1 minute passes. 50 meters in 3 seconds!. Masaki gitsune has 2 reactions.

trait.name: Shapeshifter
entries: As an action the Chiko can turn into a young beautiful woman however it requires {@status concentration} to maintain this form.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse makes three attacks with its unarmed strikes or bite.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@dice 1d6}) bludgeoning and 7 ({@damage 1d12}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Shapeshift
entries: The Chiko uses the Shapeshifter feature.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 8 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

bonus.name: Cursed Blast of Blows
entries: Immediately after taking the Attack action on its turn, it can spend 3 cursed energy to perform two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense
entries: It can spend 3 cursed energy to take the Dodge action as a bonus action its your turn.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step
entries: It can spend 3 cursed energy to take the Disengage or Dash action as a bonus action on its turn, and your jump distance is doubled until the end of its turn.

entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

entries: The curse doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe. It doesn't need to sleep. It has advantage on saving throws against disease and being {@condition poisoned}.

entries: The curse reduces all damage it takes but psychic or thunder by 4. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the curse moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a ram attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 13 ({@damage 2d12}) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Sure-Footed
entries: The curse has advantage on Strength and Dexterity saving throws made against effects that would knock it {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Cliff-Climber
entries: As long as the curse is on dry ground, it is not affected by difficult terrain.

trait.name: Unholy Rage
entries: Whenever the curse makes an attack against a humanoid creature, it has advantage on the attack roll. Additionally whenever it makes a humanoid creature make a saving throw, the creature has disadvantage on it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse may make two attacks; one with its ram and one with its kick.

entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}24 ({@damage 3d12 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Kick
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d10 + 5}) bludgeoning damage. The target must also make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be pushed back 20 feet.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: The curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns.

trait.name: Cursed Attack
entries: The curse's attacks are considered magical.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it is {@condition invisible}. Creatures with levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who can see into the Ethereal Plane can see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The curse doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe. It doesn't need to sleep. It has advantage on saving throws against disease and being {@condition poisoned}.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: The curse has two reactions.

trait.name: Untraceable Speed
entries: Whenever the curse makes a Dexterity check, instead of rolling the {@dice d20}, it can choose to automatically roll a 10 and use it for the check. It cannot use this for the Untraceable Speed Dodge.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The tiger has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the curse moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the curse can make one bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Sneak Attack
entries: Once per turn, the curse deals an extra 14 ({@damage 4d6}) damage when it hits a target with a weapon attack and has advantage on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally of the curse that isn't {@condition incapacitated} and the curse doesn't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

trait.name: King of the Jungle
entries: The curse has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) while hiding within a forested or jungle environment.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) slashing damage.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 6 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

bonus.name: Untraceable Speed Ability
entries: The curse may perform the Dash action or make a Skill Check it is proficient in as a bonus action.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

reaction.name: Untraceable Speed Dodge
entries: As a reaction to passing a saving throw or an attack roll being made against the curse, it may move up to half of its speed in a direction of its choice. This movement cannot be used to exit domains. If this movement would cause it to leave the area targeted by an attack roll it is reacting to it may roll a {@skill Acrobatics} check to attempt to fully dodge. It rolls against the attack roll, causing the attack to miss regardless of its armor class if its {@skill Acrobatics} check is higher than the attack roll. On a failure, the attack happens as normal, though it still moves as normal. This roll automatically fails if the attack was a critical hit, unless it is 10 or more levels above the attacker's level, or CR if they don't have a level. If it fails this roll by no more than 3 against an attack targeting one of its limbs with the Limb System then though it can't fully dodge it may change which limb is targeted. In the case of a saving throw it is unaffected by the saving throw if it moves out of the area of effect.

reaction.name: Untraceable Speed Assault
entries: The curse can make an opportunity attack as a reaction if it moved into range of a creature or a creature enters its range. It can only do this once per creature per round.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The curse has 35 cursed energy it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: The curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of its turns.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

action.name: Smash
entries: The Nurikabe falls down with great speed towards the ground. Every creature within 20 ft. of the Nurikabe when it falls must make a Dexterity saving throw (Dc 13), suffering 5 ({@damage 1d10}) bludgeoning damage per 10 ft. traveled by the Nurikabe (maximum {@dice 25d10}) on a failure or half as much on a success.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 5 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

entries: The curse doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe. It doesn't need to sleep. It has advantage on saving throws against disease and being {@condition poisoned}.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: The curse has two reactions.

trait.name: Armored Body
entries: The curse reduces all damage it takes but psychic by 10.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The curse can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Sneak Attack
entries: Once per turn, the curse deals an extra 26 ({@damage 4d12}) damage when it hits a target with a weapon attack and has advantage on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally of the curse that isn't {@condition incapacitated} and the curse doesn't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

action.name: Tongue Stab
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 100 ft, one target. {@h}44 ({@damage 6d12 + 6}) piercing damage and the target is {@condition grappled}. It may only have one target {@condition grappled} like this at a time.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}39 ({@damage 6d10 + 6}) piercing damage.

action.name: Anchor (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: While the curse is attached to a surface, it may anchor itself to that surface. While the curse is Anchored to a surface, the damage reduction from Armored Body is doubled and it has advantage on all saving throws or checks to be moved. However, it also has its movement set to 0 and has disadvantage to all Dexterity saving throws.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 6 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

bonus.name: Reel
entries: The curse uses its tongue to pull a creature {@condition grappled} by it up to 50 feet straight toward it. If this pulls the creature within 5 feet of the curse, it may make a bite attack as a part of the same action.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

reaction.name: Surprise Attack (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: While the curse is Anchored and a creature enters its reach that it has not been noticed by, as a reaction it may make a Tongue Stab attack against them.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The curse has 50 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: The curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns.

trait.name: Cursed Attack
entries: The curse's attacks are considered magical.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it is {@condition invisible}. Creatures with levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who can see into the Ethereal Plane can see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The curse doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe. It doesn't need to sleep. It has advantage on saving throws against disease and being {@condition poisoned}.

trait.name: Disease Imbued Strikes
entries: The curse's natural weapons all add {@damage 2d6} poison damage (already applied). In addition, if it try to inflict the {@condition poisoned} condition in a creature, they roll their saving throw with disadvantage.

trait.name: Walking Plague
entries: Every time the curse touches a creature with a body part of itself, they must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they will be infected with the common cold. On a success, they will become immune to this effect for 24 hours. This effect happens only once per round.

trait.name: Common Cold
entries: When a creature is infected with this disease, they have disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks, Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell or taste, and any Charisma checks involving speaking with other people.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: The curse has two reactions.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse may make two attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) piercing damage + 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage and the target must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the end of the curse's next turn.

action.name: Coughing Fit (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: While a creature is {@condition poisoned} by the curse or inflected with the Common Cold, it can use its action to force the creature to make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw at disadvantage. On a failure, the curse forces the creature to have a fit of intense coughing until the end of their next turn. The creature repeats this saving throw at the end of their turns. While the creature is coughing, they are unable to preform actions that require a person's mouth and throat such as speaking or performing the a verbal component for a cursed technique.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 7 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The Drone Ant Curse has 27 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: The Drone Ant Curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns.

trait.name: Cursed Attack
entries: The Drone Ant Curse's attacks are considered magical.
Sticky Legs The curses climbing speed is the same as walking speed.
Weak Exoskeleton As long as the curse is above half heath it gains +1 AC, and lose 5ft movement speed. (This is already acounted for in the sheet.)

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The Drone Ant Curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it is {@condition invisible}. Creatures with levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who can see into the Ethereal Plane can see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see you.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: The curse haves two reactions Acrobatic Fighter the Drone Ant Curse uses the environment to its advantage.
It may calculate itsjump distance and height with its Dexterity score. Its jump distance and height is doubled.

Whenever it makes an Bite or Strike while within 5 ft. of a solid surface or another object like a pole, It may use it to leverage a powerful attack against Its opponent, dealing an additional damage die of its Bite or Strike. If there are not any solid surfaces, it may use half its movement speed to drop to the ground on its legs, extending its legs up and making a powerful kick.
Once per turn, whenever it fall onto a creature, the Drone Ant Curse makes a {@dc 10} {@skill Acrobatics} check. On a success, it may make one Bite or Strike against that creature.
Pheromones When The Drone Ant Curse is within a 10ft radius around another creature with the feature Pheromones, it gains advantage on melee attack rolls.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: The Drone Ant Curse will reduce all damage it takes but psychic or thunder by 4. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this.

Mandibles When the Drone Ant Curse lands a successful melee attack while it is useing flying speed it can choose the target to make a {@dc 17} Dexterity save, if they fail then they are {@condition grappled}, when the creature is {@condition grappled} the Drone Ant Curse can't use Throw, but it gain +7 extra to hit with Bite.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Drone Ant Curse makes three attacks: one with its Strike and two with its Bite.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d8 + 7}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Throw
entries: The Drone Ant Curse picks up a creature within its movement speed. The creature must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw, if it failed it will be {@condition grappled} by the Drone Ant Curse. After that will the Drone Ant Curse use all its remaining movement speed to fly up and throw the creature down.

action.name: Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d6 + 7}) Slashing damage.

Wing Vibrations (2 Cursed Energy) The curse vibrates its wing collecting dust up and around, every creature inside a 10ft radius around the Drone Ant Curse makes a {@dc 17} Constitution save, if failed then they become {@condition blinded} until the start of their next turn.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The Drone Ant Curse can spend up to 8 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

bonus.name: Emergency Exit
entries: The curse moves up to half of its movement speed in any direction.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the Drone Ant Curse uses its Regeneration.

trait.name: Hidden Visage While the curse remains motionless and isn't flying, it is indistinguishable from a normal version of whatever it's currently disguised as
entries: However, when viewed by Sorcerers, this object while motionless appears as a cursed tool (Check table below) and the sorcerer must make a {@dc 20} {@skill Arcana} to tell that the cursed energy emanating from this curse isn't from it being a cursed tool. It may make its first attack against a creature with advantages if it was previously motionless.

Number rolled on {@dice d6}	Pressumed grade	Benefits 1	Ungraded	None 2	Grade 4	Half Cha mod additional {@dice d8} on all attacks, add cha mod to cursed energy pool total. 3	Grade 3	Half Cha mod additional {@dice d10} added on all attacks (Necrotic), add cha mod added twice to cursed energy pool total. 4	Grade 2	Half Cha mod additional {@dice d12} added on all attacks (Necrotic), add Cha mod added thrice to cursed energy pool, add plus two to all physical statitistics. 5	Grade 1	Half Cha mod additional {@dice 2d6} added on all attacks (Necrotic), add Cha mod added four times to cursed energy pool, add plus four to all physical statistics and plus two to cha. 6	Special Grade	Cha mod additional {@dice 2d8} added on all attacks (Necrotic), add Cha mod added five times to cursed energy pool, add plus six to all physical statitistics and plus 4 to cha, in addition the dm may make a custom effect similar to other special grade weapons.

trait.name: Mimicked Might
entries: Depending on what is rolled for this curse in terms of grade will bolster it's strength further, for every grade above ungraded it is recommended to increase the cr of the encounter by one, additionally increase the creatures hp total by 20 for each grade above ungraded.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: This Curse has 24 Cursed energy. All of the cursed energy, when used, is unavailable until it takes a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: This Curse regains 1 Cursed Energy at the start of it's turn.

trait.name: Cursed Armor (1-4 Cursed Energy)
entries: On its turn or on another creatures turn this curse may spend up to 4 Cursed Energy gaining 5 temporary hit points for every 1 spent.

trait.name: Back Stab
entries: When the curse makes an attack against a creature for every hidden terror curse within 5 feet of that same creature it adds {@damage 1d12} bludioning damage to the attack (Max {@dice 3d12}) in addition if this curse scores a critical hit against a creature they must make a {@dc 18} Strength save or fall {@condition prone}

trait.name: Found Purpose
entries: The curse still counts as an object for the sake of creatures using it. If the curse is used in a way matching the original form it once had before turning into a curse (determined by DM), it must make a {@dc 20} Charisma saving throw or revert back into a normal object. This turns the curse into a Cursed tool version of the original object it was with a grade matching what was rolled on the table from Hidden Visage. If the curse has no cursed energy left when this happens, it instead transforms into a non-cursed tool version of that same object. The curse only makes a saving throw for this effect once per round.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse can make 4 Slam attacks. It may switch one of these attacks out with Overhead.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 2d12 + 8}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Overhead {@recharge}
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, 5 ft., one target. {@h}62 ({@damage 6d12 + 8}) Piercing damage. The curse may also spend 8 cursed energy as part of this attack to also force the target to make a {@dc 15} Constitution save or gain the {@condition stunned} condition until the start of the creatures next turn.

bonus.name: Advanced Regeneration (1-7 Cursed Energy)
entries: This curse may spend up to 7 Cursed Energy to heal 15 hit points per cursed energy spent.

bonus.name: Shape Change (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: When a creature discovers its true nature, the curse can use a bonus action change its shape into another Mace and make a {@skill Stealth} check to attempt to evade detection again. If this check is successful, it will appear as a normal version of the Mace it changed into until inspected. If there is no visibly similar objects that the curse may change into, it may not take this action.

trait.name: Hunter
entries: When the curse rolls initiative, it chooses a target that rolled lower than it, that creature becomes the Prey. When the curse lands a Bite attack on a Prey, it may choose to spend 3 Cursed Energy to force the Prey to make a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw or gain the bleeding condition until the end of the curse's next turn.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it is {@condition invisible}. Creatures with levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who can see into the Ethereal Plane can see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The curse doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe. It doesn't need to sleep. It has advantage on saving throws against disease and being {@condition poisoned}.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: The curse has two reactions.
Cursed Energy Tracker.

The curse may make a {@skill Perception} check, adding its Charisma modifier, to see if there are any remains of cursed energy in the place it's at, and if it succeed it knows exactly who the energy is from. In case it does not know who the person is, then it can only track down where the cursed energy trail leads it to. The DC is equal to the cursed energy DC of the creature it's trying to track. Tracking residual cursed energy may not always lead all the way to the person, with the DM determining how the residual trail goes. Usage of a cursed technique or cursed energy fueled feature will often leave much more residuals than normal.

The curse has learned to read the flow of other's cursed energy. It knows when any creature it can see spends cursed energy and how much they spend when doing so. Additionally, as a free action, it can focus on a creature for 1 minute, seeing the "sparks" of cursed energy that always happen before cursed technique usage. For that minute when that creature forces the curse to make a save using their cursed technique or makes a cursed energy attack roll against it using their cursed technique they must roll {@skill Stealth}, using Charisma instead of Dexterity, against its {@skill Perception}. If the curse wins it gets advantage on the saving throw, or the attack roll is made with disadvantage. The curse can focus on one creature at a time.

entries: The curse has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks to track creatures that it has sensed the cursed energy of.

entries: The curse can have up to six tendrils at a time. Each tendril can be attacked (AC 20; 30 hit points; immunity to poison and psychic damage). Destroying a tendril deals no damage to the curse, which can extrude a replacement tendril on its next turn. A tendril can also be broken if a creature takes an action and succeeds on a {@dc 15} Strength check against it.

Until the grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained} and has disadvantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws, and the curse can't use the same tendril on another target.

entries: The roper pulls each creature {@condition grappled} by it up to 50 feet straight toward it.

trait.name: Curse-Empowered Strikes

trait.name: Cursed Strike
entries: Whenever the curse makes a Claw attack, it can spend up to 3 cursed energy to increase its damage. On a hit, it deals {@dice 1d8} extra necrotic damage per energy spent. This may also be used as a response to the curse hitting a Nat 20. This energy persists until the curse hits a target or 1 minute passes.

entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) piercing damage, 3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage. If the target is medium or smaller the curse may make a grapple check against the target. The curse may only have two creatures {@condition grappled} in this manner.

action.name: Scrap Shot
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 8} to hit, range 120/360 ft., one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 4d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage. The curse spits a massive flaming ball of scrap from its mouth.

action.name: Magnet (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: The curse activates various magnets around its body. Every creature in a 30 ft. radius around the curse that is wearing metalic clothing or armor must make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw, being dragged towards the closest empty space to the curse. If a creature is wearing only small metalic objects or holding metalic objects, they must also make the saving throw, but on a failure instead they lost said items which then cling to the curse's body. If there are loose metalic objects within the radius, they will also be pulled towards the curse and cling to its body, which grants it 30 temporary hit points. Additionally, if there are creatures in the trajectory of the flying metalic objects that are pulled towards the curse, they need to be successful on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw, taking damage depending on the size of the object and of an appropriate damage type on a failure:
Tiny object: 2 Small object: {@dice 2d4} Medium object: {@dice 3d6} Large object: {@dice 4d8} Huge object: {@dice 5d10} Gargantuan object: {@dice 6d12}

bonus.name: Regeneration

bonus.name: Furnace Recycle
entries: The curse can eat metalic objects with its mouth, which then are destroyed in the furnace inside its body and turned into cursed energy, depending on their size. The curse can eat Tiny, Small, Medium and Large metalic objects, which gives it 1, 3, 5 and 8 cursed energy respectively. It can also as a Full Round Action eat a Huge object and gain 12 cursed energy, and over the course of 1 minute it can eat a Gargantuan one to gain 20 cursed energy.

Colossal Physique Counts as two sizes larger for grappling, shoving, disarming, and carrying capacity.
Adds proficiency bonus to all Strength checks.
Strength-based attacks deal double damage to objects and structures.

Once per turn, after a successful melee attack, can force a creature hit to make a DC13 Strength saving throw. On failure, they are knocked back 30ft. If they collide with an object or another creature, they take 4 ({@damage 1d6}) bludgeoning damage for every 10ft traveled or half the damage roll for another creature. Critical hits cause the automatic failure of the saving throw and double knockback.
Can give advantage on Strength checks or saving throws 2 times per long rest.
Athlete History The curse unarmed strike will have its damage dice increase two tiers.

Strong Body The curse will now add its Constitution modifier twice when calculating hit points gained per level. The curse will reduce all damage it takes but psychic or thunder by its Constitution modifier. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this.

Forced By Wind Whenever an attack action where the curse uses more than 3 cursed energy, a slash of wind will also be created, the slash will target the same creature, and the creature makes a Dexterity saving throw to not be hit by the blade. if they fail the target takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) slashing damage.

entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 3d10 + 6}) slashing damage.

action.name: Hyper Edge(1-5 Cursed Energy)

Upon arrival of the targets, the slash splits in two. The target makes a DC13 Dexterity saving throw. If they fail they take {@damage 2d6 + 4} slashing damage per cursed energy spent, if they succeed they take half.

bonus.name: Dual Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}36 ({@damage 4d12 + 3}) piercing damage.

It will fail all Wisdom and Intelligence saving throws. On the next attack, the creature makes after Leaves Of Focus is done. will have a {@hit 4} to hit, if it is a Dexterity saving throw the DC will increase with 3. And if it is an ability check, it will gain +3 to the check.

action.name: Bite Melee Weapon Attack:
entries: {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Chain Flight
entries: The curse can attach its chains to nearby surfaces to mimic the ability to fly. While within at least 60 ft. of a solid surface, the curse can use one or more of its chains (as in its Animate Chains feature) as a mean to gain a flying speed equal to its walking speed. Chains designated to this feature cannot attack, and the curse loses its flying speed if they are destroyed.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse makes 2 chain attacks

action.name: Chain
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage. If the target is large or smaller the curse may make a grapple check against the target. If the curse has a creature {@condition grappled} in this manner, it may not grapple any other creatures with this attack.

action.name: Animate Chains (1+ Cursed Energy)
entries: The curse may pay 1 Cursed Energy per chain to create and control up to 4 chains within 60 feet of it that it can see, causing those chains to sprout razor-edged barbs made from Cursed Energy. It may also animate surrounding chains that it can see for no cursed energy requirement, provided they aren't being worn or carried. Each animated chain is an object with 20 AC, 20 hit points, resistance to piercing damage, and immunity to psychic and thunder damage. When the curse uses multiattack on its turn, it can use each animated chain to make one additional chain attack. An animated chain can grapple one creature of its own but can't make attacks while grappling. An animated chain reverts to its inanimate state if reduced to 0 hit points or if the curse is {@condition incapacitated} or dies.

action.name: Devour (4 Cursed Energy)
entries: The curse makes one bite attack against a Large or smaller target it is grappling. If the attack hits, the target is swallowed, and the grapple ends. The swallowed target is {@condition restrained}, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the curse, and it takes 39 ({@damage 6d12}) slashing damage at the start of each of the curse's turns as they are ripped apart. The swallowed creature can make use its action to make a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw to escape. On a success, they exit with 5 feet of the curse. The curse can have only three target swallowed at a time. If the curse dies, a swallowed creature is no longer {@condition restrained} by it and can escape from the corpse using 5 feet of movement, exiting {@condition prone}.

entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

entries: The curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns.

trait.name: Cursed Attack

entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it is {@condition invisible}. Creatures with levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who can see into the Ethereal Plane can see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see you.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The curse doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe. It doesn't need to sleep. It has advantage on saving throws against disease and being {@condition poisoned}.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the curse remains hidden, it is indistinguishable from normal gust of wind.

trait.name: Air Form
entries: The curse can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse makes two attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}33 ({@damage 4d12 + 7}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Wind Slash
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 11} to hit, range 40/120 ft., one target. {@h}29 ({@damage 4d10 + 7}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Whirlwind (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Each creature in the curse's space must make a {@dc 19} Strength saving throw. On a failure, a target takes 43 ({@damage 6d12 + 4}) bludgeoning damage and is flung up 70 feet away from the curse in a random direction and knocked {@condition prone}. If a thrown target strikes an object, such as a wall or floor, the target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was thrown. If the target is thrown at another creature, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 19} Dexterity saving throw or take the same damage and be knocked {@condition prone}.
If the saving throw is successful, the target takes half the bludgeoning damage and isn't flung away or knocked {@condition prone}.

entries: The curse reduces all damage it takes but psychic or thunder by 6. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this.

trait.name: Fire Form
entries: The curse can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. A creature that touches the curse or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) fire damage. In addition, the curse can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there. The first time it enters a creature's space on a turn, that creature takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) fire damage and catches fire; until someone takes an action to douse the fire, the creature takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The curse sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light in an additional 30 feet.

trait.name: Water Susceptibility
entries: For every 5 feet the curse moves in water, or for every gallon of water splashed on it, it takes 1 cold damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The elemental makes two touch attacks.

action.name: Touch
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 3d10 + 6}) fire damage. If the target is a creature or a flammable object, it ignites. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, the target takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

action.name: Fire Blast (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: As an action the curse releases wave of fire causing all creatures with a 50 ft. cone to make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw or take 44 ({@damage 8d10}) fire damage, on success the creature takes half damage. If the target is a creature or a flammable object, it ignites. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, the target takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

trait.name: Freeze
entries: If the curse takes cold damage, it partially freezes; its speed is reduced by 20 feet until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse makes two attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 3d12 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Whelm (6 Cursed Energy)
entries: Each creature in the curse's space must make a {@dc 18} Strength saving throw. On a failure, a target takes 33 ({@damage 6d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage. If it is Large or smaller, it is also {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 18}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained} and unable to breathe unless it can breathe water. If the saving throw is successful, the target is pushed out of the curse's space.

The curse can grapple one Large creature or up to two Medium or smaller creatures at one time. At the start of each of the curse's turns, each target {@condition grappled} by it takes 33 ({@damage 6d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage. A creature within 5 feet of the curse can pull a creature or object out of it by taking an action to make a {@dc 18} Strength check and succeeding.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 7 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

entries: The curse has 46 cursed energy it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

entries: The curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of its turns.

trait.name: Cursed Attack
entries: The curse's attacks are considered magical.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Cursed Mirror
entries: Whenever the curse is the target of a cursed energy attack roll or a saving throw from a cursed energy related feature or spell that covers an area, is physical and would touch it, it may pay cursed energy equal to the original cursed energy cost of the feature (before reductions), or triple the level of the spell if it is one to roll a {@dice d20}. On a 10 or higher, it deflects the effect to a random direction, is unaffected by it, and the effect now targets a different area of the GM's choice, but can't affect the curse. On a 16 or higher, the curse absorbs the cursed technique or spell within its mirror until the beginning of its next turn. The curse may spend double the amount of cursed energy it would to roll the {@dice d20} to automatically roll a 16.

action.name: Focus
entries: The curse chooses a creature it can see within 120 ft. of it as the target for focusing. Whenever the focused creature would trigger the curse's Cursed Mirror feature, the curse spends half as much cursed energy as it would, it may roll the {@dice d20} with advantage, and if it absorbs a cursed technique or spell, using its Cursed Reflection will not consume its reaction. Additionally, if the focused creature fails a cursed energy attack roll with a natural 1 against the curse, the curse passes a cursed technique or spell saving throw from the target or it absorbs a cursed technique or spell from the target by rolling a natural 20 with its Cursed Mirror, it regains cursed energy equal to the amount spent by the focused creature. The curse can only focus on one creature at a time, and focusing on it requires {@status concentration}.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 9 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: The curse takes the Disengage or Dash action, and its jump distance is doubled until the end of its turn.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: The curse takes the Dodge action.

reaction.name: Cursed Reflection
entries: When the curse absorbs a cursed technique or spell with its Cursed Mirror, it may immediatly shoot it back, targeting a creature or area of its choice as if it was the original caster of the technique or spell. The curse has 2 additional reactions it can use exclusively for this feature.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The curse doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe. It doesn't need to sleep. It has advantage on saving throws against disease and being {@condition poisoned}.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: The curse has two reactions.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: The curse reduces all damage it takes but psychic or thunder by 4. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the curse moves at least 20 ft. straight toward a target and then hits it with an antlers attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 19 ({@damage 3d12}) damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 18} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the curse can make bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Sure-Footed
entries: The moose has advantage on Strength and Dexterity saving throws made against effects that would knock it {@condition prone}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse makes two attacks: one with its antlers and one with its hooves.

action.name: Antlers
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 2d12 + 6}) piercing damage.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Cursed Blitz (4 Cursed Energy)
entries: The curse moves up to twice its speed in a line without provoking opportunity attacks, making an Antlers attack under the effects of Charge against each creature in the line.

entries: The curse can spend up to 8 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

entries: The Pollution Curse's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: The curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of its turns.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

One man's trash, another man's treasure. The Pollution Curse is a scavenger, and uses trash to attack. The Pollution Curse can spend a bonus action to make an attack with one item of trash presented below. X equals the number of Cursed Energy the Pollution Curse puts into the attack.

trait.name: Broken Bottle
entries: xd6 Piercing damage, two attacks may be made with this trash in a single bonus action.

trait.name: Hazardous Battery
entries: xd6 Lightning damage, target gains the {@condition stunned} condition.

trait.name: Weedkiller
entries: xd6 Acid damage, target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, otherwise they gain the {@condition blinded} condition for two rounds.

trait.name: Refrigerator
entries: xd10 Bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Plastic Bag
entries: The Pollution Curse can use this item to gain advantage on the next roll it makes to grapple a target, requires 5 cursed energy to use.

trait.name: Firecracker
entries: xd4 Thunder damage, target gains the {@condition deafened} condition for 5 rounds.

trait.name: Kitchen Knife
entries: xd6 Slashing damage, target gains the Bleeding condition.

trait.name: Toxic Fumes
entries: If a creature enters a space within 5 feet of the Pollution Curse, they are forced to make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. If the creature fails the saving throw, they take {@damage 2d12} Poison damage. On a success, they take half as much.

trait.name: Poisonous Ooze
entries: The Pollution Curse takes up its entire space. Other creatures can enter the space, but a creature that does so is subjected to the curse's Engulf and has disadvantage on the saving throw.
Creatures inside the Pollution Curse can be seen but have total cover.

A creature within 5 feet of the Pollution Curse can take an action to pull a creature or object out of the curse. Doing so requires a successful {@dc 12} Strength check, and the creature making the attempt takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) acid damage.

trait.name: Famous Name
entries: Whenever The Pollution Curse rolls initiative, they may force every hostile creature that can see them to make a {@dc 20} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they are {@condition frightened} of it for one minute. They can reroll this saving throw in the beginning of their turns to end the condition early. On a success, they are immune to this feature for 24 hours.

action.name: Sludge Bomb
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 25 ft, one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 2d12 + 3}) Acid damage.

action.name: Acidic Slap
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}32 ({@damage 4d12 + 4}) Acid damage.

action.name: Engulf (4 Cursed Energy)
entries: The Pollution Curse moves up to its speed. While doing so, it can enter Large or smaller creatures' spaces. Whenever the cube enters a creature's space, the creature must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity saving throw. On a successful save, the creature can choose to be pushed 5 feet back or to the side of the Pollution Curse. A creature that chooses not to be pushed suffers the consequences of a failed saving throw. On a failed save, the Pollution Curse enters the creature's space, and the creature takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) acid damage and is engulfed. The engulfed creature can't breathe, is {@condition restrained}, and takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) acid damage at the start of each of the cube's turns. When the cube moves, the engulfed creature moves with it.
An engulfed creature can try to escape by taking an action to make a {@dc 15} Strength check. On a success, the creature escapes and enters a space of its choice within 5 feet of the cube.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 9 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: The curse takes the Dodge action.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the Pollution Curse uses its Regeneration.

reaction.name: Acid Outburst
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the Pollution Curse can spend up to 10 cursed energy to make all creatures within a 20 ft. sphere centered on itself to make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw. All who fail suffer Xd6 Acid damage, where X equals the amount of cursed energy spent. If a creature succeeds on the save, they take half damage.

hp.special: (the number of cursed spirits in the swarm x 30)

trait.name: Quantity over Quality
entries: For every 30 hit points the swarm loses, a random cursed spirit dies (can be the DM's choice).

trait.name: Cursed Attack
entries: The curse's attacks are considered magical.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: You are entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning you cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see you. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see you.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Medium Cursed Spirit. The swarm cannot regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Crowd Control Vulnerability
entries: The swarm has disadvantage in saving throws against effects that cover an area of effect, such as a cone, circle or line. Additionally, any damage caused to the swarm by such effetcs is tripled if the entire area is covered by the swarm.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The swarm makes two Overwhelming Pressure attacks.

action.name: Spacing Issues
entries: As the swarm is too big, not all curses composing it can reach a single target, so the swarm causes less damage the smaller a creature is. A creature takes 1 dice less damage for every size they're smaller than Medium, minimum 1. A creature takes 1 dice extra of damage for every size they are larger than Medium.

action.name: Overwhelming Pressure
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, targets must be within 5 ft. of the swarm or in its space, hits as many targets as possible. {@h}43 ({@damage 8d10 + 7}) bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The Turtle Curse has 28 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: The Turtle Curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns.

trait.name: Cursed Attack
entries: The Turtle Curse's attacks are considered magical.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The Turtle Curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it is {@condition invisible}. Creatures with levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who can see into the Ethereal Plane can see the Turtle Curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see you.

trait.name: Ambush Predator
entries: When the Turtle Curse attacks a creature it is hiding from it deals an additional damage die.

trait.name: A Turtles Bane
entries: The Turtle Curse, as all turtles do, has trouble flipping over from its back. Creatures can try to flip the Turtle Curse over by attempting a contested Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check against it. If the Turtle Curse wins, nothing happens, but if the creature wins the Turtle Curse is flipped over and knocked {@condition prone}. If it is knocked {@condition prone} this way it cannot stand up normally, and must make a successful Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, with the DC being the number that was rolled to flip it over. As long as it is flipped over it cannot use Shell Slam.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: The Turtle Curse reduces all damage it takes but psychic or thunder by 6. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this.

trait.name: Human Wall
entries: The Turtle Curse has advantage in Constitution saving throws.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Turtle Curse makes 2 Claw attacks.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 1d12 + 6}) slashing damage.

action.name: Snap
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 2d12 + 6}) piercing damage.

action.name: Shell Defense
entries: The Turtle Curse retreats into its shell. While it's inside its shell, its AC increases by 4, it gains advantage on Strength saving throws, its speed becomes 0 and it gains disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws. While inside its shell, it can only use Snap, Regeneration, Human Wall and a bonus action to exit its shell.

action.name: Shell Slam (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: The Turtle Curse slams itself and its shell into the ground. Each creature within 15 feet of the Turtle Curse must make a {@dc 18} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone} and take 38 ({@damage 7d10}) bludgeoning damage. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}.

bonus.name: Regeneration (1-7 Cursed Energy)
entries: The Turtle Curse can regain hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

reaction.name: Human Wall (6/Long rest)
entries: Upon hitting 0 hit points, the Turtle Curse rolls a hit die. It regains the amount rolled in hit points.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: When taking damage, the Turtle Curse may use its Regeneration.

reaction.name: Shell Defense
entries: When taking damage, the Turtle Curse retreats into its shell.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The Finger Bearer is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see the Finger Bearer.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The Finger Bearer has 96 Cursed Energy. Any cursed energy used is spent, with the Finger Bearer only regaining these points after it takes a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Attacks
entries: The Finger Bearer's attacks are considered magical.

trait.name: Improved Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: While in combat, the Finger Bearer regains 3 Cursed Energy per turn, up to it's maximum.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The Finger Bearer deals twice as much damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Finger Bearer makes 2 attacks with its unarmed strikes or claw.

action.name: Unarmed Strikes
entries: {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) Slashing Damage + 6 ({@damage 1d12}) force damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) Slashing Damage.

action.name: Cursed Energy Ray
entries: As an action the Finger Bearer can spend up to 6 cursed energy, you can launch a cursed energy ball in a line of 30ft. Once it reaches the end of the line, it will explode in a 15ft. circle, making every creature in that range make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 6 ({@dice 1d12}) + 6 ({@dice 1d12}) per cursed energy spent in force damage, and half as much on a success.

action.name: Cursed Energy Explosion
entries: As an action the Finger Bearer can spend up to 6 cursed energy, forcing every creature within a 30ft circle of them to make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure they take 6 ({@dice 1d12}) + 6 ({@dice 1d12}) per cursed energy spent in force damage and are knocked back to the edges of the circle. They take half as much damage on a success and are not knocked back.

action.name: Innate Domain
entries: As a action for 5 cursed energy, the Finger Bearer fills all the space their in with their innate domain for 1 minute. While inside, all creatures but the Finger Bearer have disadvantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks to locate themselves. The Finger Bearer can at will open a portal in front of himself or of a creature currently moving in that space. The creature must make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw or pass through the portal and be moved to another place inside the innate domain of the Finger Bearer's choice.

bonus.name: Advanced Regeneration
entries: As a bonus action or as a reaction to receiving damage, the Finger Bearer can spend any amount of cursed energy and regain hit points equal to 15 times the amount spent.

bonus.name: Cursed Strike
entries: Before they make an unarmed strike, the Finger Bearer may spend up to 4 cursed energy, dealing additional force damage equal to 4 ({@dice 1d8}) per point spent. They can also use this in response to rolling a natural 20 on an attack roll with an unarmed strike.

bonus.name: Cursed Blast of Blows
entries: Immediately after taking the Attack action on their turn, the Finger Bearer can spend 3 cursed energy to perform two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense
entries: The Finger Bearer can spend 3 cursed energy to take the Dodge action as a bonus action on her turn.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step
entries: The Finger Bearer can spend 3 cursed energy to take the Disengage or Dash action as a bonus action on her turn, and her jump distance is doubled until the end of her turn.

reaction.name: Advanced Regeneration
entries: As response to taking damage, the Finger Bearer uses its Advanced Regeneration feature.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The curse has 10 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

entries: The curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns.

entries: The curse's attacks are considered magical.
