entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it is {@condition invisible}. Creatures with levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who can see into the Ethereal Plane can see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see you.

trait.name: Phasing
entries: Due to being composed of a smaller amount of cursed energy, the curse may walk through objects and structures as if they weren't there.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its claws.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) slashing damage.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 4 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: Nobara has 16 cursed energy that she can use to fuel her abilities. All of her cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Unarmored Movement
entries: Nobara can move and run along vertical surfaces and across liquids on her turn without falling during the move.

trait.name: Cursed Strike (1-2 Cursed Energy)
entries: Nobara can, when making an Unarmed Strike, add {@damage 1d6} force damage to her attack per Cursed Energy spent. Nobara can also use this in response to rolling a natural 20 on an attack roll with an unarmed strike instead of beforehand. On a miss, the energy used remains until she hits a target or until 1 minute passes.
Nobara can use this feature 2 times per round.

trait.name: Cursed Armor (1-2 Cursed Energy)
entries: Nobara can give herself 5 temporary hit points per energy spent for 1 minute. Nobara can only benefit from this feature again after 1 minute or having her temporary hit points reduced to 0.

trait.name: Curse-Empowered Strikes
entries: Nobara deals an additional 4 ({@damage 1d8}) force damage whenever she attacks with weapons (already applied). Additionally, her attacks with weapons are considered magical.

trait.name: Nail Projectiles (1 Cursed Energy, S-1)
entries: Whenever Nobara would make an unarmed strike, she may spend 1 Cursed Energy to instead hold a nail up in the air with her cursed energy and then hit it with her hammer, making a ranged cursed energy attack roll with a range of 30/120 ft. This may also be a Melee Cursed Energy Attack Roll while Nobara is within 5 ft of her target. On a hit, she deals 6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage and the nail is stuck on the target. She spends the nail hitting or missing her target.

A creature may also remove a nail from their body themselves by making a {@dc 13} Strength check against as a free action. On a success, the nail is removed. Additionally, Nobara has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks against creatures that have one of her nails pierced into them, and they cannot hide from her as her cursed energy resonates from them.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: Nobara has 2 reactions per round.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: Nobara will reduce all damage she takes but psychic by 2. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this, unless she's under the effects of Cursed Armor.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Grade 4 Hammer
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 2} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@dice 1d6}) bludgeoning and 10 ({@damage 1d8 + 1d4 + 3}) force damage.

action.name: Resonance (5 Cursed Energy, S-1)
entries: After Nobara has mixed a part into her straw doll, she may activate resonance, enabling her to target the straw doll giving heraccess to a special attack. Using one nail and her hammer she can drive the nail through the body part. When Nobara deals damage to the doll the target is forced to make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they will receive force damage based on what part of them is mixed in and suffer the effect, as shown in the table below. On a success, they take half as much damage and do not suffer the effect.
This damage overcomes resistances and immunities and is considered Minor Soul Harming.

Resonance Strength	Part Used	Effect	Damage Dice Weak	Hair, skin, blood	Target's speed is halved until the end of their next turn	{@dice 2d6} Fine	Small parts or digits such as fingers, small amounts of flesh.	The target is knocked {@condition prone}.	{@dice 5d8} Strong	Limbs like arms, legs, or vital organs	The creature is {@condition stunned} until the start of their next turn.	{@dice 8d10} All stronger effects carry special effects of the ones weaker than them, keeping the duration of the stronger effect.

Nobara is also able to use Resonance with other items as the medium instead of the doll. This only works in very unique circumstances. For example, when a creature has a clone or their technique is infused into her blood. What is allowed to act as a medium and what strength that item would be, are both determined by the DM.

Severed body parts also are only able to be used for this technique if they hold a connection to the target. If for any reason the DM determines that the part you have has lost this connection, the technique cannot be used.
Nobara is also able to use Resonance on objects. She can instead infuse part of an object into her doll and use this technique on it. This causes the object to take the damage.

bonus.name: Martial Arts
entries: When Nobara uses the Attack action with an unarmed strike on her turn, she can make one unarmed strike or attack with a one-handed weapon.

bonus.name: Cursed Weapon Enhancement (1-2 Cursed Energy)
entries: Nobara can add cursed energy to a melee weapon or improvised weapon she is wielding for 1 minute. Whenever she hits a target with the weapon, it takes extra force damage equal to the amount spent. Nobara can only use this enhancement again after 1 minute has passed. Other creatures cannot use Nobara's Cursed Weapon Enhancement, and must use their own. The duration remains while Nobara isn't wielding the weapon, in case she is going to wield it again.

bonus.name: Cursed Blast of Blows (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Immediately after taking the Attack action on her turn, Nobara can perform two unarmed strikes.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Nobara can take the Dodge action.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Nobara can take the Disengage or Dash action on her turn, and her jump distance is doubled until the end of her turn.

bonus.name: Nail Explosion (1-3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Nobara may explode a number of nails equal to the amount spent that are within 100 feet of her. If the nail is imbedded into a creature, that creature must make a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take 3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing damage per nail embedded. On a success, they take half as much damage.

bonus.name: Resonance Part Acquisition (S-1)
entries: Nobara may grab and mix a removed limb into her straw doll to achieve a strong resonance.

reaction.name: Resonance Part Acquisition (S-1)
entries: When a creature takes slashing or piercing damage within her reach, Nobara may make a Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check against the enemy's AC(+3 for weak, +6 for fine). If Nobara rolls high enough, she can grab a part of the enemy of the highest resonance strength she qualifies for and mix it with her straw doll.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: Sukuna has 498 cursed energy that he can use to fuel his abilities. All of his cursed energy is replenished at the end of a short or long rest.

trait.name: Enhanced Cursed Energy Regeneration
entries: Sukuna regains 5 cursed energy at the beginning of his turns. He will also gain 1 Cursed Energy per minute outside of combat and doing light activity.

trait.name: Blessing of The Twin
entries: Sukuna has advantage on {@skill Intimidation} and {@skill Perception} checks. In addition, he may use Chanting as a Free Action and Complex Chanting as an Action. Additionally, his extra appendages grant him the following bonuses: Sukuna gains an additional Bonus Action on his turn that may only be used to grapple or shove a creature. When Sukuna takes the Attack action on his turn, he can make an additional attack as a part of the action. This additional attack can only be a melee attack or a cursed technique feature. When Sukuna uses his cursed technique, he can choose to reduce its action cost by 1. (Ex. Full Round Action -> Action -> Bonus Action -> Free Action) If this would reduce the action cost of a reaction he gains 1 additional reaction only for using said reaction.

trait.name: Sukuna's Fingers
entries: Sukuna's Fingers are all the Special Grade Cursed Objects that make up Sukuna's soul. These fingers greatly affect his Cursed Energy Reserves and Output. For the purposes of his Cursed Technique that scale with level, his level is treated as being equal to the number of fingers he has consumed. The amount of Cursed Energy he can spend on his Cursed Energy Enhancements and Cursed Energy Reinforcement is equal to half the numbers of fingers he has consumed (rounded down, to a minimum of 1). The amount he may spend on the Reverse Cursed Technique is equal to four times the number of fingers he has consumed (Once 10 fingers have been consumed this limit no longer applies). Additionally, each finger adds 5 to his max Cursed Energy. Design Note: This sheet is being calculated as if Sukuna has ingested 20 fingers. Feel free to scale for himself accordingly. In addition, he does not have the benefits of Blessing of the Twin while he is not in his real body as all of the benefits granted by this come from his extra appendages.

trait.name: Almost a Doctor
entries: Whenever Sukuna makes a {@skill Medicine} check to stabilize someone and succeed, the creature regains 1 hit point.

trait.name: Cursed Armor
entries: Sukuna can spend up to 11 cursed energy to give himself temporary hit points equal to the cursed energy spent times 20 for 1 minute. Sukuna can only benefit from this feature again after 1 minute or having his temporary hit points reduced to 0 (No action required). Sukuna gains damage resistance to 2 damage types when this armor is used. These damage resistances may be changed on a long rest or once per round as a bonus action or reaction to himself using Cursed Armor. He gains 18 temporary hit points at the beginning of his turns while his cursed armor is active.

trait.name: Cursed Strikes
entries: Sukuna can spend up to 10 cursed energy when making an Unarmed Strike in order to add {@damage 2d8} force damage to his attack per Cursed Energy spent. He can also use this in response to rolling a natural 20 on an attack roll with an unarmed strike instead of beforehand. On a miss, the energy used remains until he hits a target or until 1 minute passes. Sukuna's cursed strikes also deal an additional 11 damage. This bonus can be doubled by spending 4 Cursed Energy.

trait.name: Curse-Empowered Strikes
entries: Sukuna's Unarmed strikes and attacks with weapons are considered magical for the purposes of overcoming resistances, and deal {@dice 2d12} extra force damage (already included).

trait.name: Otherwordly Efficiency
entries: Every time Sukuna spends cursed energy in a feature it counts as if he has spent twice as much, however the maximum amount he can reach remains the same. Additionally, Sukuna reduces all cursed energy he spends by 9 (to a minimum of 1).

trait.name: Perfect Body
entries: Sukuna is immune to disease and whenever he would make a Constitution saving throw or check he can spend 1 Cursed Energy to give himself advantage on the roll.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Tracker
entries: Sukuna may make a {@skill Perception} check, adding his Charisma modifier, to see if there are any remains of cursed energy in the place he's at, and if he succeed he knows exactly who the energy is from. In case he does not know who the person is, then he can only track down where the cursed energy trail leads him to. The DC is equal to the cursed energy DC of the creature he's trying to track. Tracking residual cursed energy may not always lead all the way to the person, with the DM determining how the residual trail goes. Usage of a cursed technique or cursed energy fueled feature will often leave much more residuals than normal. Sukuna knows when any creature he can see spends cursed energy and how much they spend when doing so. Additionally, as a free action he can focus on a creature for 1 minute, seeing the "sparks" of cursed energy that always happen before cursed technique usage. For that minute when that creature forces him to make a save using their cursed technique or makes a cursed energy attack roll against him using their cursed technique they must roll {@skill Stealth}, using Charisma instead of Dexterity, against his {@skill Perception}. If he wins, he gets advantage on the saving throw, or the attack roll is made with disadvantage. Sukuna can focus on a 5 creatures with this feature at once.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Concealer
entries: Anytime Sukuna takes the Hide action, he can attempt to conceal his cursed energy by making a {@skill Stealth} check. For the next 10 minutes, wherever he passes through he will not leave his cursed energy tracks behind carelessly, so any person that tries to track or sense his remains will need to be successful on a contested check against what he rolled + 10, if they have any ability or feature that would allow them to track his energy, and if not they can't track him at all. While both Sukuna and his Cursed Energy are hidden, the benefit of being an Unseen Attacker also extends to his cursed energy saving throws, forcing any creatures affected to roll their saving throw with disadvantage.

trait.name: Negative Flow/Focused Rage
entries: Whenever Sukuna is under the effects of the conditions {@condition frightened}, berserk, bloodlusted, are under half his maximum hit points, missing a limb lost in this encounter, or in an encounter that's total CR is higher than 30; he will gain 5 temporary cursed energy at the start of his turn which cannot go above 1/4th of his cursed energy maximum. Additionally, he can begin {@status concentration||concentrating} to increase the temporary cursed energy gained at the start of his turn to 10. When the circumstances listed end, he can no longer concentrate on this feature. (This feature can be concentrated on even while under the bloodlust or berserk conditions)

trait.name: Dismantle Maneuvers
entries: Sukuna can execute the following Dismantle Maneuver: False Slash. As part of using a dismantle attack, Sukuna may choose to automatically miss. In return, creatures targeted must make an {@dc 27} {@skill Insight} check. On a failure, he gains advantage on his next melee attack on his current turn. Creatures with the Cursed Energy Tracker feat have advantage on this insight check, and creatures who have no knowledge of dismantle are unaffected by this maneuver. Hand of Slashing. As a free action for 4 cursed energy, Sukuna coat his hands in small slashes until the beginning of his next turn. For the duration, once per round as a reaction, he may try to grab a melee weapon attack that was made against him. He must make a Dexterity({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check against the attack roll. If his roll is higher than the attack roll, the attack is negated. If they attack with a natural weapon or unarmed strikes, they take 15 slashing damage. Beyond Their Limit. Whenever Sukuna makes a creature regain hit points with reverse cursed technique, he may spend 5 additional Cursed Energy to make said creature feel a surge of energy in their bodies. They will gain the following benefits for 1 minute: Their movement speed increases by 10ft. They can do an extra bonus action. They will gain resistance to all damage but psychic. Their damage rolls will always count as if they have spent the maximum amount of cursed energy on Improved Output. After the sudden surge ends, they must make a {@dc 25} Constitution saving throw. On a failure their body will suffer backlash from the energy gained, making them gain 5 levels of {@condition exhaustion}. On a success, their body does not suffer as much but will still receive 1 level of {@condition exhaustion}.

trait.name: Master of Manipulation
entries: When Sukuna uses any features that use cursed energy, he may choose for their action cost to be reduced by 1(full round action->action->bonus action->free action->he gains one additional free action just for using said feature). If this would reduce the action cost of a reaction, he gains 1 additional reaction only for using said reaction. Additionally, Sukuna can spend up to 10 cursed energy whenever he's using a cursed energy feature that's not a cursed technique to increase its DC by the amount spent.

trait.name: Speed Supremacy
entries: Once per turn, when Sukuna takes an action, bonus action, or reaction, or uses his movement, he can blind his enemies in a quick burst of speed. The feature used will require twice as many reactions to react to. If a creature also possesses this feature, they can expend their use of it for the round to make reactions function as normal. If a feature or action allows him to make multiple attacks or force multiple saving throws, only one of them will receive the benefit of speed supremacy. Additionally, Sukuna gains one additional reaction per round.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: Sukuna has two reactions per round.

trait.name: Untraceable Speed(9/Round)
entries: Whenever Sukuna takes a reaction, he may move up to 100 feet as a part of the reaction. This movement cannot be used to exit domains.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: Sukuna reduces all non-magic damage he takes but psychic by 10. This also affects magical damage if he is using cursed armor.

trait.name: Titanic Strength
entries: Sukuna may add half his proficiency bonus to Strength saving throws. He counts as three size larger for the purposes of grappling, shoving, disarming and the weight he can carry, push, drag, or lift.

trait.name: Unmatched Fortitude
entries: Sukuna may add half his proficiency bonus to Constitution saving throws. He has advantage on the saving throws made to maintain his Domain Expansion in an equal Domain Clash and saving throws made to keep {@status concentration||concentrating} on opening a hole with his domain. Finally, he reduces all damage he takes by 38.

trait.name: Taijutsu Sorcery
entries: Once per turn, Sukuna can make an unarmed strike with advantage. If he already had advantage on a unarmed strike, he raises the critical hit range for the attack by 1.

trait.name: Cursed Weapon Wielder
entries: If Sukuna rolls a natural 20 on the attack roll for a Cursed Tool, he can make one additional weapon attack with advantage as apart of his attack using the same Cursed Tool that he scored the critical hit with. The additional weapon attack provided by this feature cannot be used to trigger another additional if he would score a natural 20 on the attack roll for the attack.

trait.name: Brute Slam
entries: Once per turn as part of an unarmed strike, Sukuna may make a grapple attempt against a creature. If he succeeds, he may pick up the creature and slam them against a solid surface within 10 ft. of himself, such as the ground or a wall, dealing another roll of his unarmed strike in damage. The surface takes twice as much damage as the creature, and if it breaks the creature takes another {@damage 2d6} damage of an appropriate type.

trait.name: Heavy Slam
entries: Whenever Sukuna takes the Attack action against a creature he is currently grappling with, he may forgo an attack to violently slam the creature into the ground. He makes a contested Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check against the creature who contests via Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check (Their choice). On a successful check, his opponent is driven into the earth beneath himself ending his grapple early, taking bludgeoning damage equal to two rolls of Sukuna's unarmed strike damage dice and being knocked {@condition prone}. On a failure, the creature escapes from his iron grasp, causing his grapple to end early and the creature may move an additional 5 ft. away from Sukuna as part of their successful check. If he scores a 20 on the Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check whilst using this feature and it was a success, the creature takes bludgeoning damage equal to four rolls of his unarmed strike damage dice and gains disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks, skill checks and Strength or Dexterity saving throws until the start of his next turn.

trait.name: Heavy Finisher
entries: Once per round as part of hitting a melee attack, Sukuna can spend up to 10 cursed energy to make his target make a {@dc 27} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they take {@damage 1d10} force or bludgeoning damage per energy spent and are thrown back 20 times his Strength modifier + the amount of energy spent and are knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, they take half as much damage, are half as pushed and are not knocked {@condition prone}. If the creature hits an object on the way they both take {@damage 1d12} bludgeoning damage. If it hits a structure, it stops completely and takes {@damage 1d12} bludgeoning damage per 10 feet left to travel, with the structure taking twice as much damage. If it hits another creature, the creature hit must make a Dexterity saving throw or also take the damage of the Heavy Finisher and be pushed as well. Sukuna can also use the heavy finisher as a way to get someone stuck in the ground. Once per round as part of hitting an melee attack against a {@condition prone} creature, Sukuna can spend up to 10 cursed energy to force his target to make a {@dc 27} Strength saving throw. On a failure, they take {@damage 1d10} force or bludgeoning damage per energy spent and are {@condition restrained} on the ground. On a success, they take half as much damage and their speed is halved until the beginning of Sukuna's next turn. If they fail by 5 or more, they are also Confused until the beginning of his next turn. They may remake the Strength saving throw at the beginning of their turns, ending the condition early.

trait.name: Acrobatic Fighter
entries: Whenever Sukuna makes an unarmed strike while within 5 ft. of a solid surface or another object like a pole, he may use it to leverage a powerful attack against his opponent, dealing an additional damage die of his unarmed strike on a hit. If there are not any solid surfaces, Sukuna may use half of his movement speed to drop to the ground on his hands, extending his legs up and making a powerful kick. Also, once per turn, whenever Sukuna falls onto a creature, he can make a {@dc 10} {@skill Acrobatics} check. On a success, he may make one unarmed strike against that creature.

trait.name: Air Jump (1 Cursed Energy)
entries: With no action required, Sukuna may perform a high or long jump multiplied by 4 on his turn while he is in the air. This jump counts as if he had a running start.

trait.name: Improved Cursed Energy Output
entries: As apart of any feature which uses cursed energy, Sukuna can expend cursed energy equal to half of the feature's original cost to amplify its output, granting it one of the following benefits: The feature's number of damage dice increases by 5. The feature's range increases by 5 times 11 in feet. The feature's bonus to hit is increased by +2. The feature's DC increases by +2. The feature's duration increases by 6 rounds.

trait.name: Legendary Cursed Energy Output
entries: If Sukuna uses the Improved Cursed Energy Output and uses either Maximum Output or Chanting on a feature, its total cursed energy cost will now be reduced by 15. This cursed energy reduction does not apply for the purposes of the Flawless Fundamentals feat.

trait.name: Divine Cursed Energy Output
entries: As a part of any feature that uses cursed energy, Sukuna can expend 8 cursed energy to drastically alter its functions as part of the same action he uses the feature with. If the feature was a Cursed Energy Attack roll, the target of his attack or attack rolls has their AC treated as if it had been reduced by 5. If the feature was a Cursed Energy save, Sukuna may change the saving throw to another depending on its original save. The changes are the following below: Original Saving Throw - Changed Saving Throw Strength - Dexterity Dexterity - Constitution Constitution - Strength Intelligence - Wisdom Wisdom - Intelligence.

trait.name: Flawless Fundamentals
entries: Sukuna reduces the cost of all cursed energy features by 18. If one of Sukuna's class features is reduced by double its original cost, the feature's cost instead becomes 0. This feature's cost can be treated as 0 up to 22 times per long rest. Reverse Cursed Technique Mastery Sukuna has a healing pool of 4500 that he can use with his reverse cursed energy. If he is suffering from a physical effect injury such as a concussion or something similar, one of these effects can be healed for every 10 hit points healed with reverse cursed technique. If he is not using the Limb System variant rule, Sukuna regrows back limbs for every 20 hit points he regains. Sukuna can use his RCT until his healing pool ends before getting burnout, during which he can't use RCT until his healing pool recovers. At the beginning of his turns while in a combat where his life is at risk, he regains 15 hit points of his healing pool back. His healing pool automatically goes back to full at the end of a short rest. Whenever Sukuna makes a creature regain hit points, they will regain half as many hit points as temporary hit points. They cannot gain temporary hit points from this feature again until the current temporary hit points vanish. For every 10 Cursed Energy spent on the Reverse Cursed Technique, Sukuna can choose to end one of the conditions currently affecting the target, or reduce one level of {@condition exhaustion}.

trait.name: Improved Output
entries: Sukuna may use his Reverse Cursed Technique on others, though the amount he heals other creatures is halved. Additionally, whenever he deals damage to a Cursed Spirit with an Unarmed Strike or a Natural weapon, he may spend up to 11 Cursed Energy to deal 5 radiant damage per 1 Cursed Energy spent. The total amount of radiant damage is subtracted from his healing pool.

trait.name: Power Style
entries: Whenever a feature increases the damage die tier of Sukuna's unarmed strikes, it also does so for melee weapons he is holding (already applied).

trait.name: Sure Striking Blade (8 Cursed Energy)
entries: Whenever Sukuna misses a melee weapon attack by 3 or less, he can spend 8 cursed energy to redirect the blade and actually hit the target.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance(5/Day)
entries: If Ryomen Sukuna fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Sukuna may make 4 attacks. Only 1 of these attacks can be Lightning Blast.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 24} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}36 ({@damage 4d12 + 10}) Bludgeoning damage and 18 ({@damage 2d12 + 5}) force damage. If Sukuna has 0 Cursed Energy, he instead has a {@hit 19} to hit and the force damage decreases to 13 ({@dice 2d12}).

action.name: Supreme Martial Solution
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 28} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}66 ({@damage 8d12 + 14}) Slashing or Piercing damage and 34 ({@dice 1d12 + 15}{@dice  + 2d12}) force damage. If Sukuna has 0 Cursed Energy, he instead has a {@hit 23} to hit and the force damage decreases to 29 ({@dice 1d12 + 10}{@dice  + 2d12}). This attack has its critical hit range increased by 1.

action.name: Hiten
entries: Melee Weapon Attack. {@hit 28} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}70 ({@damage 16d6 + 14}) Slashing or Piercing damage and 35 ({@damage 2d6 + 2d12 + 15}) force damage. If Sukuna has 0 Cursed Energy, he instead has a {@hit 23} to hit and the force damage decreases to 30 ({@dice 2d6 + 2d12 + 10}). This attack has its critical hit range increased by 1.

action.name: Lightning Blast (10 Cursed Energy)
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 28} to hit, range 60/300 ft., one target. {@h}97 ({@damage 15d12}) Lightning damage. This attack may only be used while Sukuna is wielding the Special Grade Cursed Tool. If Sukuna has 0 Cursed Energy, he instead has a {@hit 23} to hit. This attack has its critical hit range increased by 1. If this attack specifically targets Hiten's cloud, every person within 60 feet must make a {@dc 27} Dexterity saving throw and taking 165 ({@damage 30d12}) lightning damage in a failure. Half as much on a success. This can only be done once per long rest.

action.name: Perfect Stance (8 Cursed Energy)
entries: Sukuna can enter a stance of perfect offense and defense as an action. Until the beginning of his next turn, whenever a creature enters within his weapon's reach he may make an opportunity attack without spending his reaction. If he hits said attack, the creature has disadvantage on melee weapon attacks against him until the end of their turn. He can only make an attack like this once per creature per usage of this technique.

action.name: Dismantle (1+ Cursed Energy)
entries: When taking the Attack Action, Sukuna can spend up to 31 Cursed Energy to replace one of his attacks with a cursed energy attack roll against a creature within 320 ft of him. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d12} slashing damage per cursed energy spent in the attack and must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they are bleeding. On a success, they receive no additional conditions. This attack roll is made with advantage if the target cannot see {@condition invisible} objects. Multiple attacks may be replaced with dismantle, but cursed energy may be spent for each attack. This cannot replace attacks from features such as Cursed Blast of Blows. He may also choose to make three ranged cursed energy attack rolls or only one for each attack replaced. He may make three in case he aims for two or more targets, with each dismantle dealing a third of the dice of the normal dismantle (rounded down) slashing damage. A creature needs blindsight to see the slash.

action.name: Grid Slash (1+ Cursed Energy)
entries: As part of using Dismantle, Sukuna can spend up to an additional 31 Cursed Energy to force all creatures in a 10 times the amount spent foot long, 30 foot wide and 30 foot tall line starting from him to make a {@dc 27} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take his dismantle's damage or half as much on a success.

action.name: World-Cutting Slash (35-51 Cursed Energy (Total 36-82 Cursed Energy))
entries: As an action, while having at least two hands free, Sukuna is able to fire off a modified Dismantle. Sukuna can spend up to an additional 51 cursed energy (minimum of 35) on this dismantle. For every cursed energy spent, the damage increases by {@damage 5d12} slashing damage. Additionally, there are 2 ways he can fire this dismantle. He can have it cut vertically or horizontally. When used horizontally, all creatures in a cone with a range of 5 feet times the amount of cursed energy spent, will be forced to make a {@dc 27} Dexterity saving throw. When used vertically, all creatures in a 10 foot wide, 5 times the amount spent feet tall, and 5 times the amount spent long line, will be forced to make a {@dc 27} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, creatures take the damage based on the cursed energy spent. On a success, they take half as much damage. This technique counts any enhancements to dismantle towards it. If a target is reduced to 0 hit points by this technique, they are killed outright. This Dexterity saving throw ignores Evasion. Additionally, due to the complicated nature of this technique, Sukuna must first use a bonus action to form the hand sign for his domain with two free hands. Afterwards, he is able to use this technique. Due to the fact that this technique cuts space itself, it bypasses all external defenses such as Infinity, Simple Domain, etc. Additionally, it bypasses all AC bonuses, damage reductions, or temporary hit points from spells or cursed techniques such as Cursed Armor. If the DM has this technique hit a limb, it will only damage the limb itself and not the primary hit points of the creature. Design Note: If the Rapid Technique Activation binding vow is active, Sukuna cannot use World-Cutting Slash unless the dismantle it is used on is also under the effects of Chanting and he has at least three hands free. Additionally, he must use an action to make an attack with a Dismantle with the World-Cutting Slash applied to it.

action.name: Cleave (1+ Cursed Energy)
entries: As an Action, or replacing an attack as part of the Attack Action if Sukuna targets a creature he is grappling or an object, for up to 41 Cursed Energy he makes a melee weapon attack roll against a creature. On a hit, they take 12 slashing damage per cursed energy spent and must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they are bleeding. On a success, they receive no additional conditions. The cursed energy is not spent if he misses. This feature adapts its output to attempt to cut the target down in one fell swoop. When Sukuna spends cursed energy on this feature, he will learn how much cursed energy he needs to spend for the average damage of his cleave to reduce the target to 0 hit points from their current hit points, taking into account temporary hit points, damage reductions and thresholds, resistances and immunities, and any other feature which would directly affect the outcome. He will be told the maximum amount if he cannot reduce them to 0 hit points from their current amount by this calculation. It can bypass cursed armor by expending a higher cursed energy cost during casting that that of the targets cursed armor (E.g. If he fires a Cleave for 5 Cursed energy and the target has spent 4 cursed energy on cursed armor his cleave will deal damage to them as if it were cast at 1 cursed energy ignoring their cursed armor and nullifying it). If a creature is reduced to 0 hit points by this feature, they are cleaved into various small pieces. He needs blindsight to see the slash. If Sukuna uses Power Increase or Specialized Technique to increase the damage dice, each dice added will instead count as another cursed energy spent. (Ex. Specialized Technique adding 5 damage dice would count as 5 more cursed energy spent, and add 60 slashing damage.)

action.name: Spiderweb (1+ Cursed Energy)
entries: As part of using Cleave on an object, Sukuna can spend up to 41 Cursed Energy to turn the ground in a 10-times-the-amount-spent-foot-radius circle into difficult terrain. All creatures within range must make a {@dc 27} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they are knocked {@condition prone} and are pushed 5-times-the-amount-spent-feet away from him. On a success, they take half as much damage and are not pushed. Ground and structures targeted take maximum slashing damage.

action.name: Furnace Open (1+ Cursed Energy)
entries: As an Action, and for up to 51 Cursed Energy, Sukuna can make a ranged cursed energy attack roll against one creature within 20 times the amount spent feet or force every creature within 5 ft of the target to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a hit or a failure, they will take {@damage 4d12} fire damage per energy spent and receive Second Degree burns. On a success, they take half as much damage but receive First Degree burns. If they have Evasion, they do not receive effects on a success. If they fail or he hits by 5 or more, they receive Third Degree burns instead. If Open reduces a creature to 0 hit points, it kills it outright by desintegrating it. Open deals twice as much damage to objects and structures. Also, Open ignores resistance to fire damage. Inside his domain expansion, Open now targets all creatures within his domain except himself, and deals its damage in bludgeoning damage as a second instance of damage to any creature it hit as long as the domain its used within has a barrier that stops at least non-living things from leaving. This bludgeoning damage ignores resistance and kills a creature outright if they're reduced to 0 hit points by it. Whenever Dismantle hits a creature or an object Sukuna gains 1 Preparation Counter, and whenever Cleave hits a creature or object he gains 2. He must spend 100 Preparation Counters to use furnace: Open.

action.name: Exorcise
entries: Sukuna can automatically destroy a cursed spirit within 30 feet of himself with a CR of 4 or lower that he can see.

action.name: Resurrection (25/35 Cursed Energy)
entries: As an action for 25 cursed energy and 1600 hit points from Sukuna's reverse cursed technique pool, he can touch a creature that has died within 10 rounds, restoring them to life, and healing all wounds that afflicts the creature. This cannot be used to heal wounds beyond the limit of what is possible to heal with the Improved Output feat, such as lost limbs and injuries beyond that which can be healed with Reverse Cursed Technique. This causes the creature to come back to life with 0 hit points and will be considered stable. This cannot be used on a Cursed Spirit or a creature that cannot ordinarily be healed with the Reverse Cursed Technique. Additionally, Sukuna can use this to heal his body and revive himself from death while still in the window of his Reverse Cursed Technique. If he has died within 20 hours, he may spend 35 cursed energy and 1200 hit points from his reverse cursed technique pool, to heal his wounds and resurrect himself, regaining 1 hit point. He cannot heal himself from wounds that would not ordinarily be able to be healed by the Reverse Cursed Technique, such as a destroyed skull, neck, or torso, his brain being meaningfully destroyed, or being killed in an extreme manner, as determined by the DM.

action.name: Basic Barrier (1+ Cursed Energy)
entries: Sukuna can spend up to 11 cursed energy to make a dome starting from himself with a 5 foot radius and a 10ft. in height. For each cursed energy spent, the barrier's maximum radius and height both increase by 10ft, but Sukuna may choose to make it smaller if he wishes. This barrier has 20 times the amount of cursed energy spent hit points, resistance to all damage from the inside, is vulnerable to all damage from the outside and is immune to Psychic damage. This barrier has an AC of 27 and automatically fails saving throws. This barrier lasts 2 minutes. Sukuna can deactivate this basic barrier at will (no action required).

action.name: Curtain (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Sukuna covers the sky and the ground within a 2165-foot radius with a dome of darkness for 10 minutes. The curtain makes the sky become dark despite the time of day, provoking any cursed spirits out of hiding. Those without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class cannot see anything inside the barrier, merely seeing as if nothing but the vegetation was there. Creatures who can see into the ethereal plane or have levels in the jujutsu sorcerer path can see the black half-sphere. The curtain has 300 hit points. This barrier has an AC of 27 and automatically fails saving throws. It is also immune to Psychic damage. Sukuna can spend any amount of additional cursed energy, adding 1 minute to its duration and +10 to its hit points.

action.name: Hollow Wicker Basket (5 Cursed Energy)
entries: Sukuna surrounds himself with a 15 ft. barrier for 1 minute. Within that radius, the sure hit feature of the domain is negated; creatures of his choice inside of the barrier are unaffected. Additionally the sure hit effect of the domain targets the barrier instead of creatures of his choice within it, the barrier having an amount of hit points equal to 450. If at any point the barrier disappears or breaks anyone who was protected by it (including Sukuna) is immediately struck by the sure hit effect of the domain they're inside of if they're inside one. The barrier has no effect on cursed techniques or other people entering the barrier; it simply negates sure hit features and effects. When Sukuna activates this feat or as a free action while it is active he may choose to continuously maintain the barrier, requiring {@status concentration} but completely negating the sure hit effect of the domain against the barrier. To do so he must also have two free hands and while he is {@status concentration||concentrating} those hands are occupied. Sukuna must also continuously chant to concentrate on this feat, meaning he cannot activate anything that requires him to speak while {@status concentration||concentrating} unless he has some alternative way of speaking (such as a second mouth). If at any point he fails to meet either of these requirements the {@status concentration} ends, causing the barrier to immediately be affected by the sure hit effect.

action.name: Forced Ingestion
entries: Sukuna can choose 1 creature within his reach and make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check contested by their Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. On a success, he forces them to ingest the Cursed Object. On a failure, they are able to avoid being fed the object.

bonus.name: Martial Arts
entries: When Sukuna uses his action to make an unarmed strike, as a bonus action he can make another one or a one-handed weapon attack.

bonus.name: Cursed Blast of Blows
entries: After taking the Attack action on his turn, Sukuna can spend 3 Cursed Energy to perform two unarmed strikes or weapon attacks as a bonus action.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense
entries: Sukuna can spend 3 Cursed Energy to take the Dodge action as a bonus action on his turn.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step
entries: Sukuna can spend 3 Cursed Energy to take the Disengage or Dash action as a bonus action on his turn, and his jump distance is doubled until the end of their turn.

bonus.name: Cursed Weapon Enhancement (1-10 Cursed Energy)
entries: Sukuna can add to a melee weapon he is wielding for 1 minute. Whenever he hits a target with the weapon, it takes extra force damage equal to four times the amount spent. Sukuna can only use this enhancement again after 1 minute has passed. Whenever Sukuna's attacking with a weapon that's been affected by cursed weapon enhancement, his reach increases by 10 feet. Sukuna's Cursed Weapon Enhancement will deal additional damage equal to half of the cursed energy expended, rounded down + 1 (to a maximum of +6), so long as he's wielding the weapon which has the enhancement applied to it. When Sukuna activates Cursed Weapon Enhancement, he may expend 4 additional cursed energy to double this additional damage bonus.

bonus.name: Cursed Smite (10 Cursed Energy)
entries: As a bonus action, Sukuna can spend up to 10 cursed energy and imbue it in one of his weapons. This enhancement lasts for 1 minute or until he hits a creature with the weapon. If he hits the attack, it deals additional d10s of force damage equal to the amount spent.

bonus.name: Slash Skating
entries: As a bonus action for 4 cursed energy, Sukuna can coat his feet with slashes and utilize them to skate around for 1 minute. For the duration, his walking speed is increased 20 ft. and he ignores difficult terrain. He may end this at will (no action required).

bonus.name: Maximum Output
entries: Sukuna can spend Cursed Energy equal to twice the original cost of one of his cursed techniques to amplify its cursed energy output, making it gain two of the following benefits the next time its cast: The technique's range is increased by half its original range rounded to the highest 5. For example, a technique with 10ft. of range would become a 15ft. range technique. This range increase does not work with touch based techniques. The technique's damage dice is increased by half of its original dice rounded up. For example, a technique with {@dice 10d10} die of damage would add {@dice 5d10} die of damage to the total damage die. The technique's bonus to hit is increased by +5. For example, a technique with a {@hit 7} to hit would add a {@hit 4} to hit in its maximum output form. The technique's DC is increased by 5. The technique's duration is increased by half of its original duration. For example, a technique that lasts a minute would last 1 minute and 5 rounds.

bonus.name: Untraceable Speed
entries: Sukuna can take the dash action or make a skill check on a skill he is proficient in.

Protector's Stance As a bonus action for up to 31 cursed energy, Sukuna can harden his skin and enhance his senses for 1 minute. For the duration, Sukuna gains a damage reduction equal to double the amount spent, and his movement speed is halved. Sukuna may end this stance at any time for no action required. Whenever he gets hit by a melee attack, he can make a melee attack against the attacker as a reaction. Whenever Sukuna is hit by a melee attack, he can make a melee attack against the attacker as a reaction. Additionally, for the duration Sukuna gains one additional reaction just to use this feature.

bonus.name: Cursed Energy Tracker
entries: Sukuna may now take the Hide action as a bonus action.

bonus.name: Curse Enhanced Senses (4 Cursed Energy)
entries: As a bonus action, Sukuna can finely hone his five senses, allowing him to add 10 to {@skill Perception} checks for one minute. Outside of combat, this feature will last one hour instead. He may end this feature at will. He can only use this feature again once it has ended. Reverse Cursed Technique Mastery Sukuna spends any amount of Cursed Energy, regaining 30 hit points for each energy he spends, and removing the same amount of healing from his healing pool. Every 1 Cursed Energy Sukuna spends on this feature counts as 2. Hollow Wicker Basket Sukuna activates Hollow Wicker Basket.

bonus.name: Domain Expansion Malevolent Shrine (4 Cursed Energy)
entries: Sukuna opens his Domain Expansion.

bonus.name: Hiten Cloud
entries: When holding Hiten, Sukuna can spend 5 cursed energy to summon a puff of cloud within 60 feet of him. He or someone else can stand in the puff of cloud and gain a flying speed equal to the riders walking speed, but this lasts for a number of rounds equal to Sukuna's Charisma modifier. The cloud takes a free action to mount.

bonus.name: Cursed Object Transformation (20 Cursed Energy)
entries: As a Bonus Action, Sukuna can turn part of his body into a Cursed Object.

reaction.name: Reverse Cursed Technique Mastery When Sukuna receives damage, he may use his Reverse Cursed Technique
entries: Sukuna has 10 extra Reactions specifically for this feature.

reaction.name: Cursed Enhanced Body (1-11 Cursed Energy)
entries: Whenever Sukuna makes a Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, or Wisdom saving throw, he may spend up to 11 Cursed Energy as a reaction to add the number of energy spent as a bonus to the saving throw. He also has an extra reaction specifically for this feature.

reaction.name: Human Wall (10/Long Rest)
entries: When Sukuna would be reduced to 0 hit points, he may instead fall to ({@dice 1d12 + 20}) hit points instead.
Hollow Wicker Basket In response to Sukuna seeing a Domain Expansion open or being affected by a sure-hit, he may use Hollow Wicker Basket.

Taijutsu Sorcery After taking the Attack Action on his turn, Sukuna may take the Dodge Action as a Reaction. This dodge only works against Melee Attacks, and if a creature rolls a Nat 1 on a melee attack against him he may make 1 Melee attack against a creature within range.

reaction.name: Air Jump (1+ Cursed Energy)
entries: Whenever Sukuna takes fall damage, he may use spend up to 21 cursed energy to reduce the damage by that amount times 5.

reaction.name: Weapon Clashing (8 Cursed Energy)
entries: As a reaction to a melee weapon attack hitting Sukuna, he can spend 8 cursed energy to clash with the weapon of the attacker. He must make an attack roll with his weapon. If his roll is higher than that of the attacker, he nullifies their attack.

reaction.name: Redirect Energy (4 Cursed Energy)
entries: As a reaction to a ranged cursed energy attack roll hitting Sukuna or being forced to make a cone or line saving throw, he can redirect the damage with his weapon. He reduces the damage dealt by one roll of his weapon's damage die multiplied by his Charisma modifier (minimum 1). If he reduces it to at least half of the original damage or less, as part of the same reaction he can make a melee weapon attack roll against a creature within range. On a hit, they take additional damage equal to the amount reduced.

reaction.name: Burnout Recovery
entries: Sukuna can spend 8 Cursed Energy and 800 from his healing pool to regain his Cursed Technique and remove the {@condition exhaustion} gained from burning out. Make a {@dc 5} Constitution saving throw. On a success, he regains his technique without major consequences. On a failure, his eyes and nose start to bleed profusely and he takes 28 ({@damage 8d6}) psychic damage and cannot take Actions or Bonus Actions until the start of his next turn. The Constitution saving throw DC increases by 5 for every success, being reduced by 5 for every minute that passes, and returns to its original DC at the end of a long rest.

legendary.name: Evasive Attack
entries: Sukuna moves up to half his movement and can make one Unarmed Strike.

legendary.name: Reverse Cursed Technique Mastery
entries: Sukuna uses his Reverse Cursed Technique.

legendary.name: Dismantle
entries: Sukuna uses Dismantle.

legendary.name: Cleave (2 Actions Required)
entries: Sukuna uses Cleave.

legendary.name: Dismantle (2 Actions Required)
entries: Sukuna uses Dismantle.

legendary.name: Chanting (3 Actions Required)
entries: Sukuna begins Chanting.

legendary.name: Furnace Open (3 Actions Required)
entries: Sukuna uses Furnace: Open if he has 100 or more Preparation Counters.

legendary.name: Domain Expansion (3 Actions Required)
entries: Sukuna uses his Domain Expansion.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The curse has 35 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: The curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns.

trait.name: Cursed Attack
entries: The curse's attacks are considered magical.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it is {@condition invisible}. Creatures with levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who can see into the Ethereal Plane can see the curse. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see you.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its claws.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) piercing damage

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d12 + 5}) slashing damage

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 6 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: Yuji has 5 cursed energy that he can use to fuel his abilities. All of his cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Strike (1-2 Cursed Energy)
entries: Yuji can, when making an Unarmed Strike, add {@damage 1d8} force damage to his attack per Cursed Energy spent. Yuji can also use this in response to rolling a natural 20 on an attack roll with an unarmed strike instead of beforehand. On a miss, the energy used remains until he hits a target or until 1 minute passes. For each cursed energy Yuji spends on this feature, he counts as having spent double the amount, however he still cannot count as spending more than his limit.
Yuji can use this feature 2 times per round.

trait.name: Cursed Armor (1 Cursed Energy)
entries: Yuji can give himself 5 temporary hit points for 1 minute. Yuji can only benefit from this feature again after 1 minute or having his temporary hit points reduced to 0.

trait.name: This Body Is Mine
entries: Yuji gains advantage against effects that would alter his form.

trait.name: Innate Talk
entries: Yuji wishing or not, the cursed object inside of him can force him to talk with them. At any point in time, the cursed object may bring Yuji inside its Innate Domain, its soul. There Yuji can talk with them until they release him, bringing him back to the real world. No matter how long the talk actually lasted, it's only a second in the real world.

trait.name: Humanoid Prison
entries: Yuji has advantage on all checks and saving throws to resist any kind of Cursed Objects he consumes, with their DC being reduced by 5.

If Yuji fails a saving throw when consuming a Cursed Object, he instead spits out the object within 5 feet of him as opposed to the normal effects the Cursed Object inflicts on a failed save. If the effect would kill him, he not only spits it out but is also knocked {@condition unconscious}.

A Cursed Object Yuji consumes cannot force him to submit his body over to them unless a Binding Vow has been made between him and the Cursed Object. If Yuji consume more than 5 Cursed Objects, he must make a Wisdom saving throw as normal to not be possessed.

As an action, Yuji may "switch" with a Cursed Object he has consumed, giving them temporary control over his body in which he cannot swap back for a number of rounds equal to the cursed objects Charisma modifier. After those set amount of rounds Yuji may, as a reaction, make a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw. On a success, he takes control back from the Cursed Object whereas on a failure, he cannot take back control for 1 minute. The DC for this save increases by 1 for every success and is reduced by 1 for every failure. The DC returns to its original difficulty when Yuji takes a Long Rest.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: Yuji has two reactions in each round of combat.

trait.name: Reinforced Immune System
entries: Yuji has advantage on saving throws against the {@condition poisoned} condition.

trait.name: Strong Body
entries: Yuji will reduce all damage he takes but psychic by 3. Attacks using Cursed Energy or magic can bypass this, unless he's under the effects of Cursed Armor.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Slaughter Demon
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@dice 1d4 + 4}) slashing and 3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) force damage.

bonus.name: Martial Arts
entries: When Yuji uses the Attack action with an unarmed strike on his turn, he can make one unarmed strike or attack with a one-handed weapon.

bonus.name: Cursed Weapon Enhancement (1-2 Cursed Energy)
entries: Yuji can add cursed energy to a melee weapon or improvised weapon he is wielding for 1 minute. Whenever he hits a target with the weapon, it takes extra force damage equal to the amount spent. Yuji can only use this enhancement again after 1 minute has passed. Other creatures cannot use Yuji's Cursed Weapon Enhancement, and must use their own. The duration remains while Yuji isn't wielding the weapon, in case he is going to wield it again.

bonus.name: Cursed Blast of Blows (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Immediately after taking the Attack action on his turn, Yuji can perform two unarmed strikes.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Yuji can take the Dodge action.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Yuji can take the Disengage or Dash action on his turn, and his jump distance is doubled until the end of his turn.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The curse has 5 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The Cursed Spirit's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: While in combat, the curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns, up to its maximum. The combat must be one where the curse's life is at risk.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Phasing
entries: Due to being composed of a smaller amount of cursed energy, the curse may walk through objects and structures as if they weren't there.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The curse makes two Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Illness (1 Cursed Energy)
entries: When the curse hits a Bite attack, it can force the target to make a a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw against disease, becoming {@condition poisoned} on a failure until the disease is cured. Every 24 hours that elapse, the creature must repeat the saving throw, reducing its hit point maximum by 5 ({@dice 1d10}) on a failure. This reduction lasts until the disease is cured. The creature dies if the disease reduces its hit point maximum to 0. This illness is mundane and can be cured by common modern medicine.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 3 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a Reaction to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The curse has 5 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: While in combat, the curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns, up to its maximum. The combat must be one where the curse's life is at risk.

Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Phasing
entries: Due to being composed of a smaller amount of cursed energy, the curse may walk through objects and structures as if they weren't there.

action.name: Branch grasp
entries: The curse extends its branches towards a creature of its choice within 10 ft. of itself forcing that creature to make a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition restrained}. On success the creature evades the curse and isn't affected. If a creature is currently {@condition restrained} at the beginning of its turn it may use an action to attempt a {@dc 12} Strength saving throw escaping on success and remaining trapped on failure.

action.name: Constriction
entries: As an action the curse can constrict its branches around a creature it has {@condition restrained} dealing 13 ({@damage 2d10 + 2}) bludgeoning damage and increasing the dc to escape its grasp by 1 up to a maximum of 20. The curse may only have 1 medium or smaller creature {@condition restrained} at any given time.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 3 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The curse has 3 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: While in combat, the curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns, up to its maximum. The combat must be one where the curse's life is at risk.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The Cursed Spirit's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The centipedes can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the centipedes can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny insect. The centipedes can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Crowd Control Vulnerability
entries: The swarm has disadvantage in saving throws against effects that cover an area of effect, such as a cone, circle or line. Additionally, any damage caused to the swarm by such effects is tripled if the entire area is covered by the swarm.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The curse is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Phasing
entries: Due to being composed of a smaller amount of cursed energy, the curse may walk through objects and structures as if they weren't there.

trait.name: Number Advantage
entries: The Centipedes have advantage on Grapple action.

action.name: Grapple
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 4d4 + 5}) slashing damage. If the target is medium or smaller the curse may make a grapple check against the target.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The curse has 5 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. entries: The curse makes two Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d6 + 1}) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The curse can spend up to 3 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to suffering damage, the curse uses its Regeneration.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The curse has 2 cursed energy it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

entries: The curse's attacks are considered magical.

Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see the curse.

trait.name: Phasing
entries: Due to being composed of a smaller amount of cursed energy, the curse may walk through objects and structures as if they weren't there.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit -1} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 2d4 - 1}) piercing damage.

Petrifying stare (2 Cursed Energy). The curse chooses a target it can see within 30ft. of itself and forces them to make a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw on failure they are {@condition petrified} until the beginning of their next turn or until they take damage from an outside source.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The Eyeball {@variantrule Curses|XPHB|Curse} can spend up to 2 Cursed Energy, regaining hit points equal to 5 times the amount spent.

reaction.name: Regeneration
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to suffering damage, the Eyeball {@variantrule Curses|XPHB|Curse} uses its Regeneration.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: The Fly Head has 1 cursed energy that it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

entries: The Cursed Spirit's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: While in combat, the curse regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of each of its turns, up to its maximum. The combat must be one where the curse's life is at risk.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The Fly Head is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see the Fly Head.

trait.name: Phasing
entries: Due to being composed of a smaller amount of cursed energy, the curse may walk through objects and structures as if they weren't there.

trait.name: Item Storage
entries: This {@variantrule Curses|XPHB|Curse} can hold various items for its user. The curse can hold up to 1000 pounds, not exceeding a volume of 128 cubic feet. The curse weights 30 pounds, regardless of its contents. Retriveing an item from the curse requires a free action interact with the item. The curse may also store creatures of medium size or smaller, although they would need to hold their breath as it doesn't have air inside it.

trait.name: Shrinking
entries: This curse is capable of shrinking its own size. This allows for the curse to be hidden by its user. When shrunk down it emits no cursed energy.

trait.name: Weapon Bound
entries: After a minute of not being in contact with the Kenjutsu {@variantrule Curses|XPHB|Curse}, the Cursed Katana dissapears and reappears inside the curses body. The Kenjutsu {@variantrule Curses|XPHB|Curse} may then spend 3 cursed energy as a free action to pull the Katana out from its body and re-equip it.
If the Kenjutsu {@variantrule Curses|XPHB|Curse} is dead the Katana will instead dissapear completely after 1 minute of not being in contact.

Unarmed strike. Melee {@variantrule Weapon Attack|XPHB}: {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

entries: The Kenjutsu {@variantrule Curses|XPHB|Curse} can spend up to 2 cursed energy to add to a melee weapon or improvised weapon it is wielding for 1 minute. Whenever it hits a target with the weapon, they takes extra force damage equal to the amount spent. It can only use this enhancement again after 1 minute has passed. Other creatures cannot use its Cursed Weapon Enhancement, and must use their own. The duration remains while it is not wielding the weapon, in case it is going to wield it again.

It gains temporary hit points equal to the amount of cursed energy spent times 5 for the duration. It can only benefit from this feature again after 1 minute or having its temporary hit points reduced to 0. It cannot use this enhancement if it cannot take actions due to a condition or effect.

The curse has two additional reactions for this feature.
