trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: Megumi has 21 cursed energy that he can use to fuel his abilities. All of his cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest.

trait.name: Unarmored Movement
entries: Megumi can move and run along vertical surfaces and across liquids on his turn without falling during the move.

trait.name: Cursed Strike (1-3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Megumi can, when making an Unarmed Strike, add {@damage 1d6} force damage to his attack per Cursed Energy spent. Megumi can also use this in response to rolling a natural 20 on an attack roll with an unarmed strike instead of beforehand. On a miss, the energy used remains until he hits a target or until 1 minute passes.
Megumi can use this feature 3 times per round.

trait.name: Cursed Armor (1-4 Cursed Energy)
entries: Megumi can give himself 5 temporary hit points per energy spent for 1 minute. Megumi can only benefit from this feature again after 1 minute or having his temporary hit points reduced to 0.

trait.name: Stronger Shadows (1-4 Cursed Energy)
entries: Megumi's tamed shikigami has 2 passive Stronger Shadows counters (already applied). Additionally, when Megumi summona a tamed shikigami, he may apply stronger shadows counters to the shikigami for every 2 cursed energy spent the shikigami will gain a Stronger Shadows counter. Stronger Shadows counters applied this way will last for 4 hours, and while applied to a shikigami, Megumi cannot apply more Stronger Shadows counters to the shikigami in this way, unless he removes the Stronger Counters counters applied this way (no action required).

No matter from which source, a shikigami can only possess a maximum of 10 stronger shadows counters at any time. For each stronger shadows counter applied, the shikigami will gain the following benefits

trait.name: Overall Increase
entries: They gain a +1 bonus to their Strength, Dexterity, Constitution and Charisma scores.

trait.name: Vitality Increase
entries: They gain an additional hit die.

trait.name: Speed Increase
entries: They gain 5 additional feet of movement speed.

trait.name: Attack Power Increase
entries: Their attack rolls damage die increases by one die.

trait.name: Power Increase
entries: Their dice for features that heal or force creatures to make a saving throw increases one die.

trait.name: Shikigami Maintenance
entries: Megumi must maintain {@status concentration} to keep his shikigami summoned. Megumi must make the saving throw for each shikigami he has summoned. Shikigami stored within his shadow does not count as being summoned for the purposes of this feature. For a saving throw to be required, the attack must have successfully reduced Megumi's actual hit points, not temporary hit points. The DC for the saving throw is 10 or 1/4 of the damage taken, whichever is higher. If Megumi fails the {@status concentration} saving throw he can spend 1 cursed energy to succeed instead.

trait.name: Shadow Storage
entries: Megumi may store objects in an extra-dimensional space within his shadow. Megumi may physically place something into his shadow, costing no cursed energy, however he must spend 1 cursed energy to interact with an object in this space, potentially removing it. All items in Megumi's shadow still count against his carrying capacity. Megumi's shikigami count as objects for the purpose of this feature.

trait.name: 50 Meters In 3 Seconds!
entries: Megumi has two reactions in each round of combat.

trait.name: Shikigami Battle Master
entries: While Megumi is within 10 ft. of one of his shikigami that he is fighting along side, he may give himself or one of his shikigami advantage on an attack once per turn. If Megumi or any of his shikigami attack that same target during this turn Megumi will also gain advantage on those attacks as well.
In addition, Megumi's Action cost to control his shikigami is reduced by 1 (Action>Bonus Action>Free Action).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Megumi makes two attacks.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Shikigami Summoning (1+ Cursed Energy)
entries: Megumi may summon any one of his tamed shikigami within 30 feet of him. Any shikigami summoned as part of this technique last until they are reduced to 0 hit points, Megumi dismisses them at will, or he takes a short or long rest. They regain all lost hit points at the end of a long rest. The cursed energy cost and their respective shikigami are as follows: Shikigami	Cursed Energy Cost Divine Dog(s) (S-2)	1 Cursed Energy Gama (S-2)	2 Cursed Energy Nue (S-2)	4 Cursed Energy Great Serpent (S-1)	5 Cursed Energy Additionally, should any one of Megumi's tamed shikigami die, they can no longer be summoned ever again. Should this happen, they are now eligible for Totality.

action.name: Trial
entries: As a full round action (which is completed at the beginning of Megumi's next turn), he may summon any shikigami that are on the table below within 5 feet of him. The shikigami acts on its own turn and is hostile to Megumi. Once Megumi has battled against the untamed shikigami alone (does not include using his tamed ten shadows shikigami) and reduced it to 0 hit points, he will now be able to summon the shikigami with Shikigami Summoning. The distance which Megumi can summon an untamed shikigami cannot be increased.

Megumi can choose any creatures within 15 feet of him or the untamed shikigami to become part of the trial until either they or the untamed shikigami are dead. If Megumi is reduced to 0 hit points or is killed while any creature other than the untamed shikigami is within the trial, he will enter a state of stasis until the ritual is properly complete, when his death will be completed. If the ritual is interrupted before it is properly concluded, such as an outsider killing the untamed shikigami before all other creatures in the ritual has been killed, then Megumi will instead begin rolling death saves when the ritual concludes, if applicable. Additionally, if Megumi is reduced to 0 hit points, the untamed shikigami becomes hostile to all other creatures within the trial.

Shikigami	Cursed Energy Cost Rabbit Escape (S-2)	3 Cursed Energy Round Deer (S-2)	6 Cursed Energy Max Elephant (S-2)	7 Cursed Energy Mourn Tiger (S-2)	8 Cursed Energy Piercing Ox (S-2)	9 Cursed Energy Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sila Divine General Mahoraga (S-2, V-1)	10 Cursed Energy

bonus.name: Martial Arts
entries: When Megumi uses the Attack action with an unarmed strike on his turn, he can make one unarmed strike or attack with a one-handed weapon.

bonus.name: Cursed Weapon Enhancement (1-3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Megumi can add cursed energy to a melee weapon or improvised weapon he is wielding for 1 minute. Whenever he hits a target with the weapon, it takes extra force damage equal to the amount spent. Megumi can only use this enhancement again after 1 minute has passed. Other creatures cannot use Megumi's Cursed Weapon Enhancement, and must use their own. The duration remains while Megumi isn't wielding the weapon, in case he is going to wield it again.

bonus.name: Cursed Blast of Blows (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Immediately after taking the Attack action on his turn, Megumi can perform two unarmed strikes.

bonus.name: Cursed Patient Defense (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Megumi can take the Dodge action.

bonus.name: Cursed Wind Step (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Megumi can take the Disengage or Dash action on his turn, and his jump distance is doubled until the end of his turn.

bonus.name: Totality
entries: If one of Megumi's summons is killed, he can grant one of his summons its Totality form. Megumi can undo totality as a bonus action. He cannot use totality on a Shikigami that is already summoned. When Megumi makes a totality, the creature gains the strengths of the deceased shikigami. They will gain the following changes:

Their Strength, Dexterity, Constitution and Charisma scores become those of the creature who has the higher score. Then they will increase by the modifier of the creature with the smaller scores.(Ex: Nue has 14 Strength while divine dog has 17, their totality would have 19 Strength.) When Megumi combines two shikigami of the same size, the totalities size increases by 1 Their AC will become the that of the creature with the higher AC.
When making a totality, the base shikigami gains the skill and saving throw proficiency's of the shikigami used in the totality.
They receive the amount of hit die of both creatures combined, with the size of the hit die being determined as normal.

Their movement speed is added together.(Ex: Two creatures with 30 movement speed would have 60 movement speed in totality form) They will receive all features, traits, actions, bonus actions, reactions, legendary actions, skill, saving throw proficiencies, damage resistances, damage immunities, condition immunities, and senses from both creatures.
Their size will become the size of the bigger creature.

Their CR will become that of the creature who has a higher CR. Then it will increase by half the CR of the creature with the smaller CR rounded up.(Ex: Nue's CR is 4, and Great Serpent's CR is 5, their totality's CR would be 7) After calculating the ability scores, the difference in the ability modifier from the original user of a damaging feature and the totality is added to the amount of dice. This value can not increase the damage of a feature to over double its original value in dice. Megumi applies this before Stronger shadows. For save attack damage, the stat corresponding to the save is used. (Ex: Mourn Tiger has a +7 in Dexterity and Nue has a +3. Mourn Tiger's Dexterity Modifier increases by +1, meaning Megumi adda 1 additional dice to the Totality's attack to the damage dice that use Dexterity as the ability modifier, changing Mourn Tiger's bite attack from a {@dice 2d10 + 7} into a {@dice 3d10 + 8}.) This increase only applies to direct attack rolls and saving throws.(excluding Natural Weight) This value is halved(rounded down to a minimum of 1) for attacks that can be used through a multi-attacking feature.(Ex: Nue's Wings and Talon) Megumi can not make a Totality using Rabbit Escape or Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sila Divine General Mahoraga. Megumi only applies Stronger Shadows to the shikigami after they are in the totality form, so the calculations above are made using their stats without Stronger Shadows.

bonus.name: Shadow Trap (2 Cursed Energy)
entries: Megumi may force a creature standing on darkness to make a {@dc 15} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}), they make this save with Disadvantage if they are completely covered in darkness or if they are inside a veil. On a success, they don't fall into Megumi's shadow storage. On a failure, they are trapped inside Megumi's shadow storage. Their weight counts against Megumi's carrying capacity. They can hold their breath for a number of minutes equal to their Constitution modifier (minimum of 1) before beginning to suffocate. If a creature has failed the Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check, they can attempt a Strength or a Dexterity (their choice) saving throw at the end of their turns, freeing themselves on a success and appearing in an unoccupied space within 5ft.

reaction.name: Shadow Ridge (1 Cursed Energy)
entries: Once per round, as a reaction against a melee attack roll, Megumi can force a creature within 10ft of him to make said attack with disadvantage as their legs fall into their own shadow momentarily.

monster.name: Divine Dog
languages: understands it's masters' languages but can't speak them.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The Divine dog has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The Divine dog has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the Divine dog's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The Divine Dog is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Cursed Weapons
entries: The Divine dog's weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The divine dog makes two bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) piercing damage and the target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

monster.name: Gama
languages: understands the languages of its summoner but can't speak

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The Gama is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation (less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The gama can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Cursed Weapons
entries: The Gama's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The gama's long jump is up to 20 feet and its high jump is up to 10 feet, with or without a running start.

trait.name: Support Carry
entries: When the gama has a medium sized creature or smaller {@condition grappled} or Tongue Grabbed, it may swallow the creature granting the creature Full {@variantrule Cover|XPHB} in gama's mouth. While support carrying gama can only move.

action.name: Tongue
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage and the target must roll either {@skill Athletics|XPHB} or {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} against a {@dc 14}. On a failure, the target is {@condition grappled}. Until the grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the gama can't make tongue attacks against different targets. The creature may make a Strength or Dexterity saving throw as an action to attempt to escape the grapple, {@dc 14}, escaping on a success.

action.name: Tongue Slam
entries: While the gama has a creature {@condition grappled} it can slam them into a point within 30 feet of it, so long as that point possesses a solid surface or object of equal size to the creature, the creature {@condition grappled} takes falling damage as if they had fallen the full distance they are thrown.

action.name: Swallow
entries: The gama makes one tongue attack against a Medium or smaller target it is grappling. If the attack hits, the target is swallowed, and the grapple ends. The swallowed target is {@condition blinded} and {@condition restrained}, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the gama, and it takes 17 ({@damage 5d6}) acid damage at the start of each of the gama's turns. The gama can have only one target swallowed at a time.
If the gama dies, a swallowed creature is no longer {@condition restrained} by it and can escape from the corpse using 5 feet of movement, exiting {@condition prone}.
The gama can choose to partly swallow a creature. If the creature is partly swallowed, it isn't {@condition blinded} and doesn't take any acid damage.

reaction.name: Tongue Grab
entries: When an ally within 30 feet of the Toad would make a saving throw or take damage, the Toad can reach out and grab the ally out of harms way, pulling them next to the Toad's space. Causing the attack to miss or all damage/effects to be avoided so long as the Toad is outside the area of effect.

reaction.name: Projectile Intercept
entries: When a ranged attack targets itself, an ally or the summoner within 60 feet, Gama can use its reaction to intercept the attack with its tongue. It makes a contested melee attack roll against the attack roll. If Gama's roll is higher, the Gama catches it with its tongue and can either drop it or hold onto it. This cannot be used against Cursed Energy attacks. The attack must be a projectile.

monster.name: Nue
languages: understands the languages of its summoner

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: Nue is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Cursed Energy
entries: Nue has 15 cursed energy it can use to fuel its abilities. All of its cursed energy is replenished at the end of a long rest..

trait.name: Cursed Energy Recovery
entries: Nue regains 1 cursed energy at the beginning of its turns in combat.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The Nue doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Sight
entries: The Nue has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on hearing or sight.

trait.name: Cursed Weapons
entries: Nue's weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Nue makes two attacks: one with its Wings and one with its Talon.

action.name: Wings
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage. Nue can spend 5 Cursed Energy to add 27 ({@damage 5d10}) lightning damage to the roll, and also force the target to make a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw, on a failure, they are knocked {@condition prone}, and until the end of their next turn their movement becomes 0 and they have disadvantage on Strength and Dexterity saving throws. On a success they take half as much damage and do not receive any additional effects.

action.name: Talon
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Lightning Charge (3 Cursed Energy)
entries: Nue fires a bolt of lightning at a single target within 60ft. The target must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure they take 27 ({@damage 5d10}) lightning damage, are knocked {@condition prone}, and until the end of their next turn their movement becomes 0 and they have disadvantage on Strength and Dexterity saving throws. On a success they take half as much damage and do not receive any additional effects.

trait.name: Invisible Force
entries: The Great Serpent is entirely made out of cursed energy, meaning it cannot be seen by normal people. Creatures without levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer class or who cannot see into the ethereal plane cannot see it. Although, if these creatures were in a life and death situation(less than half their maximum hit points), they gain the ability to see it.

trait.name: Cursed Weapons
entries: The Great Serpent's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Shadow Burrow
entries: Great Serpent can burrow into the ground without leaving behind any hole or passage as it swims through the shadows themselves to dig.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The serpent makes two bite attacks. It may replace one of these with a constrict attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) piercing damage and the target must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage and the target must roll either {@skill Athletics|XPHB} or {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} against a {@dc 16}. On a failure, the target is {@condition grappled}. Until this grapple ends, the creature is {@condition restrained}, and the serpent can't constrict another target. The creature may make a Strength or Dexterity saving throw as an action to attempt to escape the grapple, {@dc 16}, escaping on a success.

feat.name: Reverse Cursed Technique

prerequisite.other: You must be at least 8th level have 17+ Charisma score and must have failed two death saves in the same combat and survive.

entries: After many battles and studies about your cursed energy, you learn to use your cursed energy in a completely opposite way, channeling positive energy instead of negative. You have a pool of healing you can use with your reverse cursed energy that's equal to 5 times your level times your Charisma modifier. As an Action or Bonus Action for x cursed energy, you can remove healing from that pool to regain hit points. You may also remove healing from the pool as a reaction to receiving damage. For every 1 cursed energy spent, you regain 10 hit points, removing an equal amount from the pool. If you are suffering from a physical effect injury such as a concussion or something similar, one of these effects can be healed for every 10 hit points healed with reverse cursed technique. This cannot heal the health of lost limbs.

You can use your Reverse Cursed Technique until your healing pool ends before getting burnout, during which you can't use RCT until your healing pool recovers. At the beginning of your turns while in a combat where your life is at risk, you regain 5 hit points of your healing pool back. Your healing pool automatically goes back to full at the end of a long rest, or short rest if you recover cursed energy on a short rest.

Once per round, you may try to force your reverse cursed technique to come out despite being in burnout, forcing you to make a DC25 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, you will gain 1 level of {@condition exhaustion}, which can be reduced upon taking a short or long rest instead of just upon taking a long rest. On a success, you can use reverse cursed technique as normal, but the maximum amount you can spend is equal to half of your charisma modifier, rounded down.

Finally, if you possess a Innate Technique, you gain the Reversal Form of your Cursed Technique, detailed at the end of the cursed technique's description. Using your reversal impacts your reverse cursed technique output like normal, whenever you use Cursed Technique Reversal, you must spend a number of hit points from your reverse cursed technique pool equal to the total cursed energy spent on the technique reversal, times 5 (before reductions).

feat.name: One In A Billion

prerequisite.other: Must have Flawless Fundamentals and must have all Cursed Energy Enhancements at Expert tier.

entries: You have maximized your cursed energy efficiency, using only increments of what the average sorcerer needs to activate a technique. You now reduce the cost of everything that costs cursed energy by twice your proficiency bonus, to a minimum of 1. This overwrites your selections from the Efficiency Fundamentals feature and its accompanying Cursed Feats. Domain Expansion is now reduced fully.

feat.name: Six Eyes

prerequisite.other: Must pick the Gojo Clan sorcerer family, must be a human, and there must be a Star Plasma Vessel in the world.

entries: You have the gift granted only to the strongest of the Gojo Clan, the Six Eyes. You will gain the following benefits:

items.name: Illuminated Vision
entries: You will gain 60 ft. of {@sense Truesight}. You may not have any penalties for seeing through any thin, light and non rigid object, such as a blindfold, towel, or scarf, allowing you to use it as a way of protecting your eyes from the sheer amount of information your eyes capture.

items.name: Information Overload
entries: Due to the amount of information your eyes capture, if you're not wearing any light object to protect your eyes, you will receive 1 level of {@condition exhaustion} every minute, to a maximum of 3 levels. Additionally, for every {@condition exhaustion} level you gain like this, the damage die tier of your techniques decreases by 1, and the DC your technique suffers a penalty of -1. If you wear eye protection again, this timer resets after a minute of covering your eyes rather than immediately. Outside of combat, you will only gain one level of {@condition exhaustion} every hour your eyes are uncovered, with the timer and {@condition exhaustion} resetting if you wear eye protection for 10 minutes. Finally, if you have the Improved Reverse Cursed Technique feat, you can remove these {@condition exhaustion} levels for every 50 hit points you heal with it, and you can do so even if you have not taken damage.

items.name: Cursed Energy Economy
entries: Due to the six eyes, the amount of cursed energy you spend on your techniques is ridiculously low when compared to an average sorcerer. You gain the One In A Billion boon, ignoring its requirements, the reduction is increased by half your proficiency bonus, rounded down. While wearing eye protection, this reduction is decreased to proficiency bonus less energy spent.

items.name: Infinity Optimization
entries: Given the nature of the Six Eyes, it naturally improves the efficiency of the Infinity technique. If or when you get the Reverse Cursed Technique feat, you may practice the ability to improve the efficiency of infinity. Once per month during a long rest, you may make a DC 20 {@skill Arcana} check. On a failure, nothing happens and you lose an amount of cursed energy equal to Infinity's cursed energy cost that cannot be regained until the next long rest. On a success, you gain 1 Infinity Optimization counter.

You must gain 12 Infinity Optimization counters in order to get the Infinity Optimization feature. You no longer need to use reactions or spend cursed energy to nullify attacks using the technique when you gain this feature. You also no longer have to Concentrate on Infinity.

items.name: Perceptive Eyes
entries: Nothing escapes your watch, you will gain expertise in {@skill Perception}, will have advantage on all {@skill Perception} checks, and can add your Charisma modifier to ({@skill Perception}) checks. Additionally, you gain the Cursed Energy Tracker option from the Advanced Energy Usage class feature, and may use it to, instead of it's normal effects, detect any remains of Cursed Energy in a 240 ft. radius, detecting it even if you can't see it directly. While wearing eye protection, you will no longer have advantage on all perception checks.

items.name: Technique Reader
entries: Normally trying to figure out your opponent's technique takes great deduction skills, however with six eyes you just get this info with one good look. As a free action towards an enemy within your {@sense Truesight}, you can look into a creature and receive the following information: Their Jujutsu Sorcerer, their cursed energy, whether they have Unique Cursed Energy and its properties, and domain refinement if any. In addition, if they have an innate technique, you will learn their Innate Technique's target concept and the capabilities of its Lapse technique, as well as any technique improvements to the lapse they currently possess. If the Lapse technique involves summoning creatures you only gain general knowledge of the summons' abilities. While wearing eye protection, you can't use this feature.

items.name: Technique Analyzer
entries: Your eyes allow you to uncover the secrets of techniques. As a reaction to seeing an Extension, Reversal, or Maximum technique be used while your eyes were uncovered, or as a bonus action within 1 minute of seeing them used while your eyes were uncovered, you may make a Intelligence({@skill Arcana}) check against its cursed energy DC. On a failure, you must wait to see the feature used again. On a success, you will learn the feature and its capabilities.
At 14th level, you can spend 6 Cursed Energy as a bonus action to expand your range of {@sense Truesight} to 120 feet for 1 minute in initiative, or 1 hour out of initiative.

feat.name: Black Flash

prerequisite.other: Must have scored at least 3 critical hits in a single battle using a melee weapon attack, Must have Cursed Strike or Cursed Weapon Enhancement

entries: You have become more connected with your cursed energy, now being able to use the infamous Black Flash technique. A rare scenario where cursed energy collides within a 0.000001 second margin of error, with space distorting, causing cursed energy to flash black.

At any time, the DM can rule a black flash would or would not occur, such as an encounter's tension not being suitable for the high stakes of a Black Flash, or an extreme situation in which it feels as if fate had intervened.
A Black Flash can occur in many various unique and creative ways. The following are the base rules for a naturally occurring Black Flash.

{@b Landing a Black Flash.} When you score a critical hit, which must be a Natural 20 rolled on the {@dice d20} for it to count, with a melee weapon attack that benefits from either Cursed Strike or Cursed Weapon Enhancement, you can choose to make a DC 25 Charisma saving throw. On a failure, nothing happens. On a success, you will land a Black Flash.

{@b Black Flash.} When landing a Black Flash, before the damage calculation, roll a {@dice d6}, the result will determine how much additional force damage your Black Flash will add onto the damage roll of the attack using the table below. When you reach 10th level, you will add half of your proficiency bonus(rounded down) to the result of the {@dice d6}, gaining the corresponding benefit. You may only reroll if the final value of the roll is 1, otherwise, you must take the corresponding additional damage.

entries.caption: Black Flash Damage
rows: Reroll
6 damage
16 damage
32 damage
56 damage
88 damage
130 damage (Must possess 14 or more levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer Class)
181 damage (Must Possess 21 or more levels in the Jujutsu Sorcerer Class)

entries: The Black Flash simply does not occur if the attack wouldn’t otherwise hit (such as if it was negated by an ability). After you land a Black Flash, it becomes easier to land another. Your next attack (if on the same turn) will trigger a Black Flash on a Natural 15 or higher, known as the Black Flash DC. For every Black Flash after the second, the Natural attack roll must be 1 higher than the previous. (Ex. Third Black Flash lands on a 16, the DC becomes 17. Fourth lands on a 17, the DC becomes 18.) Failing to land a consecutive Black Flash results in the DC being lost, requiring another Natural 20 to regain it, then restarting at a DC of 15.

{@b In The Zone.} Hitting a Black Flash puts an individual in the zone, temporarily bringing out 120% of their potential. You will gain the following benefits for a number of minutes equal to your proficiency bonus. The benefits will last from during the turn you landed the Black Flash until those number of minutes after it.

You receive a +2 to all your ability scores, including their maximums. In addition, you will gain a number of the following benefits equal to half of your proficiency bonus of your or your DM’s choice. Each benefit can not be taken more than once unless stated otherwise:

items: If you possess a reverse cursed technique pool, you may use your awakened state to set up a unique relay in which the burden of the technique is dispersed throughout your brain. As a bonus action while you possess this benefit, you may make a DC 20 Charisma saving throw. On a success, your reverse cursed technique pool will be refilled by 1/4th of its maximum. Rolling a Nat 20 on this saving throw will recover your pool by 1/2 of its maximum instead. You may take this benefit more than once and you must gain this benefit again to use it after successfully refilling your pool.

If you are under the effects of Technique Burnout from using Domain Expansion, upon landing this black flash you may make a DC 20 Charisma saving throw, immediately ending the effects of technique burnout on a success, recovering your Innate Technique. You may take this benefit more than once.
You roll 1 extra damage dice for your Curse-Empowered Strikes.
Your cursed energy armor, if any, gives 5 more temporary hit points per cursed energy spent.
The die tier of your Cursed Strikes will increase by 1.
Your Cursed Weapon Enhancement will deal additional damage equal to your Charisma modifier.
The amount you can spend on your Cursed Energy Enhancements will be increased by 1.
Your cursed energy attack rolls will be made with advantage, while other creatures will make your cursed energy saving throws with disadvantage.(Unless they're also in In the zone)

Your cursed techniques will add an amount of damage dice equal to your proficiency bonus. (Ex: If you land a black flash and your technique deals {@dice 4d6}, with a proficiency bonus of +3 it would become {@dice 7d6}.)

If you possess a Domain Expansion, you will count as 100 Domain Refinement higher than your current refinement, only for the purpose of Refinement Benefits and Domain Properties. This cannot grant Domain Superiority.

Your leeway and skill with utilizing Binding Vows has increased, you will have greater leeway with using Binding Vows in your awakened state, making vows that you may normally not be able to. For example, altering the hand sign for your Domain Expansion, or even something as drastic as overcoming the inability to use your Domain after suffering brain damage, by utilizing a different part of the brain.
If you land another Black Flash while In The Zone its possible you will not gain new benefits or additional benefits as well as the ones you previously gained, as determined by your DM.

variantrule.name: Slow and Steady
entries: Often for players, their characters are limited to improvement through avenues of power such as level, equipment, racial bonuses and similar features, yet there is often little place for training these skills by themselves. Perhaps you are an apprentice to an old retired soldier, sworn to teach you his tricks, or you are a student at a greatly esteemed magical academy, or perhaps you are an independent learner, only needing old dusty tomes and notes to learn your craft. There is one constant across these scenarios, and that is training.

Thus, whenever you wish to train a feature, you may spend some time to start honing your craft with it. For this, pick a feat of your choosing. Depending on the quality of your teaching, and the strength and skill needed for the feature, your DM will assign a length to your training sessions, and a Mastery die to the feat, ranging from a D4, all the way to a d20. Difficult feats that need more training will require higher die, and vice versa. For example, a very difficult feat may require a d20, and two to three hour sessions, or more if a campaign’s in-game time flows particularly faster or any other reason.

After your first training session, you now possess the feature, and may attempt to use it. However, whenever you try this, you must roll your mastery die, and land the highest possible result. On a success, you use the feat as usual, and on a failure, the attempt is a whiff, and you produce no effect, losing any resources that would be spent by the feature (such as actions, class resources, etc.). Additionally, you are unable to attempt to use this feature again for one round. If the feat grants passive benefits, you do not obtain them until you finish training.

To amend this, you may continue your training, decreasing the DC for this roll by 1 for each subsequent session. Once the DC reaches 1, you do not need to roll, and instead possess the feat as if you had obtained it through natural means. You will need to wait one day to train again after each session.

You may choose any feats of which you meet the requirements to train. You may spend more time or take a higher Mastery Die, to ignore one requirement for the feat, if allowed by your DM. This cannot be a race, an innate technique, or a level requirement, and it may not be a prerequisite requiring you possess another cursed feat. Additionally, players may be allowed to train features that are not feats, at the DM's discretion.
